Name: 11_replace_ai_jobs_engineers
Representative_Docs: ['i never thought to ask chatgpt to see what it would say and here s what it said replacing all software developers with ai is neither practical nor desirable while ai has advanced significantly there are key reasons why software developers remain essential complex problem solving software development involves understanding complex often ambiguous problems ai excels in specific tasks but struggles with nuanced problem solving and understanding context outside defined parameters creativity and innovation developers bring creativity intuition and domain expertise to projects which ai lacks creating innovative solutions or exploring uncharted territories in technology requires human ingenuity ethical and social considerations software development impacts society requiring ethical judgment empathy and foresight to consider the consequences of design choices ai cannot replace the human responsibility to build ethical systems dynamic requirements many projects involve rapidly changing requirements and direct collaboration with stakeholders developers adapt to these changes a skill that ai tools cannot fully replicate error handling and debugging ai can assist in debugging but struggles to understand nuanced issues or edge cases that require deep human insight and experience ai s dependency on developers ai systems themselves are developed maintained and improved by software developers replacing developers would halt the evolution and adaptation of ai tools ai s role in augmenting developers ai is better suited to augmenting developers by automating repetitive tasks generating code snippets and improving efficiency examples include automated testing code optimization bug detection generating boilerplate code while ai tools like chatgpt copilot and others enhance productivity they are tools not replacements for skilled human developers', 'background so my company does specialized erp software for a specific use case and we re transitioning from a desktop based software to a cloud based solution i was hired to be an ai engineer to create ai powered assistants for various departments and customers and things to speed up the engineering team since we ve kind of become the chief of staff s strike team think tank to help out solving business problems as well as technical ones the problem well the business team wasn t minding the technical side very well and hired contractors and overseas devs initially to build out the new cloud product that didn t turn out well and they have since reversed course and are building out the internal team of engineers to develop and maintain the product going forward the team is probably around people on the tech side and mid s of that is internal and growing unfortunately we re now left with a code base that people don t fully understand that s all spaghetti code with repeated logic and sometimes just code that looks like it might work but is just faked by overseas folks it s good that they are taking control of their own destiny but they re not implementing good coding practices going forward a lot of what s happening now is clean up but i ve seen no concerted effort to improve coding style caliber even with new development they re not doing any unit testing anymore which is alarming to me they had several projects to do it but they were not maintained and have since been abandoned i was told to specifically unload unit test projects by high level engineers now we have three qa people who are doing e e testing automated by playwright just in lieu of any unit tests this seems wrong to me firstly because unit tests do have a useful function in a workplace and secondly because it s putting peoples testing burden on each tester that seems bad the high level devs like senior software engineers and the architecture team don t know how things like authentication work in some of our projects just as an example core functionality of the software suites are broken if we transitioned people now to the cloud they would not be able to function on a fundamental day to day level management treats everything like it s a crisis and sets unrealistic deadlines for the engineering team in a people will take the time you give them to complete a task mindset leading engineers to cut corners and skimp on testing or refactoring for readability reliability most senior engineer and cto both left within the last three weeks management wants to figure out how bad of a code situation we re in but it s hard to assess that because the code base is in such a bad state methods that are s of lines long ambiguous naming conventions leading to confusion between past and present state of requirements dynamic typings which should have been well defined it s spaghetti code many more issues feel free to ask questions but i think you get the idea so that s the bad here s where we re at now the head of my team the chief of staff wants me to make recommendations tomorrow as part of a team to kind of reset going forward after our cto quit on friday i ve been recommending unit testing responsibilities for coders and a core group of beta testers from our customers to help us sus out all the problems i m really punching out of my weight class here as a dev who s been working in the industry for two years here right now we need to figure out the extent of the broken code but extensive use cases haven t been outlined well enough to catch all the bugs and the code is spaghetti levels of readable mid term we need to implement a more reliable robust dev environment so we prevent these issues from happening in the future i was hoping for some recommendations on things that can be done here going forward to improve the team i m being put in a position where i can shape the future of the dev environment at my work if you have any ideas frameworks articles methods or technologies to suggest i am open to anything i don t mind doing the research and figuring this out on my own but i wanted to come to a group that has more wisdom than i if you have had any experience in this kind of role please share your experience and what happened what worked etc anything would help right now i feel like management is asking how quickly can i turn lead into gold and i m standing on the outside of a mine without a pickaxe or dynamite context on why i ask and why i m still here i like the job i get a lot of responsibility and i see the path to grow here they obviously need leadership i get to work on new technology at a high level i m a dev but based on the work i ve done at companyname the case to go to dev ii eoy is clear business level logic creating devops pipelines from scratch in a new environment implementing new security schemes helping to develop new grad talent at the company i m also making good money for where i live for my experience level i want to succeed in this role', 'i asked chatgpt to write a convincing rebuttal to your second argument while it is true that ai algorithms have made significant advances in the ability to generate code and assist with certain tasks it is not accurate to say that they will never be able to completely replace human software engineers in fact it is very likely that ai will eventually be able to outperform human software engineers in many areas first of all the argument that software engineering requires human expertise and creativity is misguided while it is true that software engineering involves a wide range of tasks many of these tasks can be automated using ai algorithms for example ai algorithms can be trained to understand requirements design and implement complex systems and test and debug code these algorithms can learn from large amounts of data and can quickly adapt to new challenges and opportunities furthermore the ability to think creatively and critically is not unique to humans ai algorithms can be designed to generate novel solutions to complex problems and they can be trained to evaluate and refine these solutions based on feedback and performance metrics this means that ai algorithms can be just as creative and critical as human software engineers in conclusion it is not accurate to say that ai will never be able to replace human software engineers while ai algorithms may not be able to fully replace human software engineers right now it is very likely that they will be able to do so in the future as ai technology continues to advance it will be able to perform a wider range of tasks and will become increasingly adept at solving complex problems this will enable ai algorithms to outperform human software engineers in many areas and it is only a matter of time before this happens']
GPT_Summary: Discussion on whether AI can truly replace software engineers, with most agreeing it can’t due to human creativity and real-world complexity, while some argue future AI might eventually match or exceed human developers

Name: 12_advisor_thesis_phd_supervisor
Representative_Docs: ['i messed up a lot i know i made a lot of mistakes and now i don t know what to do anymore at this point i feel like just giving up and going on with my life but i know that i will always feel like a failure i ask for advice but i kinda know what i have to do i just don t have the strength to do it i just feel that i need to write this somewhere here is the context i started my msc in computer science in in a foreign country i never wanted to go the path of ph d but i aimed to qualify myself for future r d jobs in ai going through the master s wasn t easy i am diagnosed with adhd but i managed to go on at the beginning of i started looking for advisors for my thesis and found what i believed to be the most renowned professor in the area at my faculty i contacted him he suggested a few projects and i picked one project that was supposed to be developed in cooperation with a researcher from a company that was my first mistake i thought that this what a great opportunity for my cv maybe i could even publish some papers but now i know that this was a great mistake knowing about my adhd i should totally have gone for something simple just to finish the degree i started working with this company researcher which became my real advisor they hired me as a paid intern and the professor at the university just forgot about me i started developing the research project and it was fine during the first months at the stage when i was implementing the research solution when it was the time to experiment with it the results were terrible and i got stuck i had no idea how to proceed and i felt it would be impossible to write a thesis out of that project meanwhile the interaction with this company researcher was terrible he had a very difficult personality i felt totally uncomfortable near him as he was very unpredictable sarcastic used to make me feel the dumbest person in the world i knew i had to come to him for help but i avoided that at all cost specially because i felt that every single word i texted to him was the dumbest ever we met once a week and it was terrifying for me i remember to almost pass out of anxiety a few times time passed and by jan it became clear to him i wasn t progressing and he decided to fire me from the company i claimed it was because of other duties from university but the reality was that i was completely stuck he said he would still help me with the thesis if i wanted i felt terrible as i knew i completely messed up my future and felt miserable thinking that all other jobs would be like that and that i would mess up every single opportunity i ever get i started applying for other jobs and by may i got hired by another company which turned out to be amazing it made clear how easier and happier life could be meanwhile i worked on what i believed could be the basis for my thesis but didn t have the will power to write it and didn t contact either my former manager or my university advisor by nov i finally started being medicated for adhd and that gave me finally the strength to go on it was extremely hard but by feb i finished writing it however it wasn t reviewed by anyone i knew that i should have been sending the drafts to my advisors but after waiting so long i wanted to email them with at least the full draft i finally emailed my university advisor asking for review but he replied asking me to send it to the other guy he didn t answer me for about a month then i emailed my advisor again asking if he could check it he asked me to email the other guy again i did so and he finally answered me by mid april he finally sent me back something basically saying that i wrote very badly with many mean sarcastic jokes and asked me to rewrite the abstract i did so and returned to him by the beginning of may but he never answered me anymore i wrote times to him again asking if he maybe got the opportunity to check my corrections but he never answered anymore at this point i know i have no alternative but to beg them for reviews but it is very hard to me even writing emails to them is very psychologically hard to them i can t even imagine having to call or try to meet them in person thinking about that makes me wonder about how low they think of me and how terrible my research is part of me would prefer to give up and them to never look at what i wrote in fact at this point i was expelled from university but if i finished my thesis i could get my degree after paying some fees i would prefer to give up and simply go on with my job which is going well but i think that giving up would look very bad on my cv as i would have a years gap and most of all i would feel terrible for having lost a huge amount of money and so much time of my life sometimes i think that i would rather ask my university to get another advisor and start another project from scratch but this thought seems crazy meanwhile time passes and i keep stuck without doing nothing about this situation and a half year after having started', 'hello everyone i am a master s student who is about to graduate in my research i was reasonably successful total manuscripts published under review before the quarantine i was all set to continue on for a phd i am here seeking advice on what step to take in the future perhaps writing it out will help me too writing is thinking when i began my ms i was and still am interested in computer vision i was able to secure a supervisor but was an idiot and screwed it up essentially i was poached by another advisor but then was told no by him leaving my without an advisor i talked to my graduate coordinator and my present supervisor who was just hired sought me out i joined the lab and have given it all i grew a lot partially overcome my fear of public speaking and became a better writer however there are many things in the lab that have begun to bother me my advisor s focus is entirely on the company this is evident because he is normally only concerned about publications and not about my thesis or other degree requirements for him my thesis was a last minute thing i had kept mentioning that i will begin to write my thesis but he always assigned me a manuscript to author in the end i had about a month to work on my thesis and i had to do it while working on yet another manuscript he realized what my priority was and gave month s work to someone else rather than waiting for me to finish my manuscript this doesn t bother all much that it was a mediocre paper at best what bothers me he doesn t seem to care about the amount i have and the affect on me the work unfortunately also feels a little superfical and does not have rigor it almost feels like i live in a bubble because there is constant praise about the type of work we do lets go to nature let s try science never works and a dismissive attitude towards other people s research even in completely different fields in fact my interest in deep learning sort of became a running joke in the lab before i begin to describe the negative aspects the positive ones are the pi is young and very ambitious he is insane about publications and will provide feedback on a manuscript in days the lab is very well funded the negatives as time has passed and new people joined the lab i began to feel he treated me different than other students some of the other students with less work but perhaps more intelligence than i are treated with more respect in group meetings there is usually a condescending attitude towards me i have been told privately i may have reached my ceiling the work is endless i just finished my thesis i am still expected to work on manuscript and revision on a paper any compliment i am given is basically routed back to himself and he has told me anxiety is for babies when i used the word anxious to describe how i felt about some results i suffer from a lot of anxiety in general i am a people pleaser and not a very assertive person so his words affect me severely and leave me in a dark place i do not mind constructive criticism and welcome it i know this because when he comes from a positive place and explains matters to me in a positive tone i am able to handle the feedback just fine it does not weigh on me i am not an unproductive or bad student i have a gpa of patent best paper award and a good number of publications in fact since the quarantine began i prepared manuscripts given talk written patent in addition to my thesis however i do feel my work has lacked rigour and that is on on me i am not able to handle the pressure of we gotta publish asap and end up writing papers without fully investigating however truthfully the pi does not see the holes in our work at times i feel he doesn t even know much of what we do due to these reasons i am considering finding another advisor and doing my phd in another lab but same department school i definitely feel i am missing out on future work here as it a promising technology and i will likely not be able to explore the novel work i ended up discovering here in the lab hence the patent but at least i will be working in an area i enjoy computer vision machine learning am i crazy for throwing it all away to pursue yet another degree for years', 'tl dr i was basically promised an unpaid position at my current school s lab after graduation a number of months ago but now i was told that it is no longer possible without reason i don t understand what i ve done wrong what should i do i wonder if i could receive some advice i m a final year master s student looking at graduation in a couple months my goal had been to get into research ai related and apply to phd since i started my master s so i went to the appropriate lab and found a project to work on with a first year phd student in my second semester i know a bit late but i had no idea how to get started with research at first i worked on their project it was only the two of us for months throughout i worked fairly hard pretty much full effort because i was under a fair bit of pressure due to the difficulty of getting into an ai related phd program i d been told by professors that it requires first author publication but they seemed to be bogged down by running multiple projects simultaneously and doing rebuttal work for previous publications towards the end as i do more literature review i became more and more disheartened about the project s concept not innovative been done many times before not a good concept to start with and the possibility of the project reaching publication ready status so i started coming up with ideas for a new project of course i tried to come up with ideas to help their existing project but i just didn t have faith in its basic premise at that point i thought perhaps i can still help them while doing my own project but they refused citing similarity in problem setting our solutions and directions were undeniably completely different but maybe the problem setting overlapped and conflict of interest which was understandable their advisor quite absent due to outside obligations got in touch with me i think it s because they had paid me during summer to work on their student s project which i did so the meeting was a check up and i explained the situation advisor advised me to continue to work with their student to get publication credential before doing anything on my own since i didn t have the experience to do good work i thought it was solid advice but i just simply and sincerely had no faith in that project i don t think the advisor worked closely enough to understand the project well enough it was also in this meeting that the advisor stated without qualification that i may have an unpaid position here at the lab after i graduate to continue my research until i apply for phd after the meeting as i further developed my idea i asked the again phd student to advise my project because i pivoted further away from their project and also wanted their support they agreed i told the advisor about this arrangement they acknowledged but soon maybe since my project was still strictly speaking within the same problem setting the phd student become unwilling to attend our weekly meeting and we never discussed anything research related there is very few professors at my school that do this line of work and all of them extremely busy and it was almost taboo to contact them too much plus i was timid i looked around the labs searching for phd students interested in the project but ultimately no one was the only person doing anything related was the original phd student i worked with but they were of course off the options list due to my timidness and fear of rejection i reached out only a few times to different professors without any response a phd student told me that if i wanted to work with a professor i need to put my best foot forward so i thought maybe i ll need to devise and prove my concept really well to get any support so i worked on my project by myself this had been a pretty psychologically painful experience since i was working on this pretty difficult problem without any feedback fast forward to today i have some pretty good results also i reframed and pivoted my project again such that it now pretty much has no overlap with that phd student s project yes i think he s still working on it since i haven t seen it on arxiv so i reached out to the professor thinking that there shouldn t be an issue to first get the paperwork going for the unpaid position then with that secured i ll reach out to people in the lab again for collaboration this time i think people should be more interested because the scope and methodology are crafted out and the code has been developed and optimization experiment ideas can just be thrown at it which is the fun part however i was told that an unpaid position was not possible this is quite devastating for me because i m a foreigner and rely on a work offer to use my opt i should ve asked the advisor earlier so that i have more time to react but i thought perhaps a solid proof of concept was necessary and that the advisor s unqualified statement regarding an unpaid position not being an issue made this a matter not to concern too much about i wonder what you guys think of my situation what i may have done wrong and what i should do to continue to work on my project which needs a lab to do thanks a lot']
GPT_Summary: Students struggles with toxic advisors, failed thesis or lab situations, and fear of derailing their academic careers despite working hard and wanting to continue in AI research.

Name: 13_machine_machine learning_learning_model
Representative_Docs: ['i ve recently launched homemade machine learning repository that contains examples of popular machine learning algorithms and approaches like linear logistic regressions k means clustering neural networks implemented in python with mathematics behind them being explained each algorithm has interactive jupyter notebook demo that allows you to play with training data algorithms configurations and immediately see the results charts and predictions right in your browser in most cases the explanations are based on this great machine learning course by andrew ng i hope this repository may help those of you who just started the journey towards machine learning and programming in python the purpose of the repository was not to implement machine learning algorithms by using rd party library one liners but rather to practice implementing these algorithms from scratch and get a better understanding of the mathematics behind each algorithm that s why all algorithms implementations are called homemade the main python libraries that are used there are numpy and pandas these two are used for efficient matrix operations and for loading parsing csv datasets when it comes to jupyter notebook demos then such libraries as matplotlib and plotly are being used for data visualizations currently the following topics have been covered supervised learning in supervised learning we have a set of training data as an input and a set of labels or correct answers for each training set as an output then we re training our model machine learning algorithm parameters to map the input to the output correctly to do correct prediction the ultimate purpose is to find such model parameters that will successfully continue correct input output mapping predictions even for new input examples regression in regression problems we do real value predictions basically we try to draw a line plane n dimensional plane along the training examples usage examples stock price forecast sales analysis dependency of any number etc linear regression math linear regression theory and links for further readings code linear regression implementation example demo univariate linear regression predict country happiness score by economy gdp demo multivariate linear regression predict country happiness score by economy gdp and freedom index demo non linear regression use linear regression with polynomial and sinusoid features to predict non linear dependencies classification in classification problems we split input examples by certain characteristic usage examples spam filters language detection finding similar documents handwritten letters recognition etc logistic regression math logistic regression theory and links for further readings code logistic regression implementation example demo logistic regression linear boundary predict iris flower class based on petal length and petal width demo logistic regression non linear boundary predict microchip validity based on param and param demo multivariate logistic regression recognize handwritten digits from x pixel images demo multivariate logistic regression fashion mnist recognize clothes types from x pixel images unsupervised learning unsupervised learning is a branch of machine learning that learns from test data that has not been labeled classified or categorized instead of responding to feedback unsupervised learning identifies commonalities in the data and reacts based on the presence or absence of such commonalities in each new piece of data clustering in clustering problems we split the training examples by unknown characteristics the algorithm itself decides what characteristic to use for splitting usage examples market segmentation social networks analysis organize computing clusters astronomical data analysis image compression etc k means algorithm math k means algorithm theory and links for further readings code k means algorithm implementation example demo k means algorithm split iris flowers into clusters based on petal length and petal width anomaly detection anomaly detection also outlier detection is the identification of rare items events or observations which raise suspicions by differing significantly from the majority of the data usage examples intrusion detection fraud detection system health monitoring removing anomalous data from the dataset etc anomaly detection using gaussian distribution math anomaly detection using gaussian distribution theory and links for further readings code anomaly detection using gaussian distribution implementation Representative_Docs: ['there s a lot of linear algebra in general less calculus you d probably only need multi in machine learning related classes but cs has a lot of discrete math and it s probably you ve never done discrete math before', 'you ll most likely need to calculus linear algebra and discrete maths but it s not too bad and you might even enjoy it i did calc and linear algebra discrete maths graph theory and differential equations and the forgot basically all of it i wasn t the best at maths in high school and had to do a pre calc cource even then all you need is to pass maths can be tricky but anyone can do it if they study when you get stuck ask a tutor sometimes a key piece of information will make everything make sense for me it was the unit circle p a lot of it is just following formula and remembering techniques the maths you learn will come up a bit in other compsci classes discrete maths helped for algorithms linear algebra helped in graphics and machine learning the calc not so much but learning trig and vectors has been useful plus general algebra calc could be useful if you wanted to program a physics system and it does turn up a bit in machine learning', 'typically cs students will take calculus and linear algebra and discrete and dsa if you count that as a math class chemistry students usually have to take calculus and multivariable differential equations and linear algebra it depends on the school though while the more advanced calculus courses might not be necessary for softwareengineering or web development they are very helpful for certain fields like computer graphics animation and machine learning i just meant that op would have most of the math prerequisites done already']

Name: 19_interview_internship_company_interviewer
Representative_Docs: ['semester s coming to a close and i wanted to post something to this subreddit i see a lot of internship results and not as many new grad statistics so here s my season i think it s more readable at my blog post here but i get it if you don t want to navigate away the contents are below with a summary and my takeaways semesters come to an end and i thought it would be nice to reflect on the new grad job hunt statistics are shown below and if you don t want to read everything a summary s also below that sankey diagram statistics companies applied to responses hackerranks online assessments total phone interviews initial and post oa onsites total offers return withdrawn applications summary takeaways from my experience keep applying i didn t receive other offers until late october leetcode is insanely prevalent in techhubs i was asked lc style questions in almost every interview heaps tries and intervals come up very often dp does not i was quite surprised that i ran into questions using heaps and tries so frequently apart from the typical topics strings linked lists trees array graph etc i would definitely spend more time on those topics if i could redo this cycle i also ran into merge intervals and variants of it at least times i was asked a fat dynamic programming questions and spent the most time preparing for it that being said my friend was given like triple dp hard questions for his google interview so ymmv system design is just as important as leetcode i was asked to explain the structure of one of my projects or a typical sys design question in many of my interviews and had at least system design round in every onsite but i didn t have to deeply address topics like scalability or things that are probably more relevant to senior devs i d say this is really just object oriented programming but i had occasional chances to talk about scalability and real world implications which was cool career fairs are busted my response rate at career fairs was considerably higher vs i was rejected from bloomberg from my online application but got to a final round interview from my career fair browse r cscareerquestions in moderation i browsed the subreddit almost everyday to the detriment of my mental health i saw so many doom and gloom posts posts from students some not even in college yet giving advice as if they were industry veterans i am guilty of this too but i try to only answer questions geared towards people like me and give disclaimers when i can and people just generally shitting on other people companies not in faang or the reverse of shitting on someone because they want to join a faang i found that noting certain users who were experienced level headed and gave good advice insights was pretty helpful as far as filtering through some of the chaos u talldean and u nutrecht in particular are people who come to mind take this post with a grain of salt i m just some college student who wants to share his experience in recruiting i am not experienced enough to make judgments on the industry nor am i some super genius coding whiz i am honestly a quite below average student at cmu for context i am not a computer science major statistics amp machine learning some of the interviews i did reflected this and were more probability data oriented my response rate was likely lower than that of a typical cs student at cmu i had internships and a programming instructor job from past years my gpa was ok it was enough to pass cutoffs but i don t think it was anything eye popping i felt that i had a better than average recruiting season however i also felt that i ran into more obstacles that i had not experienced in previous years namely companies only hiring for interns and being rejected because i moved my application too slowly i know that intern classes fill up too but this was the first time i ve received responses from recruiters that i had performed well on the interview but was being rejected because there were no more spots i e redfin my preparation involved skimming ctci for behavioral and core topics to address leetcode and skimming competitive programmer s handbook i did not whiteboard or code out any of the practice problems for ctci i only looked at the question and thought of the solution in my head to check i mostly used cph for dynamic programming preparation which i ended up not being asked at all for the entire cycle i began applying at the start of september and sent applications i applied to almost exclusively tech hubs around nyc seattle sf bay area and other i had my return offer from capital one that expired on october but i was surprised to find that i was unable to speedup the process for most companies my overall response rate was but i found a huge difference between shotgunning online applications and going to career fairs i had an insane response rate from companies i talked to at my school s career fairs and a lot of these companies didn t even have openings online for new grad they recruited almost exclusively from these events i was pretty overwhelmed with balancing recruiting and classwork after around the nd week of september i was getting responses everyday and my schedule quickly filled up i had an interview sometimes multiple everyday from mid september to late october i was missing weeks of classes due to onsiting and often scheduled interviews weeks in advance because i couldn t do them earlier this probably ended up messing up some of my interviews and recruiting because i was scheduling so far in advance my timeline was greatly increased and it resulted in some rejections due to the company being done with new grad recruiting my requested location running out of spots or just being too burnt out to continue interviewing once i had received an offer i was happy with i didn t want to interview anymore and withdrew the rest of my active applications my grades had also began to seriously suffer from missing class and i just wanted the season to end i ended up withdrawing my application from google amazon bloomberg tripadvisor epic roblox and a host of other smaller companies i felt especially guilty about bloomberg and amazon because i had spent so much time on their intermediate interviews to get to their final rounds only to quit the blind list was very efficient as far as studying leetcode went i felt pretty comfortable with almost all of my online assessments and interviews i was unable to finish only leetcode style coding challenge although i found that passing hackerranks did not guarantee moving on to the next round i finished samsara s in minutes to get ghosted of the phone screens and onsites i did not pass one was due to forgetting the syntax for raising an error in python one was due to blanking out on generating permutations and one was due to being completely stuck and not knowing what data structure to use k sorted array i also failed a couple of trivia quiz based hackerranks and failed a non trivial amount of behaviorals if i wasn t feeling it couldn t make a connection with the recruiter i usually just completely bombed it of the questions i was asked the topics i ran into the most were trees and intervals by far i was also asked a non trivial amount of strings linked list array trie and heap priority queue based questions i was asked dynamic programming questions the system design i was asked mostly involved designing a simple application product like uml diagrams although i was often given the opportunity to discuss other things like scalability parallelism implications on user experience real world feasibility and tradeoffs my first onsite was in early october and was with mastercard formerly apt i ended up being so nervous for that interview that i didn t end up with much sleep and ended up completely bombing the first round i did pretty well the rest of the day but i already knew in my heart that i had failed which ironically lifted a lot of the pressure off of me and probably made me perform well on subsequent rounds i was feeling really bad after that so i ended up taking a couple days off from recruiting after i flew back i ended up with offers not counting return one was with a late stage startup in la clutter and the other was a public fintech company in sf lendingclub i was very satisfied with both companies contrary to what i had read online and was very happy with what i saw in person so i knew i was going to decide between the of them in the end my decision came down to solely personal reasons so i ended up taking the latter i wouldn t be surprised if the former became a unicorn in though and exploded in evaluation so that was my recruiting season i felt like a huge mountain was lifted off my shoulders once i signed the highlight of recruiting was definitely the hotels and meal reimbursement delivering worth of boba to my hotel and showering in glorious bathrooms were definitely experiences this process definitely has a lot of luck involved and i know that it probably doesn t mean much coming from some random person but if you re struggling and losing hope i get it i felt especially awful in october and felt that i was a complete idiot who couldn t hope to pass any onsite i was watching people i knew secure huge offers from well known companies and i couldn t help but feel jealous and ask why that couldn t be me the only advice i have in regards to that is to keep applying and that it only takes company if you applied to places and get offer that you enjoy then does it really matter what the results were for the other applications and also take r cscareerquestions in moderation my mental state was definitely not helped from religiously browsing the subreddit', 'again i know this post is late but i was writing this information up anyways and figured i d share it here in case it could help anyone this post is a little more in depth than my first one which talks about your mindset and just general tips on how to do well on your interview for anyone still preparing for an upcoming interview and wanting more insight here s a more in depth explanation of the whole process scheduling your interview in most cases highly competitive schools will have an alumni interviewer contact you a few weeks after you ve applied but some schools require you to schedule your own interview weeks before the application deadline for example harvey mudd states on their website that they highly encourage students to schedule an interview before their application deadline this can happen during a campus visit or scheduling online here the point is that you need to check the school s admissions website to see what their policy is if they tell you that an alumnus is going to contact you then you wait for an email after your application is submitted if they want you to schedule the interview yourself and they highly encourage an interview then you need to find the correct channel through which to schedule it when you are scheduling the interview whether online through a portal or via email with alumni consider the timing of the interview itself you want to be accommodating to the interviewer s schedule as they usually also have full time jobs but if it seems like they re open you should avoid early mornings traffic issues end of the day interviewer could be tired or lunchtime interviewer could be hungry to effectively be flexible and judicious in your time slot i would proactively send the interviewer at least three times in the week that fit both your schedules and meet the above criteria but make sure to always prioritize the interviewer s schedule as they are usually busier than you are the interviewer will usually choose a public location to meet like a coffee shop or library so make sure to research where it is and how to get there preparing for the interview like i mentioned in part it s important to properly prepare for your interview through research and practice in particular you want to prepare answers for questions that are most likely to be asked in your interview when i say preparation i don t mean memorization of a specific script this can backfire causing you to come off as robotic in your answers or get flustered if you don t remember the exact phrasing you had in your mock interview just have the main talking points ready to go for the following questions tell me about yourself this is where just about every interview will start and represents a softball for you to get warmed up the answer here isn t too important but it can be a great time to show some personality quirkiness and unique hobbies i usually tell my students to talk about their background a little where they ve grown up the school they go to family etc some hobbies they have the more specific and strange the better and academic interests again specific is better here don t give everything away about yourself here because you ll have plenty of time to talk about your achievements and best qualities but also don t be afraid to show a little about who you are why do you want to attend our school this question probably requires the most research but luckily you may have already done this research in your why us supplemental essay for the school if so just review the reasons in your essay as to why you wanted to attend the school if you wrote the essay properly the reasons should be well researched specific and connect to your admissions angle if the school didn t require a why us essay then you have some work to do you ll have to go to the school s website and find the specific department that you re applying to there you want to try to find any specific courses professors to research with student organizations scholarships or overall educational philosophies that you can connect with the best details to use are ones that allow you to talk about some of your extracurricular or academic achievements for example have you already done some convolutional neural network research at a summer program or with a research mentor try to find an undergraduate research program and professor that specializes in cnn research and talk about how you wish to work with them and bring your own experience to the table if you re unsure whether the detail you researched is sufficiently specific ask yourself if it is something that makes that particular school unique or if it s something that other schools may have as well good food good weather an urban setting etc are not good reasons as these things apply to many schools what will you contribute to the community this question is the ideal place to talk about your leadership roles and experiences during high school you want your answer here to be supported by contributions that you ve already made to your current community because it demonstrates that you ve already had success in following through with your contributions i like to suggest that my students choose one general personality trait or perspective and then at least one specific student organization that they want to get involved in for example a student might say that they want to spread positivity to everyone they interact with at brown in their discourse discussions collaborations and relaxation then they might say that they especially want to bring their positive energy to the engineers without borders chapter there where they can leverage their engineering education to help build crucial infrastructure and machinery for communities in developing countries what makes you unique this question is tough for students because it s difficult to pinpoint a single attribute that makes you unique there are plenty of people with your ethnic background even if you re mixed race plenty of students interested in entrepreneurship or creative writing but in my opinion it s not a single attribute that makes you unique but usually a certain combination of attributes there are probably very few applicants that are mixed race and started their own business and write creative fiction explore the intersection of your interests and you ll usually find something compelling to talk about in addition to preparing your answers to interview questions you also want to prepare every other aspect of the interview to make you feel prepared and confident come up with questions that you want to ask your interviewer for suggestions refer to part arrange your transportation to the interview meetup spot ahead of time if you re driving yourself make sure you ve driven there at least once before the day of the interview update your resume unless your interviewer specifically asks you not to bring one bring a couple of copies of your resume for you both to look at during the interview at the very least you will appear well prepared bring a bottle of water or breath mints these things can help you feel comfortable and confident the day of the interview on the day of you want to make sure to dress the part when choosing an outfit go for something business casual or just a little bit nicer than your everyday clothes i d refrain from showing up in a full suit and tie or really formal dress because the interviewer will probably just be wearing whatever they wear at work but you do want to look good and feel confident so wear something that you think makes you look professional for the timing of the interview itself make sure to double check your email correspondence to confirm the time location and date don t rely on memory if you have an interview set for pm at a local starbucks make sure to leave early enough to account for any unexpected traffic or emergencies the farther the location the more time you should leave as a cushion if you get to the location early i would refrain from messaging the interviewer that you ve arrived minutes early it will put unnecessary pressure on the interviewer to get there early as well spend the time in your car reviewing your supplemental essays and your research on the school for nerves think about doing some power poses no seriously your physical posture has a dramatic effect on your mental state in harvard business school professor amy cuddy famously gave a ted talk entitled your body language may shape who you are in her talk cuddy discusses the power pose which can be used to physically boost your confidence before a nerve wracking task like a job interview you put your hands on your hips and essentially make a superman or wonder woman pose simply by assuming a stance of confidence you can trick your mind into following your body it sounds strange but in her research cuddy discovered that by spending a few minutes in the power pose study subjects were able to boost their own feelings of confidence and perform better in job interviews a private space like the bathroom is the perfect place to do this right before the interview once the end of the conversation this is how you can show demonstrated interest follow up correspondence the day after an interview you should send a quick thank you message to your interviewer to let them know that you appreciate their time and that you genuinely want to attend their alma mater here is a template greet the interviewer by name thank them for their time and express some enthusiasm questions thank them for their time again example hello jaime i wanted to reach out and say thank you for taking the time to talk with me about university name i particularly enjoyed hearing about your freshman experiences on south campus and our discussions on the role of ai in education if you have any further questions for me please do not hesitate to reach out thanks again for taking the Name: 22_paper_journal_journals_papers
Representative_Docs: ['hi our group paper was submitted to a bmc journal for a special issue the paper was rejected from elsivier after one round of review one of the reviewer wrote their entire review using llm fml after waiting for months and still stuck at the editor assigned stage we decided to email the journal asking about the status of our manuscript it turns out an editor was even assigned yet should we give up and submit the paper somewhere else considering the first author need this paper to get published before the end of the year to graduate in time also we found another journal from bmc with better review time for recent papers is there any problem publish in the same system again', 'first time trying to publish something submitted my paper qualitative study ipa psychology months ago after about months in with editor limbo it was rejected the decision was based on reviews from two referees one provided highly critical feedback some of it i acknowledge as valid particularly regarding a lack of clarity in how themes were developed in the analysis it has some over the top comments as well which i would normally not see in a standard ipa publication reviewer also called my paper unsound which as i understand it means it just isn t fit for publication anywhere reviewer gave a much more positive assessment called it important research and pushed for acceptance and rewrite the editor did not offer specific comments only the final rejection then i got another email with a suggestion of transferring the manuscript to a list of possible other journals in the same publishing house likely made with ai most of the suggested alternatives have a higher impact factors and lower acceptance rate than the journal i originally aimed for which may make publication more difficult i am currently considering three options a contact the editor to ask why a revise and resubmit was not offered i suspect this will be counterproductive so i probably will not do it b i am considering revising the manuscript in response to the critical reviewer s comments and proceeding with the proposed transfer to one of the alternative journals however i m concerned that the new journal may dismiss the manuscript outright upon seeing the initial negative review even if i make substantial improvements at the same time i would like to see this paper published as soon as possible and the transfer option could potentially expedite the process unless the presence of that first review ends up working against me c revise the manuscript and submit it to a new journal from another publisher the problem is that my topic fits only a limited number of journals and i would have to reformat the whole thing also considerably cut down on the content as the other journal i had in mind has a much stricter limit on size what should i do i got some serious whiplash reading the two reviews one was like my god you suck the other was pretty enthusiastic in tone is it common for the editor to be very conservative on the choice this is not a major journal by any means', 'hi all long time lurker who is finally facing an academic mini crisis and seeking advice for an anonymity sake i have changed the names and dates a bit and will be vague about some of the specifics i am a first year postdoctoral fellow at an american university studying the application of machine learning and large language models to another scientific discipline about a year ago myself and my lab mates came up with an interesting idea for how to apply a new technique to an old problem we saw that no one else had done this and were excited to have found something unique we quickly did some basic experiments wrote them up and submitted them to a mid tier journal in my specific field it s one of the top five ish journals but is still a specialty journal it s a sub sub journal of something you ve heard of during their peer review process author names are visible reviewer names are not this is standard in my field we submitted in january of and deposited a preprint after that there was a significant period of waiting and i found that the journal had to request different reviewers over the course of six months while we awaited our peer review eventually they were able to gather a few reviews and gave us a decision of major revisions the reviews were mixed both recognizing the novelty of our work but also recognizing the limited scope of our hasty experiments they suggested substantial additional experiments which would require months to build out because i felt that the journal was a good fit for this project and that the reviewers suggestions would improve the final product we communicated this to the journal editor and began revisions in the six months of waiting for review there had been a couple of preprints that had been released that were related to our initial work i skimmed them and thought they were mostly complementary they cited our preprint used slightly different methods overall i didn t spend much time reviewing them the revision experiments took almost five months as i wrapped up the resubmission manuscript i returned to our peer reviewer s comments to do a line by line response i then started to notice something our reviewer had suggested a weird way to split up our experiments that was identical to one of the related preprints by yen et al yen is a post doc at another american lab his lab is very productive i looked closer and saw some more oddities reviewer had suggested that we cite two older papers one of which was partially relevant but whose first author was yen he gave a detailed explanation that had minutia about this old yen paper of the five other suggestions reviewer made all ways to expand our work to broader aims this yen et al paper did each of them making our findings quite a bit less novel some of the language was remarkably close a string of or so words phrased in a weird way to describe a common method even a subtle misunderstanding of the work s purpose was present in both the review and in yen s paper interestingly yen gave the date for when data collection had started for his paper two days after reviewer recieved our manuscript looking closer at the preprints i realized that three of the four came from the same lab and yen was a st or nd author on all of them all been submitted as preprints before we recieved our peer review comments and one of the papers was recently chosen as an oral presentation at a high profile ml meeting obviously i was convinced that reviewer was this yen character and i was livid i felt that the scientific peer review process and this journal had betrayed me this guy had read our paper as part of peer review suggested novel ways to expand the work and then went to do them himself before we even had a chance to read his suggestions he took our ideas to his lab and has now built a little team exploring different facets of this work while our paper languished however in some ways i understand that this is partially good our idea was solid solid enough that one of the two people outside my lab who was forced to read our manuscript has now devoted most of his academic energy towards this topic and in no way does his work constitute plagiarism he cites our preprint in each of these follow up papers and most of the overlapping work wasn t really ideas we had generated but his suggestions for improvement but obviously it has left me disheartened disillusioned and mostly just mad we submitted our revisions a few weeks ago i talked to a few mentors about how to handle this situation each had different takes yes reviewer is almost surely yen and he has acted in a way that is antithetical to the peer review process but making a claim like this is difficult and if there is some chance i was wrong we would look insane paranoid it s overall a bit of a faux pax to dig this much into a reviewer s identity so in our response we decided to phrase it something like this a few papers have been released that we consider to be in direct competition with ours cite these authors should be excluded from reviewing our revised manuscript as they have a new conflict of interest i think this allows the journal editor the option to dig if he was interested but if he doesn t care then he probably wouldn t have cared either way however emotionally i am still struggling with this i want to know if it truly was him and i want him to be publicly shamed for abusing peer review i know reviewing articles is a hassle is unpaid etc but i really try to help the authors and journal when i m asked to review an article and it kills me to know that some people are out there using it to farm ideas for anyone who has been through this likely all to common scenario how have you dealt with it how do i get over this sense of being mistreated and continue in a productive way']
GPT_Summary: Researchers discuss delays, rejections, and ethical issues in academic publishing especially slow reviews, conflicting referee feedback, and reviewers misusing manuscripts.

Name: 32_ai_use ai_use_using
Representative_Docs: ['ah the audacity of such an insinuation to so carelessly fling about the accusation that my meticulously crafted post was borne of a generative ai is not only an insult to my intellectual autonomy but also a gross underestimation of my rhetorical prowess to presume that every articulate cogent expression of thought is the product of chatgpt is as misguided as it is lazy a reflection perhaps of the critic s own inability to distinguish between human ingenuity and machine aided drivel firstly let us address the preposterous nature of the assertion if the very act of writing with clarity coherence and precision now immediately warrants suspicion of ai involvement then we are indeed living in a deeply cynical age this accusation implies that human beings are no longer capable of stringing together sophisticated thoughts without resorting to artificial intelligence which is patently absurd the mere fact that my words resonate with a certain degree of intellectual heft does not ipso facto mean that i relied on a machine to generate them moreover the argument put forth by my accuser lacks any substantive evidence they have not engaged with the nuances of my writing nor have they provided even a scintilla of proof to back their claim this knee jerk reaction betrays a certain intellectual laziness a refusal to grapple with the actual content of my post in favor of a cheap baseless accusation one might even argue that this reveals more about their own dependency on ai generated content than anything about my writing to further dismantle this baseless claim one must consider the distinct cadence and flow of my prose which bears the unmistakable hallmarks of a uniquely human touch my argument is layered with subtleties that reflect the complexity of human thought something no ai no matter how advanced can fully replicate the cadence of my sentences the deliberate choices of diction and the intricate interweaving of ideas are uniquely mine born from years of honing my craft to dismiss this as the work of a machine is to overlook the essence of human creativity and intellect furthermore the suggestion that chatgpt is responsible for my post is not only baseless but also lazy it absolves the accuser from the responsibility of engaging with the substance of my argument rather than acknowledging the merit of the points i ve made they retreat into the tired trope of ai involvement as if to discredit me without the need for real critique in conclusion the accusation is not only unfounded but a distraction from the real issue at hand the importance of authentic thoughtful writing in an era increasingly saturated with shortcuts my post stands as a testament to the enduring power of human expression written by my own hand not an algorithm', 'i find this very similar to artists who were upset about the new ais that can make art from just a prompt they are now embracing these tools as part of their workflow as something corporatized if art love relationships and curiosity is what makes lift worth living why would we want to relegate those tasks to machines do we really care purely about mindless through writing that you demonstrate your ideas since we think through language they are designed demonstrate your thinking whilst building your skills at writing skills unlike the ability to crunch massive formulas plenty of math crunching child geniuses turn out to be pretty bad at arriving at original ideas which then create a positive feedback loop of learning and growth plenty of those human going away the people currently using ai for work school are on average more productive by compared to those that don t they re doing more with the same resources including ai personally if it weren t exactly what s given to them they can help you do x y times you d be tired by using just your brain but i m sure the calculator received the same friction ai is receiving today i understand though cause it s not perfect but it s only getting better i mean models hallucinate of the time so that s large but that s why i wish more educators were interested in being in the loop instead of fighting Name: 39_philosophy_cognitive_neuroscience_cognitive science
Representative_Docs: ['i m currently an undergraduate in my second year doing a double major in computer science and philosophy my institution university of washington is quite competitive for the former but not so much for the latter i have been very involved in computer science research and have been doing research since my first quarter in a hot field ai so i am certain that if i continued in that direction i would be able to secure a spot in a competitive cs phd program the trouble is that my heart is just tired of doing cs i love talking about the ideas but in terms of actually conducting the research i just can t do it i almost had a breakdown submitting my last paper and somehow i have signed myself up to lead another research project i also do not want to go into industry i really did not like my time at an internship i did last summer it took me here to really realize that doing cs work is just not for me on the other hand i m always excited to do philosophy and after a lot of reflection in these past few months i feel like i would want to pursue a philosophy phd and do philosophy academically i ve done well s in all my philosophy classes read philosophy books outside of class for fun and have good relationships with professors one whom i am continuing independent study with i m a pretty lonely and materially unambitious person so facts like difficulties with romantic relationships not having kids not being financially stable etc don t deter me i am the type of person to consume myself in the work i do and i feel like my dream work is philosophy my dream if you will indulge me would be to make my academic career working on a continental approach to the philosophy of ai specifically working out an ontology phenomenology and politics of information in deep learning models here are some more points for consideration the reason i want to pursue a philosophy phd is because i feel that even though cs is getting better at entertaining non technical perspectives e g in hci or social computing philosophy isn t really seriously considered what i want to do is to work on the philosophy of ai not ai with a philosophical bent which often ends up subjugating and appropriating philosophy for cs purposes but i m curious if my view is false or incomplete my institution isn t ranked very highly for philosophy even though i ve enjoyed the classes but it s also a very analytic department and there are no faculty who work in the philosophy of ai closest person is one person in philosophy of mind moreover philosophy of ai is a very specific and new field and the approaches are basically all analytic i don t think i m interested in philosophy of mind approaches i think they re done in a vacuum and old this is all to say that my dream of working in continental approaches to the philosophy of ai is highly improbable despite this i think that at least philosophy of ai seems to be an area of philosophy which maybe has more funding and attention due to recent events and trends than others so maybe there is some hope there i m sort of hanging on to this idea even though my department is analytic i ve done a lot of my own independent reading in continental philosophy and taken classes with continental philosophy content english comparative history of ideas comparative literature etc marxism phenomenology derrida etc so i feel like i have enough of a grasp of continental philosophy to know that i want to pursue it my parents of course want me to go to cs industry and are very unhappy about a philosophy phd my connection with them isn t too strong and ultimately if i think doing a philosophy phd is the right choice i will do it but in an ideal world i d like them to support me and to be able to support them if needed when they get older i know that given the material conditions pursuing a philosophy phd over a cs phd is pretty stupid but i really just do not want to pursue cs deep learning rigorously beyond undergrad i would love to philosophize about it from the outside where i think it needs to be what i would love for people to comment on is if i need a reality check if my goals are possible and if they are what steps i can do to maximize my possibility of actualizing it in the remaining two years of my undergrad', 'this is very long i apologize jesus the comments don t understand what a passion is i m sure you know philosophy isn t a great major alone for industry or high paying jobs so it s really smart to pair with cs double majoring is a great idea and doing one major for the career aspect and another for your passion is awesome plus a lot of people double major cs and philosophy because of ethical issues within data privacy especially healthcare and ai i m not sure if i d recommend a master s in philosophy if you re going into cs because master s can be quite expensive but if money isn t an issue and you are truly passionate about philosophy and not satisfied by your coursework in undergrad i d recommend at least doing it at a public in state school for a much cheaper price while doing a cs internship or writing a thesis that has to do with cs in some capacity i am a rising senior philosophy major technically junior but i m graduating a year early so i haven t finished all of my upper level philosophy coursework requirements yet and i absolutely love it what made you choose that major i was happier writing and reading than in any stem class i love medicine too but i spent more hours reading about ethical issues in medicine than learning about practicing it so my research focus is bioethics medical ethics i hope to get a phd in bioethics and pursue academia and clinical ethics consulting so philosophy will give me the ethics background and everything else i learn through research assistantships internships and electives i especially love learning how to productively efficiently and logically discuss moral issues which philosophy does a great job training you to do i m just very passionate about it and love going to class while many people don t what are the classes professors and peers in philosophy like depends on the class most of the time you read the assigned reading and come to class and talk about what you found interesting professor asks some hard questions there are no answers to and they guide you to a certain style of thinking i e are you a utilitarian or consequentialist or deontologist etc then they teach you how to effectively argue your position in upper levels you ll write a few papers pages depending on the professor mine were mostly intro philosophy courses at my university are exam based usually to prove you did the readings lots of reading i was reading pages week in my upper levels for each class so around week classes are heavily discussion based professors are incredibly smart and experts in their field peers are chill some love it and some take a course because they think it will be easy some are a little interested and will take it to fill an elective i found that my peers are diverse in backgrounds some want to be lawyers others doctors and others just want to take courses for fun what s the environment like in a philosophy class the classes are really good at getting everyone regardless of their experience with philosophy to have a productive discussion sometimes it can be dead silent sometimes because no one wants to talk or that one guy knows everything and always talks but it s generally not bad what s the lesson course plan like which one was your favorite here are my major reqs intro phil course general phil intro to asian phil into phil of law etc history of philosophy courses logic course value theory course metaphysics epistemology course philosophy electives the classes i took to satisfy reqs general philosophy intro took at a cc in hs plato amp aristotle history of phil methods of reasoning logic philosophy of law elective philosophy of mind elective buddhist metaphysics metaphysics medical ethics elective contemporary ecologies elective descartes hume amp kant history of phil faith and reason elective honors seminar thesis writing elective honors course moral dilemmas value theory my favorite professor was dope i got an awesome writing sample out of it and i learned a lot did you do a double major to compensate for your philosophy degree and what other majors did you choose nope but i do recommend everyone else double major if they are not sure they want to pursue philosophy at a higher level how do i qualify myself for the master s program in philosophy once i get into college depends where you re applying get good grades in the major in your philosophy courses but to be very competitive do an honor s thesis or a thesis if possible try independent study if not possible talk to your department head for advice and have solid relationships with your professors if you can publish an essay in an undergraduate journal of philosophy that would solidify your chances of getting in you ll need letters of rec at least in philosophy a writing sample pages a personal statement and some schools require the gre and last question who is your favorite philosopher he s not my favorite in a good way but i find his work fascinating peter singer sorry this was very long but i wanted to be as clear as possible on how philosophy is at the undergraduate level in the end take some courses and see how you feel good luck let me know if you have any questions', 'what areas of philosophy interest you i have almost graduated with a philosophy degree have always been interested in computer science and almost chose cs over philosophy am yet to find out whether the philosophy degree was a mistake for a cs career lol if you did go for philosophy in the end i would at least recommend opting to take modules related to computer science mathematical logic philosophy of maths philosophy of mind and philosophy of psychology metaphysics too maybe you could look into philosophy of ai there is more to it than just ethics as most people might think i have also found that philosophical thinking especially the more analytic stuff sometimes reminds me of programming can t remember exactly what but was reading an ethics paper that seemed to be describing a system of views that used recursion sometimes i wonder if some philosopher would benefit from programming knowledge anyways philosophy and computer are not unrelated another point there are plenty of cs online courses bootcamps etc and generally more people know what programming is than people really know what philosophy is so it will be easier to self teach and find community for computer science than philosophy if you want to learn both serious academic philosophy is hard to come by outside of university unfortunately of course if your interest in employability vastly outweighs your interest in philosophy just choose the computer science major pros and cons']

others i have above average leadership and interpersonal skills mostly when compared to other computer geeks and i really see myself becoming a manager at some point when i either reach my engineering potential or a management position looks more appealing i certainly could see myself pursuing an mba as well while employed later down the road before i make the jump over to management again this is do both quit your phd and focus on learning machine learning data science skills or forget about that and really focus on finishing your phd a lot of it depends what kind of job you want and what kind of programming skills you currently have tl dr a computational stem research job in academia is almost certainly impossible to get if you don t have a phd however many phd dropouts do pretty well transitioning to being an analyst programming etc in the corporate world or government and a lot of people there either won t know or won t care how long it took you to leave your phd treat your research like a job on your resume if you have more specific questions please ask didn t realize what a wall of text this was until i posted it sorry lol', 'hello i m in desperate need for advice from people who have more experience than me in the field particularly cog sci cs ai cog neuro i would really appreciate any input i come from a math biomed background did a master s in computational neuroscience and during that time i exposed myself to neuro ai related research after more of my own research reading attending seminars though i found myself most intrigued by cog sci related topics in particular i ve been really interested in understanding and replicating human level intelligence in artificial agents and the questions regarding knowledge representation and learning in humans so after graduation i applied to do a phd in the us in computational cognitive science in psychology departments i didn t get into any of the programs i applied to i turned away to look for jobs in the industry for a bit but realized soon that the jobs that i can get with my qualifications so far don t interest me so right now i m a bit torn i think it s obvious that i have to continue my studies to end up doing what i want to do to be honest i don t even have a clear picture of what i want to do after grad school perhaps because i don t fully know what options are available in the industry all i can think of is deepmind google but i don t think i want to continue on in academia but if i wanted industry jobs i think i d be better off approaching my area of interest through cs the downsides of this option is that on top of what will already be two gap years by april i ll have to spend more time doing another short undergrad master s to build up comp sci coursework to even qualify do a phd in cs i ve never been fully detached from some kind of biomed throughout undergrad and master s so i fear that the coursework will be a waste of time if i realize pure cs isn t my thing i am aware that everyone has their own timeline and i should focus on my own i guess it just doesn t help when i see friends who are almost finished with their phds or mds while i feel pretty lost overall and then there s the option of applying to cognitive neuroscience labs i am based in canada and mostly looking in canada at the moment but open to international options i regretted not having looked more broadly last time around when applying because maybe computational cognitive science is a pretty niche field at the moment in any case i ve been trying to read more into the field s research but i can t seem to find research labs that align with what i ve mentioned perhaps my research interests in learning and reasoning knowledge representation is too broad i ve been trying to read more into areas like visual intelligence and language learning they re pretty new to me so i m having trouble absorbing info but i can probably get myself to like them more my biggest problem with this option though is that i can t picture my job prospects down the road in these cases i really wish i had a clearer bigger picture of things overall in terms of potential industry career options and of the current state of the art in research i feel like i m always behind with relevant research even though i try to keep up and that there s something i m not looking into to get me where i need to be i thought i broaden my research perspectives in general as for industry jobs what are some options i m not aware of and what do you think is the best way to get there once again i would appreciate any sort of neuro more relevant to prev research but job prospects unknown for phd or other options', 'it s been a long years but i m finally at the end of my phd i hope to finish by the end of the summer but at
