long reply but that post has since been deleted i realized i had a fairly comprehensive guide to the admissions process particularly with how to start approaching each component so i decided to expand and generalize my post i have a post on common application mistakes here it started off as a comprehensive post organized like this one but i decided to focus on the particular mistakes i observed reviewing dozens of applications instead it includes tips about most of the components of the application process but with a focus on the application package itself when planning the application process with families i break it down into seven to ten parts depending on if the applicant is a us citizen permanent resident or not and whether the student has exceptional talent as an athlete performing artist fine artist academic or author here are my recommendations for someone starting out between june and september before or during senior year what north americans and most europeans call summer preliminary steps first decide whether this year is the right year to apply many students especially those not familiar with the process and in international curricula like a levels and ib would be able to assemble a much stronger application after completing secondary education requirements the best time to apply will depend on many factors such as academic performance extracurricular experience and financial capacity but for those looking at competitive universities the answer is almost always after graduation whether you re a domestic or international student and regardless of your curriculum i have a guide here second figure out where your legal domicile and residence are and whether you ll be treated as an international student this will affect primarily where you will be eligible for in state tuition certain types of financial aid you ll be able to get and whether you will have to provide proof of being able to afford to attend a school requirements for domicile and residency vary from state to state including how undocumented non citizen residents are treated if you are not a citizen and you don t have a green card permanent residency you will probably be considered an international student no matter where you re living however there are certain other eligible non citizens who live in the united states such as those granted asylum and beneficiaries of law called daca i won t go into detail here but don t worry about what students you re competing against if you re not a citizen green card holder or other eligible non citizen the admissions process is the same regardless of where you live the only exception i can think of is that international students attending high school in the us may be eligible for interviews that students living abroad are not third if you are possibly applying this year create an account for the common application i generally recommend it over scoir coalition but your school counselor may have a different preference which should take priority there are other applications accepted by multiple universities such as the universal application and one for historically black colleges and universities but the common app and scoir are the most widely accepted i won t go into the relative strengths and weaknesses here but i highly recommend the common app particularly for international students to the coalition s credit their application has improved a bit for international students but it s still annoying to use parts of the college admissions process and what to do now academics academics focus on doing well you may also have some choice for classes this fall with various electives the ideal senior year curriculum will include english mathematics one or two physical sciences one or two other humanities or social science subjects e g economics history psychology art music and a language other than english the optimal balance will depend on your desired major s primary courses of study if you are a very gifted student and are aiming for the most competitive universities consider enrolling in community college courses formal online courses and or preparing for ap a level or ib exams to compensate for weaknesses in your curriculum particularly if you re applying next year a level exams are available in the fall although it will almost surely be a huge task to prepare for them now if maintaining high grades is a challenge for you focus on that you don t want to overextend yourself and compromise your performance in school there are also academic considerations not directly related to admissions such as receiving college credit for exams and the possibility of graduating early for international students the ib will be sufficient and good performance very impressive as it s a challenging curriculum but other international curricula can make it difficult for those in cie gcse as a levels the most competitive applicants will be enrolled in five a level courses or self studying if their school doesn t allow it this still might not cover all of the above so you ll have to make some compromises on the curriculum those in local curricula such as india s cbse icse isc and state curricula will likely be missing a few of the above i have some specific suggestions for cie and local curriculum students but it s too much detail for this post and highly dependent on a student s interests abilities and schedule standardized tests sat act standardized tests if you ve taken south asian would say given the sat or act consider whether you feel you ve performed at your highest potential in most situations if you re aiming for competitive universities your second priority beyond academics should be to do as well as possible even if that means having to take the test multiple times which test to take how many times and when to take them will be situation schedule and ability dependent extracurricular commitments should be a consideration as well if you re not applying to college this year you can take standardized tests after you complete high school if that s more convenient for you the university of california which used to require that tests be taken before graduation no longer uses tests extracurricular activities ecs extracurricular activities i usually recommend making sure you have substantial extracurricular commitments in five categories intellectual pursuits outside the classroom leadership service activism self improvement hobby and physical activity that doesn t mean you need to have five extracurricular activities as one ec can fit into multiple categories the first three are essential for the most competitive colleges and the last two are highly recommended there are additional categories that are course and situation dependent such as pre professional business experience and family responsibilities which ecs to continue and what new endeavors to pursue is highly dependent on your interests and beyond the scope of this post i ve written about an overall strategy focusing on intellectualism here although such a sophisticated approach isn t necessary if you re not applying to very competitive colleges essays essays depending on your commitments this summer and your extracurricular experiences it might make sense to start working on your essays right now or it may not some of the students i work with have already started drafts while others i ve advised to wait i describe the timeline in the post above we usually get the main common app essay done in about two weeks although it s not usually top priority in july and i m more flexible you ll need a lot of time for essays but apart from the main common application and scoir coalition essay and the university of california personal insight questions short essays most of the prompts won t be available until september i don t recommend writing college specific essays in advance based on last year s prompts as they are changing more frequently now a few of my students and i learned that the hard way in recent years with harvard upenn and columbia the university of chicago has published its traditionally quirky prompts so that s something you can get started on as for essay content that s beyond the scope of this post but in the intellectual pursuits post above i have some discussion about essays as a way to bring context to your academic and extracurricular pursuits and demonstrate your communication and critical thinking skills if you don t read a lot start reading fiction preferably not young adult and memoirs of people you admire the more good writing you read the more you ll internalize what good writing is and the better your writing will be if you re not applying this year don t worry about essays right now but consider taking notes on your intellectual and extracurricular experiences as you progress so you can later put them in context and read learn how to use chatgpt i ll probably have another post about uses for chatgpt in the admissions process generally which i m constantly exploring but it can be very helpful for essays i don t suggest that you use it to write essays but it can provide excellent feedback and make competent editorial suggestions the more familiar you are with good writing the better you ll be able to judge whether chatgpt s suggestions help you achieve your goals letters of recommendation lors letters of recommendation now is the time to think about whom you might ask for lors for most competitive colleges you ll need at least three one from your school college or guidance counselor or preferably an administrator if your school doesn t have one and two teachers who have taught you an academic subject academic lors the most competitive colleges recommend or even require letters from one stem science technology engineering mathematics teacher and one non stem teacher social science humanities or arts economics can be considered either in a pinch but i generally advise a math or physical science teacher and an english humanities or other social science teacher for some international students english is the only humanities subject and there are no social science or arts many schools allow a third teacher lor but this rarely adds a lot that s new and can take away time from admissions officers focusing on other parts of your application other lors if you have a major extracurricular activity involving a supervisor such as a coach or mentor it s a good idea to get an lor from that person too many schools now accept peer letters of recommendation this can be from a schoolmate or recent graduate college student who knows you well letters from politicians and government officials can be helpful in some circumstances but usually not as much as parents and students think the most influential lors will come from someone who knows you well and has an education from an institution similar to the ones you ll be applying to i discuss lors in more detail here the post is aimed at international students but the advice regarding lors applies to domestic students as well in general it will be helpful to prepare brag sheets to provide for your recommenders highlighting your relevant strengths and stories that demonstrate your positive qualities college selection interviews and campus visits college selection i ll probably have another post with tips on how to select colleges but the process should start now if you don t have test scores yet you should wait until you get your results to finalize your college list but now is a good time to create a preliminary list based on how you re doing and what academic areas you re interested in if you have test results you re happy with you can often narrow down your list a bit i have a post on how i select colleges here i explain in that post why i don t use the categories of target safety reach and dream but rather focus on whether or not to apply to any given school and crafting a list that makes sense interviews and campus visits interviews most of the top private schools offer interviews for national research universities alumni interviews are usually offered after you submit your application however some require that you request an alumni interview usually from september onward many liberal arts colleges lacs allow you to schedule online or in person meetings with admissions officers they re usually informational rather than evaluative at this stage but participating in one can demonstrate interest which many schools particularly lacs track there are a few non lacs with interviews you have to sign up for such as harvard and notre dame so check the schools you re interested in offerings may differ whether you re a domestic or international student for example at harvard even domestic students should consider signing up for a recorded interview on a platform such as initialview if you re not completely fluent in english immerse yourself with american english speakers to increase your fluency as english proficiency is an important factor you ll be evaluated on in a live interview campus visits now is not the best time to visit college campuses because most personnel are not there and most classes aren t in session but you may want to consider visits to a few colleges you re interested in regardless of whether they track interest this applies to international students too as long as your family can comfortably afford it i love to plan road trips and i ve crafted a few for families traveling through california the great lakes region upper midwest and upstate new york and new england i also visited all eight ivy league colleges in one day although i wouldn t recommend that to prepare for admissions i recommendation is two school visits per day of travel morning and afternoon you can often arrange to spend a day or two on campus attending classes once the academic year starts in september and even stay a weekend with a student it helps to know someone already but the admissions office might be able to find someone for you financial aid and scholarships i m not going to write a comprehensive guide to scholarships and financial aid e g need vs merit based need aware vs blind efc required forms etc here but this is what you need to do to get started students should have a candid discussion with their families about how much they can afford for college and how much debt they re willing to take on this can be difficult for many families as most parents don t discuss financial details with their non adult children if parents are very uncomfortable with that they can share details with a counselor school or private to give them an idea about the range of colleges that are feasible if they haven t already they should also speak with a qualified financial advisor about financing options if you re a domestic student there are various external scholarships you can apply to if you re an international student there are a few major international scholarships but see if your government has a program to sponsor study abroad and find out its conditions such as the schools that are eligible note that the ability of financial aid will depend heavily on whether or not you re a us citizen or permanent resident i ve assembled a database of college based merit scholarships for international students here many of those scholarships are available to domestic students as well and just happen to be open to international students regardless make sure your parents taxation filings e g form schedule c and w in the us and itr in india are up to date and that they know how to obtain documentation of income and assets the tax documents you ll need are those that cover the most recent tax year that has ended already so you can prepare copies now unless you re filing in october in the us assets on the other hand should be documented later this includes investments real estate valuables and ownership interest in businesses they re operating exceptional talent athletics arts science or literature athletic recruiting if you are an athlete who might perform at a level comparable to current athletes at colleges you want to attend check their athletic rosters to see where you might fit in competitively with performance statistics and physical attributes the athletic recruiting process can begin very early as early as before ninth grade but there may still be room on teams this year note that getting recruited for a university athletic team does not legally obligate you to play but it s wrong to get recruited in bad faith and it could have consequences from peers from your school or entire country if you re an international student who actually want to play sports you ll want to create a one page student athlete profile with your grades including gpa if you have one and test scores along with at least one picture and performance statistics you ll also need video of yourself playing during a real competition particularly tournament play you can reach out to coaches on your own but i recommend working with a professional recruiting consultant with experience and knowledge of different programs arts if you re a fine artist assemble a portfolio of your works if you re a performing artist prepare recordings video or audio as appropriate of your notable performances if you re applying to a fine arts performing arts school or particularly prestigious program at a general studies university you may need to get a professional evaluation if you re applying to a regular college that happens to have fine arts offerings usually just a portfolio is sufficient if you don t have portfolio worthy artistic works or performances plan to create some academia and literature if you have made a significant contribution to the physical sciences social sciences or literature then let your academic supervisor s and associates know that you re planning to apply to college they may be able to introduce you to professors who have influence with admissions offices if you have accomplishments not guided by anyone in particular you can cold email professors with similar interests how to identify such professors and write those emails is beyond the scope of this post but in general professors in niche subjects are happy when anyone shares their interest i have a student who is a lyricist and composer of traditional music recently get a reply from an ivy league professor that he d put in a good word for the student at the admissions office when application season rolls around a few years ago i had a student who cold emailed a professor and ended up working on research with mathematics graduate students at another ivy league school where he eventually was admitted these emails take just a few minutes to write but can have a big impact on admissions plus the process of identifying appropriate recipients and corresponding with professors can help you figure out what colleges might be the best fit for you visa visa for international students if you are not legally entitled to reside in the united states you will need a visa usually that is an f student visa the most important thing non us citizens can do is prepare the financial information in the scholarships and financial aid section more recent documentation of income than last year s tax filings is recommended for the certification of finances which most colleges need at some point particularly if you re an international student and visa interview the student visa process doesn t start until after you decide where to enroll if you ve never traveled outside your country and are from somewhere us visas often get rejected consider an international trip to any country to establish a travel history i have a comprehensive post about visa interviews here the interview doesn t take place until a few weeks to months before classes begin but you should read it to understand what preparation you might need for a successful bid also if you travel to the united states to visit colleges you ll need a tourist visa visiting colleges is a well known and appealing purpose to travel to the united states if you complete an interview for a tourist visa you likely won t have to complete an interview again for a student visa citizens of countries which have visa free or visa on arrival travel privileges to the us obviously don t need an interview for tourism purposes and can often get a waiver for student visas english proficiency if you have ever attended high school th grade or later in a country other than the united states canada the uk ireland australia or new zealand except on an exchange program then you should plan to take an english proficiency test this advice includes recent immigrants to the united states and students from countries such as india the philippines and nigeria where english is an official language and even if you re enrolled in an english medium international school and will be exempt from english proficiency tests at most colleges if you re applying to colleges in the united states and canada only toefl is the most preferred after that comes ielts then the duolingo english test i discuss my reasoning here if you re applying to english language programs outside the us and canada it s fine to take only ielts but i often recommend both toefl and ielts if it s not too much of a financial burden i recognize that this is not common advice you re more likely to hear or read that english proficiency tests are just a box to check off and you should get a waiver if you can and it doesn t matter what score you have as long as you meet the minimum requirement but i believe that in the era of chatgpt and test optional admissions english proficiency scores are extremely important just as everyone says that you should submit a high sat score i recommend that you submit a high english proficiency score on toefl with in each section and on all sections of the ielts is indisputably high the best thing you can do to prepare for english proficiently tests is not to study for the test itself but to immerse yourself in english and actually increase your fluency i also recommend completing an interview through the intialview platform to demonstrate your conversational skills although this year there may be more options if you decide to work with a professional counselor who is a native speaker of north american or british english it s often beneficial to take a proficiency test and complete a recorded interview as late in the process as is reasonable as a personal aside i find that students conversational and writing skills improve over the course of the process as they may be writing dozens of essays and spend many hours conversing with me in casual american english i particularly like to explain idioms and figures of speech conclusion that should cover just about everything process wise i know it s a lot to take in so feel free to ask for clarification or more information in the comments i will focus on replying to questions and comments that are relevant to many people']

hard work will be worth it or will these qa test automation sdet jobs be replaced by ai or more skilled engineers like some people online say thanks', 'i am going to be graduating soon and was just wondering how people think the progression of ai will go in the next years after the chatgpt craze i would be lieng to you if i said i didn t feel a little nervous about the future of my chosen industry but then again i don t really know much about llms or potential agis i know everyone here is tired of hearing these types of questions but i just want to know where people currently stand on the future of cs and ai i could probably sift through the countless other posts like this and find some answers but i wanted to ask more specific questions are we currently working towards a future where ai becomes increasingly more advanced with human help if so for what purpose other than automation of human labor do you believe that the automation of cs related fields is possible whether it be in the far or near future if you believe it is not possible explain why why is automation via llms or agi so often percieved as threat to the functioning of society and if you agree it is a looming threat then why are we trying to accelerate in its direction', 'i ve been on this career guidance sub reddit for many weeks now largely on my own i have been doing extensive research into the rapid rise of specific kinds of automation that are especially prone to reducing jobs and displacing workers on this topic i ve even taken a class from mit online the more i research this problem the more fascinated i become and the more urgent and concerning the magnitude seems i m sure i m not the only one who sees major change coming for all workers everywhere i d like to do something about it become part of the solution if i can the problem isn t a question of whether there will be jobs most likely there will be the problem is how to help more people navigate this very rapid and disruptive change with greater ease sanity and clarity up to million jobs in the us will be disrupted by automation in the next years according to the latest research citing mckinsey and others i am investigating possible new career options for myself for roles to help people whose jobs are or soon will be disrupted by automation i m talking to career coaches scholars authors and those at think tanks like the aspen institute on the one hand we are becoming ever more dependent on technology paradoxically and on the other hand technology has the power to displace more workers from all kinds of images and processes bank loans the future is already here it s just not everywhere yet hopefully this means more of us can free up more time at work and in our jobs to be more well human to spend more time engaging with others and to focus more of our attention and work efforts on empathy care collaboration connection and creativity on this topic there are some interesting new books published recently including futureproof rules for humans in the age of automation by kevin roose and work disrupted opportunity resilience and growth in the accelerated future of work by jeff schwartz and suzannne riss some examples include technologies like robotic process automation rpa low code no code programming natural language processing and many emerging platforms based on artificial intelligence and machine learning to help put this exponential change into perspective at current rates of growth rpa alone is expected to have near universal adoption within the next years source ai multitude tl dnr can anyone recommend people for me to talk to about career coaching that is specifically tailored to the rising problem of job disruption due to automation']

Name: 85_plagiarism_detectors_prove_detection
Representative_Docs: ['recently there has been much discussion of the use of ai detectors and policies for discipline if a student s work scores higher than some arbitrary percentage this is despite the well known false positives and negatives these checkers create everybody including university administrators themselves agrees that the tools are highly unreliable the fact that it discriminates against students whose first language is not english fails to create accommodations for neurodiverse students generally fosters a climate of suspicion and mistrust between students and faculty which undermines the learning process and is inconsistent about where the limitations on their use should be drawn there are also ethical issues around universities that require all students to do additional work submission of earlier drafts etc as a type of collective punishment across the student body for what a few students may be guilty of and a perversion of legal principles making students guilty until proven innocent by a low score i am not a legal scholar but i think universities may be setting themselves up for more problems than they can imagine students accused of such misconduct and penalised may have recourse to the law and civil litigation for damages incurred for such claims this would require of the faculty that they demonstrate in a court that their detection tools are completely reliable something they simply can t do one could claim that students have voluntarily agreed to follow the rules of the university at registration but the courts generally require such rules to be reasonable and the inconsistencies about what is acceptable use and what is not across universities and even within schools intra university also mean they would not be able to do so this then places the university in the correct legal position it should be he who alleges must prove or face having to cough up court imposed financial penalties i think this was an important consideration that has led to major universities around the world discontinuing the use of ai detectors what do you guys think about this argument', 'in some cases they don t realize that they don t really understand the software and they feel justified that the evidence is foolproof when it is not in other cases they may be shifting the determination of whether a violation occurred to a third party institutional committee review process this is not necessarily out of paranoia it could be that the professor is not positive and wants more seasoned eyes on the case if we are speaking of similarity reports those are generally quite accurate for what they are designed to do they correctly identify matches to content elsewhere we must then open the reports and look at flagged matches to see if plagiarism occurred unfortunately some professors rely on the raw percentage match and make threats like the similarity report needs to be under x that rule might coincide with a strong plagiarism free paper most of the time but similarity checkers cannot distinguish between true plagiarism and coincidental matches i see this most often with references headings restatements of questions in assignments etc if we are speaking of the ai detector that turnitin recently introduced that thing is wildly unreliable and should not be trusted at surface value i ve caught a couple of submissions with ai but that wasn t because of turnitin s new tool that was because the writing was vague misaligned with the project and clearly not the work of the students in question in any situations like that the professor needs to have a conversation with the student in which it will become readily apparent if the student simply relied on ai output rather than authoring their own work in either case humans must apply their human eyes to any flagged content not doing so is misguided and irresponsible', 'as a student i ll explain the logic teacher said their going to use ai detector to make sure you didn t cheat almost every student has had an experience where the ai detector falsely flagged them or a friend of theirs thus forcing them to defend their work and prove to the administration that it wasn t written by ai so now when you finish an assignment and go to turn it in a little voice in your head says remeber last time when u didn t use ai and the teacher claimed you did so you had to fight to prove you didn t so then you say well how can i ensure that doesn t happen again ahh i know i ll use an ai checker bc if it flags it right now then i can make changes to the parts that seem suspicious generally speaking your right in that you should just submit the assignment but it doesn t change the fact that students are unfairly accused of cheating due to these shitty ai detection systems which causes everyone to become anxious that they too will be falsely accused because nothing has been done to prevent this from happening again so they use ai detectors in order to avoid being falsely accused it s the anxiety of possibly having your reputation and academic career ruined most smart students realize after a little while that it doesn t matter bc their all so shitty that you can t take precaution to prevent this situation from happening']

day break up what you need to study in sections if worst comes to worst offer to f k your teacher', 'i read a mind for numbers by barbara oakley because i want to get better at working with numbers and learning the book was really helpful and i wish i had this when i was in college wanted to share some of my notes here and recommend that you consider reading this book if you want to improve how you study and learn these are some of my notes my favorite quotes those who are the most disagreeable tend to be the most creative this is because creative people challenge existing answers and assumptions merely glancing at the solution to a problem and thinking you truly know it yourself is one of the most common illusions of competence in learning the dread of doing a task uses up more time and energy than doing a task itself procrastination can be like taking tiny amounts of poison it may not seem harmful at the time but the long term effects can be very damaging one might say that work substitutes for talent or better yet that work creates talent simple explanations are possible for almost any concept no matter how complex key questions how can you avoid falling into the trap of thinking that quicker people are automatically more clever can you find ways to turn your disadvantages into advantages general notes two modes of thinking focused mode diffused mode when you procrastinate when studying you are leaving yourself only enough time to do superficial focused mode learning the resulting neural patterns will be faint and fragmented and will quickly disappear use the focused mode to first start grappling with concepts and problems in math and science after you ve done your first hard focused work allow the diffuse mode to takeover relax and do something different those who are the most disagreeable tend to be the most creative this is because creative people challenge existing answers and assumptions focused attention can often help solve problems but it can also create problems by blocking your ability to see new solutions one of the first steps towards gaining expertise in math and science is to create conceptual chunks mental leaps that unite separate bits of information through meaning basic steps to forming a chunk step focus your attention on the information you want to chunk no television on in the background don t look at your phone etc step understand the basic idea you are trying to chunk step gaining context so you see not just how but also when to use this chunk when learning a new concept look at sample problems with worked out solutions one disadvantage of looking at worked out examples to form chunks is that it can be easy to focus too much on why an individual step works and not on the connection between steps when you first begin to learn something you are making new neural patterns and connecting them with pre existing neural patterns that are spread through many areas of your brain if you chunk without understanding it is a useless chunk that won t fit in with other material you are learning learning takes place in two ways a bottom up chunking process practice and repetition help you build and strengthen each chunk so you can easily gain access to it when needed a top down big picture process it allows you to see where what you are learning fits in context is where bottom up and top down learning meet chunking how to use a problem solving technique context when to use that technique instead of some other technique illusions of competence and the importance of recall attempting to recall the material you have learned retrieval practice is far more effective than simply re reading the material many students experience illusions of competence when they are studying when you have the book open in front of you it provides the illusion that the material is already in your brain you don t want to wait too long for the retrieval practice try to recall the material you ve learned within a day if at all possible re write your notes in the evening after a lecture once you have something down you can expand the time between upkeep repetitions to weeks or months getting a concept in class versus being able to apply it to solving a genuine physical problem is the difference between a student and a full blown scientist or engineer your brain has roughly four spots available in working memory when you are first chunking a concept it takes up all four spots once a concept is chunked it takes up only one slot in working memory merely glancing at the solution to a problem and thinking you truly know it yourself is one of the most common illusions of competence in learning pick a mathematical or scientific concept from your notes read it over then look away and see what you can recall working toward understanding what you are recalling at the same time then glance back re read the concept and try again the ability to combine chunks in novel ways underlies much of historical innovation reference the book where good ideas come from by steven johnson the difference between creative scientists and technically competent but non imaginative ones is their breadth of interest the bigger your chunked mental library the more easily you will be able to solve problems as you gain more experience in chunking the chunks you are able to create become bigger two ways to solve problems through sequential step by step reasoning focused mode through more holistic intuition creative diffused mode in building a chunked library you are training your brain to recognize not only a specific problem but different types and classes of problems so that you can automatically know how to quickly solve whatever you encounter you will start to see patterns that simplify problem solving for you the retrieval process itself enhances deep learning and helps us begin forming chunks it can help to recall material when you are in a physically different location than where you learned the material doing something physically active is especially helping when you are trying to grasp a key idea interleaving versus overlearning interleaving means practice by doing a mixture of different kinds of problems requiring different strategies mastering a new subject means learning to select and use the proper technique for a problem you learn this by practicing problems that require different techniques to solve them emphasize interleaving instead of overlearning overlearning means continuing to study or practice a problem immediately after some criterion has been achieved for example correctly solving a certain type of math problem and then immediately solving several more problems of the same kind overlearning provides diminishing returns techniques for limited study time read but don t yet solve assigned homework and practice exams review lecture notes rework example problems presented in lecture notes work assigned homework and practice exam questions chunking verses choking how to increase your expertise and reduce anxiety this section discusses how to enhance and refine chunks steps to building a powerful chunk work a key problem all the way through on paper do another repetition of the problem paying attention to the key processes take a break gives your diffuse mode time to internalize the problem sleep do another repetition add a new problem repeat steps on the second problem do active repetitions mentally review while doing something active testing in itself is a powerful learning experience it changes and adds to what you know also making dramatic improvements in your ability to retain the material this is known as the testing effect build a mental solution library a key to building mental flexibility and expertise is to build your library of chunked solution patterns summary of chunking chunking means integrating a concept into one smoothly connected neural thought pattern chunking helps increase the amount of working memory you have available building a chunked library of concepts and solutions helps build intuition in problem solving when building a chunked library it is important to keep deliberate focus on the toughest concepts overcoming procrastination we develop a passion for what we are good at it is a mistake to think that if we are not good at something we do not have and can never develop a passion for it keep a planner journal so you can easily track when you reach your goals and observe what does and doesn t work write your planned tasks out the night before arrange your work into a series of small challenges don t blame all your problems on external factors enhancing your memory we have outstanding visual and spatial memory systems the memory palace technique placing memorable nudges in a scene that is familiar to you allows you to dip into the strength of your visual memory system see this article on litemind for more information learning to use your memory in a more disciplined yet creative manner helps you learn to focus your attention by memorizing material you understand you can internalize the material in a profound way one of the best things you can do to remember and understand concepts in math and science is to create a metaphor or analogy for them use spaced repetition to help lodge ideas in memory create meaningful groups that simplify the material you are trying to remember if you are memorizing something commonly used search online to see if someone has already come up with a particularly memorable memory trick beware of mistaking a memory trick for actual knowledge there is a direct connection between your hand and your brain the act of organizing and re writing your notes is essential to breaking down large amounts of information into smaller digestible chunks writing and saying what you are trying to learn seems to enhance retention several recent studies have shown that regular exercise can make a substantive improvement in your memory and learning ability the rest of my summary is here']

Name: 97_chatgpt_students_gpt_chat
Representative_Docs: ['i ta d for a professor this semester and she took steps like this to help mitigate ai usage the thing about this is and i showed her you can feed the rubric lecture transcript and those sources into chat gpt to analyze and then provide the prompt and it will generate a relatively decent and in depth response she had a scaffolded assignment that i used as an example and chat gpt compiled legitimate resources that i then quickly reviewed generated an outline lit review and statement of purpose and first draft in all under an hour she looked it over and with just a quick glance said it looked like a or b level work i then provided some feedback to chat gpt for the final draft and it popped it out and you can do this for any stage but in one day actually just an hour or so an entire semester long assignment was completed i considered doing my thesis on the ethical use of emerging technologies so i ve played around with multiple platforms like chat gpt and others that create presentations watch and respond to youtube videos others that summarize sources and many more and with these programs only becoming more advanced being linked to the internet and chat gpt being able to learn and match writing styles it s going to get really difficult to stop and with so many students moving to at least one online class and an over a third taking exclusively online classes they ll always find a way around whatever is out in place', 'the intro bio course i ta ed for a few times is notorious for its academic honesty issues even before chatgpt it was very easy to find example pdfs of the written assignments online the supervisor tried to have us at least mention it with our students at the start of the semester we can t stop you from using it but we don t encourage it and incorporated a section into the assignments where students could at least disclose what they were using ai for e g checking their submission met rubric guidelines coherence etc some students took us up on it but they were the ones that clearly had decent writing skills already and were just using chatgpt for polishing a few tas suggested that we asked them instead for the link to the chat if they used it but we couldn t enforce this only a few semesters ago i could more easily compare the writing quality to what i knew of the student and make a semi confident call if it was ai or not but it s a lot harder now i didn t notice until the end of the semester how one student had submitted mostly stellar written assignments but bombed most of the in person assessments lol but our course ultimately is not able to adequately handle this it s an intro level course and a good chunk of the students in the course are non majors upperclassman just trying to meet a graduation requirement the supervisor did care but really only the most egregious uses across the s of students taking this course were caught realistically just not worth the effort sidenote and i think this is all just stemming from the larger issue of higher education being not worth the sticker price and students understandably taking shortcuts where they think they can to get that degree great essay by jared henderson on this i can t exactly blame a nd semester senior econ major for using chatgpt to write a lab report on intro bio experiment that they ll never think about again but at the same time additional submission of ai prompt generation history as well as requiring students to source lecture materials somewhere in the assignment sounds about as good as anyone can do to deter inappropriate are allocated amp x b admin for people who think they can manage the project experienced for people who have experience of either development or a specific technology you do not have to be an expert complicated amp x b any experienced help in doing this will be gratefully received even if you do not intend to assist in the long term', 'gt i m at the tail end of my cs degree at university naturally this has involved working on a lot of programming assignments and i ve found that the algorithm data structures concepts i ve learned to actually be pretty useful in many of the assignments i ve written the skills of a software developer are simply different from skills of a computer scientist it s like the people who associate being good at math with being good at physics one does not necessarily imply the other i know plenty of pure mathematicians majors who just avoid physics whenever possible in general those who are good at math tend to be better at physics but that is not always the case amp x b gt it just turns out that using x data structure can make the program run much better and why not actually apply the concepts we ve learned it s just that everyday programming don t need much data structure knowledge one just needs to have a general understanding of the structures say hashmap to utilize hashmaps in code one does not need to implement it or do some recursion iterative process on it in the real world in fact recursion is heavily discouraged due to heap issues and basically never found outside interviews the technique of recursion feels as important as knowing the first hundred digits of pi good for showing off and feeling good but unimportant for solving real world problems and in the real world why would you need to learn to reverse a linked list say you really need to reverse something you would do reverse and any variant you come up with yourself even if it is theoretically faster would most likely be far slower than the popular libraries since the libraries are already heavily optimized in c and think of all the readability issues say you could outperform the reverse by for that you trade off with more lines of code do you think your co workers and future co workers would enjoy reading that code probably not and think about it carefully say we were developing an app that shows your bank account balance when do you realistically use data structures most likely there will be a few rest api calls www bank account calls on a page data structures just aren t really that important in everyday workforce amp x b gt is this true in your experience if so what do you actually care about while programming design patterns writing legible code devops figuring out customer expectations yes design patterns clean code and at end of day meeting customer expectations on time which by the way almost all the time no one has a clue what he or she wants so ya a developer s job is to figure out a solution to a sometimes nonexistant problem this includes contacting all sorts of people to accomplish your task hence why some developers are adamant on differentiating themselves with programmers anyways say an array might consume a bit less memory than a list however foregoing an arraylist simply because of a minute issue at the time could lead to scalability issues much later on the industry constantly changes and pigeonholing for an optimal solution at the time could just lead to more headaches later on real life programming is not as simple as this is better so solutions tend to be generalized and even if there were more efficient solutions out there with bit tricks and all at the end of day a code that is not as readable is just worthless code to everyone else it s a trade off with optimal answer also in the real world it s generally not the performance complexity that is the issue the issue is something entirely different figuring out what your manager consumer needs from you with a set deadline making money is more important than doing fibonacci sequence in o memory there s also the issue with security networking and all in the real world also leetcode teaches nothing much about good coding practice i don t care if you can write all your solution in main function or a few functions the ability to organize your code to multiple projects is just not found with leetcode questions and in the real world choosing languages and environments matter something again not found in leetcode say you were doing quick machine learning work python for its sckitlearn numpy scipy tensorflow would be the choice say you were dealing with kafka scala would be the choice say you were dealing with front end some variant of javascript would be the choice and so on and many times even if there are better environments languages more suited for the task it is better to simply forego them as your team s change could affect every other team s tasks library versions and all are headaches making these judgements are invaluable skill sets in the real world in leetcode people don t learn about this or at least don t have to be exposed much to such scenarios and using packages developed by others to quickly finish your task instead of reinventing the wheel being able to google bing stackoverflow is priceless handling large projects multiple services libraries etc and debugging are also critical and writing unit tests etc skills these skills are unfound in leetcode questions real world programming also includes lots of e mails meetings conferences powerpoints etc which none of them can be found in leetcode yes presentations and all are important if you can t present in front of the higher ups don t expected to go far in this career leadership traits are wanted in almost every white collar field and software is no exception i would argue these soft skills will get you far far further in life than the hard skills like raw coding plus become too good at the hard skill and you end up becoming irreplaceable aka not get promoted it s a dumb system but it s the system we live in amp x b and like most other jobs software development at end of day doesn t need x programmers despite what the media states it needs members that people can get along with in the team a x programmer in the real world are people who can abstract out company tasks and distribute evenly to others i hate to admit it but many of the traits found in business majors would help developers go far in this field granted the person knows how to write clean code gets along with ppl etc once you go above senior developer you realize you pretty much never program it s like attack on titans as you get promoted you get further away from the actual role i guess it s like every other career mcdonald s ceo does nothing remotely similar to mcdonald s worker shrugs at bad analogy but hopes op understands']

Name: 104_bootcamp_data_boot camp_analyst
Representative_Docs: ['i m and in a data science boot camp right now after moving away from accounting which has actually been a really nice segue bc it plays into my skill sets here s my quick take if you re motivated and willing to really immerse yourself in learning something new it can be great if you think it s like college where you ll get a job offer from completing a program that s not the case the boot camp has been great in introducing me to new technologies and how the different frameworks work in relation to languages and how to connect the dots so to speak also learning what stuff is junk outdated or obsolete learning actual industry practices from people in the industry i already have a bs and years of professional work experience so going back to college for another cs bs isn t really an option i am however considering getting a masters but even still most masters degrees in data science are more related to learning machine learning and ai and i m happy to stay in the analytics role although i am still applying for the masters program to that end do your research not all boot camps are created equal there s different pros and cons some guarantee a job some offer repayment upon getting hired some are just straight tuition based additionally you ll need to decide whether or not you have in class local options or if you want online remote at the end of the day when it comes to getting hired a lot of people have already touched on this but getting that first job is a trick after years of experience you re pretty sought after that being said you re portfolio is really what potential employers want to see and that goes beyond class work recruiters know what they re looking at if it s an assignment you need to take what you ve learned in boot camp and create your own projects i ll definitely say this i could have learned everything i m learning at home alone online but it would ve taken forever to understand how technologies work together boot camps are great bc they cut through the noise and have an organized curriculum activities and homework you ll do projects where you ll see how to combine different technologies together instead of just learning one thing until you re blue in the face additionally you have access to instructors and tas if you get stuck and at least for my boot camp they are really interested in the students being successful and bred afterwards bc it s good advertisement for them we have a pretty solid career guidance that helps with resumes linkedin portfolio websites what sort of language to use to get through hr filters etc', 'hi friends i m a mechatronics engineer trying to ultimately transition to field of business analytics i am still not even sure whether after i became a business analyst i want to get management skills and go project management direction or i want to get more machine learning skills and go business intelligence direction the typical business analyst job posts show that they only need some sql and visualization skills as well as some other business skills the reason i was attracted to the data science bootcamps e g brainstation was the fact that most graduate manage to get some job within a reasonable period of time however according to my research most of the jobs may be in the data analytics level rather than data scientist level i would be okay with a data analyst job for the first step but according to the course curriculum only one unit is related to database and sql and the rest is more advanced material that entry level jobs e g data business analyst does not really require now i was wondering whether you think for someone in my situation is it best to quickly get a project based online sql and tableau course and start applying for jobs or do you think undertaking this program still could be worth it for me more specifically how quickly would i be able to get a business data analyst job if i just spend a couple month taking massive amount of online course and building my resume how much do you thing the career services in these bootcamps are effective in graduates getting the jobs more specifically do you know how many percent of graduate get hired by the very companies that are with the bootcamps how much percent of the content of the course would you think would not be used in a data analyst position i would appreciate any further suggestions you might have for me regards', 'i m really interested in feedback from people in the industry on whether it s worthwhile for me to pursue some additional skills certifications via boot camp or whether i would be throwing my money away currently i m an it business analyst for a government entity i have about years of experience in this type of work i come from a liberal arts educational background and kind of fell into ba work by chance then found out i really liked it i routinely conduct trainings perform qa work create resource materials for end users design workflows gather requirements and write use cases user stories etc i have experience working in agile however my tech skills are still a weak spot i work alongside other bas from programming development backgrounds and i m envious of their technical hard skills i ve been reading more about data analytics and it interests me i like the idea of taking a mass of data and using tools to draw out useful information i m looking at a boot camp program through rutgers with a data science track that purports to set you up with the tech skills you need to work as a data analyst my question is are companies looking for bas who can also function as data analysts and as a somewhat experienced ba with new data science skills could i expect a salary jump that would make the boot camp cost worth it for reference my current job will top out under six figures if i m promoted to the next highest ba title which won t happen super soon but probably would eventually i can expect to top out at low six figures if the cost of the boot camp was lower i would dive in just to acquire the skills and expand my repertoire but at almost k i would need to know the course boosted my earnings potential for it to be a reasonable investment thanks for any feedback you can provide']

Name: 106_read_paper_reading_papers
Representative_Docs: ['multiple readings is a study strategy that helped me instead of focusing so much into not missing anything you can skim through the papers at different levels read abstracts and other metadata first select a few articles and skim through headings first and last lines of paragraphs image captions select articles you want to read more closely even those read a couple of times top to bottom really fast scanning for prominent points schedule two hour sessions to delve into those articles you decide to read the most closely draw a section concept diagram use sidenotes by the paragraphs and subject headings in the margin complete the concept diagram with the sidenotes and headings review once before the end of the session reread important articles let the information sink and come back to it a couple days later inhibiting filtering out unnecessary input selecting the right materials summarizing and organizing information drawing associations to what already know reflect on what you read with essays and other writing exercises including thoughtful long form note taking these are the key skills of academic reading finally i only recently came across scite ai and i want to look more into it i thought that it might help filter out the noise when discovering literature', 'in science ai is going to be transformative and is absolutely something that shouldn t be banned but embraced in the last year to months i ve seen tools that reduce that hurdle of reading a paper in a brand new field by summarizing the entire field and explaining each concept in simple terms in the paper you are reading it s difficult for freshmen to understand the novelty of a paper when they don t even know what an elisa is or why a western isn t quantitative what the heck is a ligand enzyme or kd in this complicated paper about alphafold making new cancer drugs gone should be the days hoping to find a decent review paper but having ai being able to write at least a simple qualitative one tailored to your needs of course they also have to know the limitations of ai in its inability to parse the details or write anything that is anything beyond cursory maybe a good exercise is to compare a funded nih proposal with the best proposal any ai could write on the same topic and compare realize that if they got ai to generate fake sources the issue isn t that they used ai the issue is they were lazy sloppy and deceptive by just making up sources in hs we had a girl whose father worked for the bureau of labor stats and in oral debate she would just make up stats on the fly and this was before ai she basically made herself a joke knowing how to use ai will differentiate you from being a joke either in science never getting any proposal funded or being a business consultant having your clients withdraw their business because you don t seem like a legitimate expert from being a real subject matter expert two weeks ago the google ceo just admitted that ai now writes of all code for google like that we have to understand how to allow ai to replace redundant and simple repetitive tasks these tasks may have been considered skills in the past but something irrelevant now for other skills we need to reformulate the learning and evaluation in the s we could give students a task of finding three ancient asian scientists who studied astronomy but we couldn t give the same assignment today not expecting the students not to just use a basic google search', 'here are some toughts that helped me a paper reading is a skill that develops over time a significant amount of things in the paper are fluffy things that need not be read to understand the paper and only to clarify content or for low level details learning to spot what and what not to read will significantly reduce the amount of mental stress caused by reading b in the same vein read papers in three steps read the abstract if it sounds interesting make a rough read of the paper maybe like a minute read if it is really really interesting then you read everything at a latter date c i reach level in very fee papers in those i take notes then i usually try to discuss them with someone d seems you are in cs i have found than evaluation sections tend to add very little to the paper understanding so i usually skip them after a quick glance e just schedule some time to read every couple of days and take it as a light activity something to be listening music to only papers in the level are worthy of your full mental activity and of the slow grinding dull pain that comes from reading it at that depth f use a good paper manager medley or zotero once a paper is finished write some overall remarks about it these notes are indexable eg debates ai tunning of file system parameter uniqueness in their use of genetic algos really debatable the approach to fixing tunning the ai might be an interesting angle to cover evaluation is interesting could take a look at that for a paper background for auto tunning is very good get inspiration from it i write these notes at the end of a level read and serve me well to go back to the problem and remind me what the paper was and what was or not interested']

Name: 107_career_engineering_want_life
Representative_Docs: ['hi reddit i m a year old girl who just started my rd year at university ba hons in creative writing and english i m at a point where i really need to start figuring out what kind of career i want to go into but i have such a vague idea and i fear there are probably so many career options that i don t even know about i was hoping that if i list all the things that come to
