most people would probably ask chatgpt nowadays but i do not support use of ai despite my degree i don t think i want to be an author writer i like being part of creative projects but writing books just isn t really my forte i ve pondered the idea of screenwriting but i think the rate of success for that is niche and i want security in my career though i m willing to work in retail or at entry level leading up to it i want to do something creative i m willing to learn whatever skills for example i really want to start making miniatures when i move out and have space for craft materials but i think creating props for stop motion animated films is too niche to be an assured career i m a mediator personality type and our biggest priority in life is a career we love i know it takes passion and dedication to be one of the lucky few who gets into a job they genuinely adore but it s hard to dedicate myself to something if i don t know what it is yet i like the idea of careers that mix in person work remote work to break up the week a little bit or just some sort of variation rather than the same day to day routinely work i ve always thought my role might be in film tv there are so many names when the credits roll and i don t know what most of their jobs are i ve always wondered if any of them could be suited to me i prefer to work on something as part of a team rather than being responsible for others finally here s a bunch of things that inspire me music especially twenty one pilots their creative lore stop motion miniatures i m writing a stop motion screenplay for my dissertation based on fantastic mr fox among other things soundtracks specifically immersive world other universe fiction marvel harry potter although i m not a fan of rowling etc sorry this is such a long one and my list is probably not that useful but any guidance would be greatly appreciated because i feel a bit hopeless every time someone asks me what i want to do after uni and i just say hopefully something in film and tv but that s probably wishful thinking', 'i m am having a hard time figuring out what i want to do with my life i do very well in school th percentile and i have a chance of getting into ivy league schools so theoretically that means all my options are open but i can t seem to figure out what i want i know i probably want to work in a job that has to do with research or problem solving because generally those are my biggest strengths but i m also deeply interested in philosophy which is kind of throwing a wrench into my decision making normally i would be very interested in big life changing careers like machine learning or physics to maybe discover something about the universe but because i know that life is really meaningless it would make more sense to do something that pays well and is enjoyable rather than revolutionary i m not sure if that makes sense but i hope you get what i mean but there are main areas that i m deciding between and i just can t figure out which one i want research with this i would probably work as a physicist or a machine learning research scientist these could allow me to possibly discover something revolutionary about the universe or consciousness while also pushing humanity forward i also like the idea of getting a phd and pushing humanity s knowledge forward these jobs also hav a degree of philosophy integrated and that is arguably my biggest passion but these jobs are highly competitive take a long time to get into and i would have to live with the fact that anything i discover is ultimately meaningless and not even reliable because of epistemological questions i m not gonna get into engineering an engineering job would let me solve problems design new things and is usually pretty flexible one of the biggest reasons i m drawn to it is for whatever reason i m very attracted to physically making a change to the world like making a new road or mine i m fascinated by mining also and i don t know why but i just like the idea of getting my hands dirty and really doing something yes i know that engineers don t actually build the stuff but it s more hands on and physical compared to the other options but generally the salaries are the lowest of the and i m not really a tinkerer tech tech is still interesting to me but the main reason i d choose it is their salaries and job security tech is very lucrative and is pretty future proof i m pretty interested in cyber security and data science sounds pretty up my lane because i like research and statistics i am also learning to code in one of my classes and i think it s pretty fun so far i m just a little worried about the ageism and constant need to learn new languages because that s my least favorite part sorry for the long post idk if it makes a difference but i m an intj so maybe one of these fits that better what do y all think do you have any tips to help me decide thanks', 'hello everyone i m currently at a crossroads in my career and i ve been struggling with finding the right direction i ve been studying mechanical engineering for about seven years now but my issues aren t with grades or the difficulty of the topics while being slow i m doing well academically the problem is that i m no longer sure if this is even the right field for me to give you some background i initially chose this path because i naively believed in sustainable engineering projects such as the ocean cleanup and because i wanted to contribute to society through technology my parents also encouraged me to pursue engineering selling me on the ideas of high tech innovation silicon valley dreams and the belief that engineering drives societal progress i am from germany and at that time was not aware that most jobs would link me to the automotive or fossil industries that i already disliked at that time initially i focused on design and simulation working with cad and fea but i found these areas incredibly boring over time and didn t see them as particularly innovative i then specialized in controls only to realize that opportunities in this field were largely reserved for electrical engineers i later moved to vibration and dynamics but found the work to be driven more by academic curiosity in math than practical impactful outcomes many of my peers who pursued phds in this area ended up switching careers entirely moving into banking business consulting software development or dropping out altogether throughout my studies i was involved in various projects like a formula student team and work at an industrial research facility these experiences were often frustrating with projects failing and the work feeling monotonous even my roles as a student assistant didn t ignite much passion i continued into a master s program because it seemed like the logical next step but i still didn t have a clear vision of my future my attempts to specialize in controls didn t pan out and i even dabbled in machine learning only to find that it is mostly tech hype and that this is not an adequate qualification for a mechanical engineering masters degree over time i ve realized that much of mechanical engineering isn t as innovative or high tech as i initially thought i see more cutting edge work happening in software data analysis and sensing technologies this realization combined with my growing concern for sustainability has made me question my place in this field i feel conflicted about working in industries like automotive and aerospace which contribute to climate change and i don t believe that innovation alone can offset the environmental damage these industries cause i am really concerned about limate change and enviromental protection but i see those problems as societal problems nowadays if i were to stay in meche i would like to work for trains bicycles or renewable energy all but the last category usually have absolutely no job offerings posted it is extremely rare to get into these fields it ppers all to be about automotive aviation or chemical oil and gas industries that i dislike in fact there seem to be very few job openings for meche in general particularly in specialized areas like fea this also has to do with the decline of the german car industry and because of nationwide bans for hiring at those companies environmental engineering on the other hand seems to offer more opportunities in germany with more job security as well but i have doubts about this path too the work i ve seen seems to involve a lot of basic excel tasks and following empirical rulebooks which feels very uncreative and not particularly scientific or innovative right now i m working in vibration analysis which feels repetitive and lacks creativity it s hard to imagine doing this kind of work for the rest of my career it is basically one experiment that we do on similar products over and over again i m starting to wonder if becoming an expert in any field inevitably leads to boring monotonous work or if this is a sign that i m in the wrong field entirely given all this i ve been seriously considering changing careers i ve thought about moving into areas like wastewater engineering or river engineering which seem more aligned with my desire to do work that benefits the public flooding and water management are becoming increasingly important issues and these areas seem to offer more meaningful work i ve also considered completely different paths like becoming a teacher maybe in physical education and math or even pursuing medicine to help people directly i m really at a crossroads and would appreciate any advice or experiences you all can share has anyone else been in a similar situation how did you find your way forward what keeps you motivated in your career especially if the work feels repetitive or lacks impact thanks in advance for your help thanks for reading']

Name: 108_professor_students_class_discussion
Representative_Docs: ['my contemporary american fiction professor sent out an announcement via canvas today which literally said the words i am begging you not to use ai she went on to explain that she s had to be involved with sixteen academic dishonesty hearings in the last week because of ai use also my professor who literally works in ai research for writing and the professions has sent weekly reminders that she does not tolerate students use generative ai for any assignment one of hers was absolutely scathing that one mentioned being involved with over ten academic dishonesty hearings in the first two weeks of the semester when we hadn t even done a major paper yet she talked about how cheating is disrespectful wasteful of her time can ruin your future career and makes you look stupid because my classes are all online this semester i have several discussions on the canvas forums each week we also do a ton of peer review even in my courses that are required for english majors despite being or level courses some students works are shockingly bad they don t use proper grammar or punctuation they don t do citations properly if they re even done at all they can t form a thesis statement they don t know how to format academic writing their vocabulary is extremely limited many discussions posts essays are very obviously chatgpt some students even fail to erase the ok here s your answer or whatever the bot says at the beginning of its responses when they copy paste it into the discussion it s very discouraging i don t like or want to call anybody stupid but yes i think they re less literate than they should be at college age i m an older student i went to college for semesters shortly after i graduated high school maybe i was just too young and inexperienced with college level writing at that point and there was no ai to be fair but i strongly believe my classmates were more literate even then just over a decade ago', 'professor here who has caught several students using ai to write essays definitely go to the chair of the department or dean with this and make your case it s unlikely the professor is out to get you or anything but there are several things i feel like i need to say to this sub i don t know any professor who uses ai detectors as a default you know how much work that would be like we don t have enough to do already especially if that professor has a hundred essays at the end of the semester and limited time to submit final grades to the university it s just not feasible to run a check on everything rather here s what most likely happened professor reads essay and something looks suspicious professors read hundreds of essays every semester they get a sense for how students write given the rise of students using ai to write essays professor runs it through a checker and lo and behold it confirms a suspicion now the professor has a choice i m not condoning any professor who gives the student a zero and closes the book there should always be transparency and opportunities to contest but it seems most people here are pretty quick to condemn the professor about what really might be university policy personally i actually break this policy by not immediately reporting students i catch but i don t think it s fair to condemn professors who are abiding by university policy do you all understand how many students actually do use ai you think professors should just let this go it s our job to try to grade fairly and it s not fair that some students just decide not to do the work for all you saying that ai detectors are bullshit no they re not are they reliable of course not but they are decent enough tools when i ve had suspicions from reading an essay if i run it through different ai checks and every one of them says the essay is to ai written that s not bullshit it s not proof but it s not bullshit i give my students a zero explain to them my concerns tell them about the ai check and then give them the choice to contest it or take the zero but if they contest it it becomes official which can go on their record no student so far has attempted to contest it it s hard for me to come to any conclusion other than they know they were caught and they know under scrutiny it would be revealed so they take the zero because they can still scrape by with a passing grade all of you complaining about professors relying on ai detectors don t seem to understand how much work we do how many essays we read and how stressful it is to try to grade equitably we re not out to get you we re trying to increase accountability', 'i have been an adjunct professor for several years now i teach online courses at universities a couple of them are very well known my latest job is to teach an asynchronous course for an online program affiliated with a major university what s different for me in this role is that i had nothing to do with the creation of the course so i have very little control over how to deliver the content and no control over the content itself with a little digging i saw that about a year ago this school discontinued the use of an ai detector that could be used by teacher s to analyze a student s work the reasoning behind the discontinuation was due to the ai checker not being considered accurate so using it as a means to accuse students of cheating was problematic the course that i am teaching or basically just facilitating relies heavily on weekly discussion topics to which students first need to respond before providing feedback to their fellow students posts in many instances with these discussions i know that the grade i am providing is for chatgpt or some other ai tool many answers to the discussions are ridiculously long and overly thorough and incredibly well researched i absolutely know that it is not the work of the student that posted it the saddest part for me when grading these artificially produced answers that i am forced to give a high grade to because that are absolutely perfect is that i then have to grade an actual post from an actual human someone actually thinking about the topic and providing real world examples however if within that actual response the student failed to answer part of the question of was a day late with their response i need to award them a lesser grade than the robot responses as i try and pretend that i m still grading fairly amidst this madness i am not exaggerating when i say that some of the students used ai in the first discussion of the course which is the discussion where students are simply asked to just introduce themselves to the class and talk a little about what they know about the topic they are about to learn many of them clearly used ai even to introduce themselves and then they used ai to create responses to the other student s introductions when responding to someone simply introducing themselves instead of just writing nice to meet you you have a lovely family thank you for your service or i have a dog as well which would have been all that was needed to receive the highest grade possible for just a response to an introduction there are instead page long detailed technical posts citations included the fact is that students will be able to get away with using ai for every single assessment in this course in other words they can receive an a in this course and when the course is over the students will not even be able to articulate a basic description of what was taught in the course i was thinking today about how ridiculous this can get how s this sound as a way to complete the cycle of madness a student uses ai to create his entire response to a discussion topic the student s classmates create their feedback to the response using ai as well the professor me uses ai to create my feedback to all of the students within the discussion this way there is an entire conversation occurring between all of these artificial entities and the cutting and pasting humans involved are teaching nothing and learning nothing so the students earn and are awarded degrees that are eventually if not already worth nothing before you call me cynical or tell me i should do something about all this please know that i truly believe that the insanity i just described is probably the most guaranteed way i would have to continue being offered classes to teach at this university and i would think that the surest path to never working at the university again would be to rock the boat at all be failing a student i suspected of using ai to cheat sorry if this depressed anyone but i just needed to let it out somewhere other than within my course']

part in that first a bit of context i finished cs p and cs x a few months ago using no ai help and doing the hardest problemsets after that i made some small projects with the help of ai the exact kind of help i will go over later i ve been reading posts on the topic of how whether to use ai when learning and i haven t found a satisfactory answer the prevailing opinion seems to be do not use ai it will rob you of debugging problem solving skills and in some contexts i understand this while in other contexts it sounds a bit like i learned to drive without power steering therefore you should following lecturer gives some introduction and goes over basic examples the following problemset is designed in such a way that you use those building blocks you went over in the lectures to make something different in this scenario it is perfectly reasonable and even conducive to learning for someone to debug this without any help as you have already been introduced to the concept and the bug what the chunks you need to make are and let s say step is to make a window i assume you would need to find an external source of information on this to get you started since the knowledge you previously obtained from cs x and cs p probably isn t enough without googling i could maybe come up with some way of printing a window with ascii to the terminal maybe even implement movement by printing a new text box in the terminal when some input is pressed but going from this to a full working gui is probably not feasible without some external source of information if we take this to be true that some external source of information is needed is there utility to googling sifting through articles or watching youtube tutorials copying their code and then molding it to your usecase instead of having chatgpt get you started with the same example code that you then mold to your use case in the same way is there utility to searching for and reading often verbose documentation instead of having chat gpt tell you in plain english this function takes x and y as input and gives you back z i know people often say its your job to read documentation but does it have to be nevermind the fact that i don t work and have no aspirations to work in it i mean more in the sense if this is the way something has been done until now why do we have to keep doing it in the same way my main question here is is there utility to avoiding ai use in these specific contexts is this a matter of sticking to tradition or actually obtaining better learning outcomes obviously asking ai to outright solve your problems will hamper your learning and problem solving skills but using it to offload tedious tasks like sifting through articles or deciphering documentation seems harmless to me i would be happy to hear other opinions thanks in advance']

Name: 118_students_use ai_ai_professors
Representative_Docs: ['i teach an in person undergrad intro level bio lab and i ve fortunately not had issues with ai usage among students in it but last semester when i did the grading for an online principles of bio for non majors course ai usage among students was a huge issue a lot of students considered it a joke class and would put extremely little effort into the assignments but i still had to grade their assignments so i read all of their answers for a lot of the short answer questions i d notice that a handful of students would include terminology not used in the question or their reading and was kind of odd terminology to use like wherever they got it was almost correct but got a crucial detail mixed up with some other biology concept it said in the syllabus that for this course ai usage especially not disclosed is considered the same as plagiarism and thus is academic misconduct so it earns a on the assignment and some students still had the nerve to be surprised at their grade i had a few repeat offenders who maybe didn t think we were serious and did have to appear before the student conduct board', 'you re overthinking it because there is a slight chance your professor may have suspicions about ai usage and you also just realized how much ai optimizes intentionally over assigned anxiety inducing grad school work you re getting downvoted because the type to go to grad school consists of a bunch of fellow anxious nerds that like to overemphasize academic honesty and perfectionism as it s intuitively the only means of gatekeeping our dwindling authority on specialized knowledge has left in the current disinformation age augmented by ai that being said using ai is academically dishonest when not previously discussed with your professors however if you have already crossed the threshold why not go all the way and ensure your ai assisted works don t show signs of ai generated content if you reread your work and see no obvious signs of ai i e copy paste prompts chat conversations repeat unusual phrases generally sloppy transcription etc then you re probably good unless your professor is especially suspicious of you due to previous issues you re not getting caught even with turnitin since it s not accurate and can only be used as supporting evidence leading up to and following an admission of guilt that means from now on don t mention ai at all in conversations with your professors or cohort even if accused and you too can peel back the veil of cheesing grad school in students use ai instructors use ai industry professionals use ai no one gives a fuck anywhere but academia because the type to get caught using ai were usually so inept at hiding it they deserve to be admonished', 'it depends on that course s ai policy in the syllabus but for the courses i ve ta ed using ai is considered academic dishonesty and is to be treated like plagiarism i ll also say that these are not coding classes i ve ta ed an introductory biology lab for bio majors an introductory bio lecture for non majors an animal behavior course and this upcoming semester will be ta ing biology of sleep i have no experience dealing with coding and how to handle ai usage by students with that i ve definitely encountered ai usage especially in the intro bio for non majors course i caught it due to differences in their homework answers and written answers on classwork or exams that absolutely seemed like they were written by different people with completely different vocabularies and working understandings of biology ai answers also used terminology not covered in any class materials that were often not quite right thankfully since i m not the instructor of record it s not really my job to prove they used ai my job is to flag it and pass it on to the professor which i gladly do imo the intro biology for non majors prof was wayyyyy too lenient on ai usage even though the syllabus said it was to be treated like plagiarism nothing happened when students used ai in that course not even deductions in grades students in animal behavior are juniors and seniors and i ve yet to see any of them use ai i do appreciate that i ll sometimes get some really poorly written work in that course but i know it was written by the student i appreciate a student who turns in their work so much however i ve seen one student fail due to ai usage in the intro lab for bio majors he used ai on multiple homework assignments even keeping the text of his prompts lol and continued to do so after multiple warnings i sent each assignment where ai was used to the professor and this student got an f in the course very long answer with specific examples to say unfortunately students will use ai for homework or any online assignments if you re not the instructor of record there s a professor pass assignments where ai was used on to them to decide if this merits some sort of penalty grade reduction whatever written in class assignments are a good way to stop ai usage and have an assignment where ai wasn t used to compare to other assignments but i don t know how realistic that is for your course']

Name: 119_students_assignments_grade_responses
Representative_Docs: ['my ta positions this term requires that i grade quiz short answer responses paper outlines final papers etc between two person classes i was grading short answer responses to a non proctored canvas quiz today and so many of the responses were structured the exact same way with the exact same wording and i just feel like i am reading clearly ai generated responses it s not a hill i will die on but it is frustrating miraculously one of the submissions caught me off guard this person was being overly silly and wrote quite humourously but they actually met almost all criteria for the grading rubric while also making me laugh out loud it felt nice to read something a little unhinged but very obviously human anyways i think i m losing my mind this term grading ai slop presumably good luck to all my other ta s out there', 'i m working on a project to build an ai powered auto grading system for math assignments and exams and i d love to get some input the goal is to help save time on grading while still giving students meaningful feedback it s not about replacing human grading tas or professors would still validate the grades but we re hoping it can make the process a bit smoother this idea came from a professor who told us how grading so many exams and homeworks was overwhelming to manage they came up with a policy they d randomly pick a few questions to grade for accuracy and the rest were just marked for completion it totally makes sense given the workload but the downside is that students don t get full feedback on how they did this can leave them in the dark about what they need to work on especially before exams that s where we re hoping this system could help it would grade all questions provide detailed feedback and save a lot of time but here s the thing we don t know how academic criteria for grading are usually defined are rubrics common for most assignments and exams or is it more about professional judgment as you go we want to make this tool actually useful for professors and tas not something that feels like extra work or a bad replacement for human grading if you have any advice on how you approach grading or how we could make this better i d love to hear it thanks so much for your time and help', 'really unfortunate but a feeling i can empathize with as an arts student in a non traditional english program something i m finding is that courses are now being geared adapted to teach things or ask for things that ai doesn t provide a lot of value towards in terms of helping a student to cheat missing the mark becoming a soulless and uninterested student lol like we critique ai and the impact of ai ecosystems and frequently have seminars now where our contributions are what gets graded you could try to cheat and regurgitate some insight you asked ai for but if it s too basic you re not likely to receive a good mark if it s too dense and as a student you re not even sure what it s saying not likely to receive a good mark and of course if it turns out what you re regurgitating is wrong then one of the other or kids will call you out on it aside learning to critique discourse even that of one another is a skill that there has been a shifted focus toward imo all in all i unfortunately don t have an answer as to what to change at the ta level but i feel like the general dissection of how ai is actually detracting from learning examining things like is it just that students don t have time for ridiculously long aimless essay assignments or is it that they think ai can teach them fundamental concepts things that we used to use kinesthetic and hands on learning to understand will lead to more engaging and useful teaching methods methods of evaluation curriculums etc i don t actually know how common this bit is but our college in my courses don t outright ban the use of ai but they encourage you to very critically consider what you re using what its impact is and why and to simply cite where needed if it was something like a learning tool to support some part of producing a super basic report anecdotally i think ai is commonly used to get away with tedious writing and drills and i don t think those things were ever really engaging students and making them fall in love with their discipline it took so much outside of the box teaching and interactive learning for me to enjoy what i m doing now']

Name: 120_plagiarism_plagiarism ai_generated_ai generated
Representative_Docs: ['ai generated writing isn t the same as plagiarism ai generated writing is original so will pass plagiarism checks just fine ai detection is looking for different things and has nothing to do with source citing etc', 'i once used my checker plagiarismcheck org to check my assignment for ai it was partially ai generated so i was there to check the of ai since it is a plagiarism checker and ai detector combined somehow it also flagged for plagiarism even showing the source oops', 'ai checkers have to be used by profs the same way plagiarism checkers are used plagiarism detection tools do not find plagiarism they find matching text the prof has to interpret the result of the text matching for example if two full sentences match an article somewhere on the internet but they have quote marks and a citation then it s not plagiarism even though there is an exact match ai detection is similar if the ai detector concludes that some paragraph or even a whole paper was ai generated the prof has to interpret this report so they have to read the paper in light of that report but they can just go by the report to say it was cheating there are obvious problems with ai generated papers and they are easy to see once someone is looking for them']

outputs the exact way that a human would pronounce these in context that s not the only thing i need from a text normalizer i also have a ton of abbreviations in the text that i need the full words for for example fmr lt gen army inf st div i need this normalized to former lieutenant general army infantry first division how do i get to that from the original text with all those abbreviations i also don t know if python s num words knows how to convert ordinal numbers it might not so it might not even know that st needs to be changed to first how do i make an algorithm that knows that another thing is acronyms if an acronym is pronounced as the individual letters i want it to be normalized as initials so something like ncaa would be normalized as either n c a a or n c double a since both of these pronunciations are acceptable however something like fifa or nato i want left intact since these are pronounceable acronyms and a normal human would read these and would say fifa or nato not f i f a or n a t o so how do i make my normalizer correctly initialize ncaa to n c a a but will leave something like fifa alone as it is with no initialization does anyone think they can help here how do i make a proper text normalizer and how do i use machine learning to do it also how long would something like this take like could it be done in less than a week oh and i m going to need the exact same thing in italian portuguese spanish french german and dutch as well how do i do it can i do it in a week for all languages', 'hi everyone tldr how to use ml to automatically assign best possible weights so my algorithm knows how much to value each feature to make a decision based on training data my project i am creating a product title shortening algorithm that does something like the following input mm jack wireless bluetooth compatible receiver adapter wireless aux receiver adapter for headphone pc music mp output chars max mm wireless bluetooth adapter for headphone except that the titles i am working with are in portuguese the program does this through a simple nlp algorithm that is based on grammatical segmentation of the title meaning it essentially splits the title into noun chunks and then if appropriate intelligently merges these chunks together to produce new chunks after the title has been segmented i then run it through a prioritization algorithm that is incomplete and as of now simply takes segment length and position into account to output the final shortened title meaning longer segments that come first take priority it might be relevant to emphasize that for of the cases the non ml algorithm that i have at the moment already picks the best choice but it doesn t use any of the many potentially relevant features i describe below and seems to me to still have a lot of room to improve my problem i am extending this prioritization algorithm by adding more features to consider such as number of nouns adjectives connecting words known words unknown words word frequency in large corpus of text such as wikipedia word frequency in all of the titles word frequency in the product descriptions etc i also extracted every possible adjacent word combination to form millions of n grams then kept only those that repeated or more times to form a list of thousands of terms that range from words in length that should in theory be more descriptive Representative_Docs: ['i ve been out of school for almost years now working for a little over year i have an undergrad in math and physics and i consider myself an overall intelligent person i am paid okay at my job but it s a role that doesn t relate to my studies often the case with many people but i am trying to find a role that suits me better with some success lately my grades at school were not good enough to get into grad school i applied for one grad school in mathematics under a special program for those who may not have a great gpa but would be taken in and have to do better in their first year to make up for it that sorta thing i was never accepted and didn t want to waste any money applying further to other schools as my gpa was quite a bit less than most grad schools cutoffs i would someday like to go back to get my master s and study concepts in data mining machine learning ai data science etc although i particularly enjoy branches of math within linear and abstract algebra anyways i feel my dream is getting further and further out of reach firstly if i wanted to study a branch of pure math there are very few if any jobs that provide hands on experience with those topics so while people can go back and get an mba or master s in psychology with industry experience math is a different story data science might be a bit easier since i could potentially find some work in that field and work my way up references will become a problem i am seeking jobs where i would work closely with or under a data scientist master s or phd level but grad schools prefer and usually require mostly professors as references i had profs who provided me with references when i applied to grad school before but for them to do that years later would be ridiculous money although this is more me simply whining but i make a decent wage and it d be difficult to give that up for years i feel that sure if i made it out of grad school i d have something very worthwhile to put on my cv but i d also be missing out on years of solid industry experience and money and it s not like there are any part time or online programs in a pure science like math again my gpa will always haunt me i was very ill during my studies and had to miss a lot of class and couldn t spend much time on actual studying and assignments i have letters from doctors and specialists to substantiate these claims but schools haven t been very open to hearing me out my gpa will follow me forever and i feel there will never be a way to make up for that no matter how smart i may actually be does anyone have any success stories that started out like how i did and guidance that anyone can provide i m currently doing things like self learning machine learning concepts brushing up on my python skills and working on a publication in a peer reviewed journal all while trying to get real world industry experience but i feel that even those will not be enough to make up for a low gpa and having references that aren t professors i know i can get to know the profs at schools so i can have someone to potentially vouch for me but how would one go about doing that and would it really make any difference cliffs math degree with decent paying job wanna go back to get master s and phd someday low gpa holding me back trying to make up for it with industry experience a publication and getting to know the right people it s it realistic or just a pipe dream', 'first apologies if this isn t the right sub to ask this if you know one that s more tailored to this kind of advice feel free to let me know so i went to a top university and majored in econ with minors in math and finance however i didn t really try as hard as i could ve in undergrad and it s now coming back to bite me in the ass my cumulative gpa is around a my last two years gpa is around a and i have no internship or job experience in the fields of study that i majored minored in the only job experience i have is tutoring for a math company in which i have moderate leadership experience and communication skills that i can leverage i m at a standstill in my desires where part of me wants to go into data science since it interests me more than a job in economics finance but i have the degrees in economics finance which could help me with finding a job more as well as getting into a mba versus a data science program where all i have is a computer science course that i took in college if i do go down the path of applying to a data science grad program i run the big risk of simply not getting accepted since a lot of the schools are looking for people with moderate to extensive background in computer programming data structures and algorithms and the whole reason why i decided to look into grad school despite being vehemently against it while i was an undergrad is because i m struggling to find a job right now which all require experience that i do not currently have of course i do still plan on applying to jobs throughout the entire process but this is something i ve been doing for months now with very little luck even with moving into interviews and second rounds let alone any offers of employment if i do happen to receive an offer before starting grad school assuming i can find one to accept me i ll reconsider continuing with school but for now it s become brutally clear that i simply need leverage to stand out from the playing field and grad school might be the only way for me to do that and to nullify my undergraduate grades tl dr i m in a negative feedback loop of not having internship experience in college not being able to find a job and having no strengths i can leverage when applying to two different grad programs one that is more suited given my undergraduate degrees and one that more closely aligns with what i actually want to do', 'firstly for those reading thanks in advance for taking the time to do so i have a fairly unusual educational path that sort of makes me hesitant to apply for phd programs in the future i will need some help to break that down into actionable goals that i can accomplish to paint the timeline of events here s my situation a little more than ten years ago i entered a small state liberal arts college unknown and unranked with the goal of pursuing a career in pure mathematics research there i took an introductory computer science class and scored poorly on it due to lack of interest back then this information is important later on i proceeded to take a lot of advanced math courses studied abroad published a math paper in an academic journal no impact factor received a speaker award at a national math conference and attended an reu i completed my studies with a b a in mathematics with an overall gpa of and a major gpa of the research experiences i had gained during my undergraduate years were very rewarding and fun immediately after undergraduate i decided to continue my academic path by attending graduate school at a lower ranked public r tier university because my initial research interests didn t feel applicable to the pressing problems that mattered today and that i didn t have a good understanding of the problems i wanted to solve i decided to terminate my ph d to acquire more work experience in the industry i pivoted towards that by taking a mix of pure and applied math classes in graduate school completing my masters thesis replicated my advisor s work in partial differential equations and concluding my ph d studies with a terminal m s in mathematics with an overall gpa of after graduating with my masters i spent a year teaching mathematics to middle and high school students i also self taught myself programming fundamentals in python by taking courses online and those combined efforts eventually secured me my first data science position at a renowned financial services firm i continued to build onto my data science career by building dashboards and scheduling etl pipelines eventually transitioning to my current employer doing similar tasks i have been fairly successful in building a professional network during this time to expose myself more mathematical opportunities in my career i decided to enroll in a part time online m s program which combines computer science statistics and business fundamentals my current gpa is a but i am targeting a after completion of the degree there i took a wide variety of coursework and eventually developed a strong interest in reinforcement learning after completing the relevant coursework i took a gap semester to reproduce code in academic papers relevant to rl literature and continued to build out my portfolio showcasing applications of reinforcement learning using state of the art tools like openai gym using puzzles that inspired me as a kid like sudoku chess rubik s cube etc i also had the rare opportunity to apply some reinforcement learning in my career to build out a data engineering platform for rl training using docker and jenkins i am in the intersection of my career where i am struggling to define the best pathway to reaching my goals of pursuing data science and machine learning research the minimum requirement for most industry positions in research require a ph d at minimum after all of these times trying to best identify what i want to pursue in research i wonder if it would be worthwhile for me to apply for ph d programs in computer science or mathematics based on my regrettably confusing background here are my questions would my scattered past be so detrimental to my application that it wouldn t be worth applying to ph d programs at all would it be better if i just focus on data engineering and pursue reinforcement learning research independently on the side should i just completely ignore research if i decide to pursue the ph d in computer science would my past grades in undergraduate require me to apply for post baccalaureate degrees in cs to make sure that i have the necessary pre requisites or would previous work experience side projects and my story play a significant role in accessing my application if i decide to pursue the ph d in applied mathematics are there any research groups that study reinforcement learning and game theory under the math department from my understanding most rl research is aligned under computer science what would be the best way to combine my interests in data science and research in my career given this situation is it grad school is it continued self study is it impossible for me to even consider it at this point i have a few options if i decide to pursue the ph d pathway i don t have plans to apply for ph d programs any time soon because i know that i need to acquire more relevant research experience in rl via visiting student appointments with research labs get a solid set of recommenders to attest my value in research continue studying reinforcement learning and game theory on my own time and building out my portfolio to reflect my understanding and find more work opportunities to continue to hone that admittedly i ve made a lot of bad decisions in my past and i am disappointed with how things are panning out i hope there is a reasonable pathway that i can consider to reach to my overall career goals thanks for your time and patience with me and i look forward to hearing this subreddit s response']

Representative_Docs: ['tool description notebooklm is an ai powered research and note taking tool developed by google designed to assist users in summarizing and organizing information effectively notebooklm leverages gemini to provide quick insights and streamline content workflows for various purposes including the creation of podcasts and mind maps macro is an ai powered workspace that allows users to chat collaborate and edit pdfs documents notes code and diagrams in one place the platform offers built in editors ai chat with access to the top llms claude openai instant contextual understanding via highlighting and secure document management arxival is a search engine for machine learning papers the platform serves as a research paper answering engine focused on openly accessible ml papers providing ai generated responses with citations and figures perplexity ai is an advanced ai driven platform designed to provide accurate and relevant search results through natural language queries perplexity combines machine learning and natural language processing to deliver real time reliable information with citations elicit is an ai enabled tool designed to automate time consuming research tasks such as summarizing papers extracting data and synthesizing findings the platform significantly reduces the time required for systematic reviews enabling researchers to analyze more evidence accurately and efficiently storm is a research project from stanford university developed by the stanford oval lab the tool is an ai powered tool designed to generate comprehensive wikipedia like articles on any topic by researching and structuring information retrieved from the internet its purpose is to provide detailed and grounded reports for academic and research purposes paperpal offers a suite of ai powered tools designed to improve academic writing the research and grammar tool provides features such as real time grammar and language checks plagiarism detection contextual writing suggestions and citation management helping researchers and students produce high quality manuscripts efficiently scispace is an ai powered platform that helps users find understand and learn research papers quickly and efficiently the tool provides simple explanations and instant answers for every paper read recall is a tool that transforms scattered content into a self organizing knowledge base that grows smarter the more you use it the features include instant summaries interactive chat augmented browsing and secure storage making information management efficient and effective semantic scholar scholar is a free ai powered research tool for scientific literature it helps scholars to efficiently navigate through vast amounts of academic papers enhancing accessibility and providing contextual insights consensus is an ai powered search engine designed to help users find and understand scientific research papers quickly and efficiently the tool offers features such as pro analysis and consensus meter which provide insights and summaries to streamline the research process humata is an advanced artificial intelligence tool that specializes in document analysis particularly for pdfs the tool allows users to efficiently explore summarize and extract insights from complex documents offering features like citation highlights and natural language processing for enhanced usability ai scholar qa scholarqa is an innovative application designed to assist researchers in conducting literature reviews by providing comprehensive answers derived from scientific literature it leverages advanced ai techniques to synthesize information from over eight million open access papers thereby facilitating efficient and accurate academic research', 'i have a samsung galaxy tab s lite it came with a stylus so i didn t have to buy that separately it also was on sale for like on amazon when i bought it and it has gb of ram and gb of storage i use it primarily for reading papers and taking notes here is a basic workflow i m still working on parts of it install zotero on your computer this is my reference manager to be honest i don t get how people function without them i have the firefox extension and can save references and pdfs immediately from my browser the references are put into folders that i create based on topic and arranged with the author publish year and journal visible and it s really easy for me to find what i m looking for search is a little wonky for me but eh use a cloud based platform onedrive google drive dropbox that you can access through your tablet as a vehicle for your papers explained in next step i keep everything on onedrive since i have basically unlimited storage from my school and all my documents get autosaved install zotfile to zotero this allows you move files from your laptop computer to your tablet for reading on your tablet you want to make sure that you send the paper to a file you can access on your tablet hence step so you can download the appropriate app and your files should show up once they sync make the annotations on your tablet as needed and save the annotated file with the same original file name in the same folder zotfile sent it to go back to zotero and retrieve the annotated pdf zotfile does this thing where it knows if a pdf was sent to the tablet and there is an option after you send it to get it zotfile will look for the same file so make sure the filename is unchanged and in the same location it sent it to and will grab the file with the annotations you can also extract these annotations if you want and zotero will keep them attached to your reference with the page number the annotation was made on i think written notes aren t retrieved but highlights and textboxes are edit btw i just checked and you can export these annotations to markdown html or zotero rtf but no pdf the html looks good enough and the export contains links back to your zotero references there may be something online that allows you to do this export to pdf but i m not sure still working on this summarize and synthesize your notes annotations in a third party software like onenote notion etc i m playing around with notero which is an extension for zotero that allows you to pull your notes and paper into notion and organizes it you can look into this prior to this i was using mem ai to synthesize my notes and i would tag my notes and see connections between papers with the same tag appear and that helped me after years of grad school and regular school i discovered that i suck at keeping my notes organized and i couldn t trust myself to keep paper notes even if i had a notebook i d sometimes rip a page of notes out to summarize and type out yeah i m dumb and then i d need to see the original note but it would be lost so i opted for this so far it s working for me but to each their own for class notes i use my tablets note app i like it the best and then upload a pdf of it to onenote i highly recommend onenote for classes and staying organized i don t like it for research though because i live having a way to connect pages e g tags', 'in the realm of academic research effective management of references and citations is crucial over the years tools like mendeley and zotero have served as the backbone for countless researchers aiding in the organization and accessibility of scholarly materials however as the landscape of research evolves so too do the needs of its practitioners this brings us to a comparative discussion of three notable tools in this field zotero mendeley and the newcomer afforai mendeley a veteran tool with robust features mendeley launched in has been a stalwart in the research community boasting over million users globally it offers a comprehensive suite of features including reference management citation generation and a platform for researchers to collaborate its strength lies in its detailed customization options and the ability to handle complex research needs through various plugins however some users might find the learning curve a bit steep which could be a deterrent for those who need a more straightforward user friendly interface zotero widely trusted for its simplicity and efficiency zotero introduced in commands a loyal user base of approximately million it is particularly favored for its ease of use and effective management capabilities including generating citations and bibliographies seamlessly while zotero is highly efficient the concern arises with its pace of innovation as research methodologies and technologies advance there is a growing need for tools that can adapt and incorporate newer functionalities to support modern research workflows afforai the modern entrant with ai integration saw the launch of afforai an ai powered reference manager tool designed to meet the contemporary demands of the research community afforai is a zotero alternative afforai differentiates itself by integrating artificial intelligence to enhance the research process this includes features like summarizing and comparing papers auto populating citations and providing a more interactive file viewer with annotation capabilities its user interface is designed to be intuitive reducing the learning curve and making it accessible even for those new to research management discussion points ease of use afforai promises a simpler more intuitive interface compared to the more traditional layouts of zotero and mendeley how important is user interface and ease of learning in your choice of reference management tools feature set while all three tools provide robust functionalities the integration of ai in afforai offers a unique proposition do you think ai tools enhance the research process or do they complicate the traditional research methodologies reliability and trust zotero and mendeley have been around for over a decade and have established trust within the research community as a new entrant afforai needs to build this trust what factors would convince you to switch to a newer tool like afforai from more established ones like zotero or mendeley conclusion choosing the right tool often depends on the specific needs and preferences of the researcher whether it s the tried and tested reliability of zotero and mendeley or the innovative ai driven approach of afforai each tool offers unique benefits why you might want to use zotero if you are an experienced researcher or looking for a reference manager that is trusted by numerous experienced researchers regardless of the more classified design and potential of a steep learning curve then zotero is for you why you might want to use mendeley if you are an experienced researcher with time to learn how to use the different aspects and plugins mendeley offers then it is undoubtably a fantastic tool that is reliably used by over million researchers globally why you might want to use afforai if you are new to researching or are looking for an easy to learn modernized reference management tool that still boasts the same powerful features as zotero as well as seamless integration of ai to reliably summarize search and compare your uploaded papers then afforai is for you']

Name: 134_course_python_machine learning_learning
Representative_Docs: ['as someone who is taking a machine learning class in a university this is my feedback ml is tough i d say if your really good at math and have an intermediate understanding of python then you should try machine learning ml is basically a bunch of math formulas like regular computer science but you have to really understand them in order to know when to apply which ml algorithm if you re a mathematician you ll feel more comfortable when you start seeing summation formulas derivatives statistics and sigmoid functions the reason you want to learn python is because machine learning is usually done through it python numpy handles handles everything as arrary matrixes and you ll have to deal with large data sets using python in order to use the ml algorithms to their full potential all of that being said i would never recommend someone who is just learning how to start programming to start at machine learning it s a difficult subject to grasp and can turn you away from programming if you re new to codding simple things like why two arrays of different sizes not broadcasting together in python can really take up time to understand and that s not even ml', 'udacity is a place to start coursera ai course by andrew ng is like the first step to start the journey there are lot of aspects of ai machine learning and deep learning start with the theory then go into machine learning that s where most of the data analysis is done and then follow it up with deep learning for that you would need to know python as a programming skill to learn python you can do the mit edx course or udacity either way you will be familiar with it i started my journey last year feels good to say it since it s happy new year haha i can say i know some stuff i am trying to practice as much as possible from sites like github kaggle for machine learning project euler for python if you need any help i would be more than happy to', 'i tried my luck at r learnmachinelearning with no luck i hope this is an appropriate sub to post this if not please kindly redirect me thank you i currently work in r amp amp d at my current employment and my team and i are getting on board with ml applications at the moment we are all very experienced programmers python c but we are very new to ml concepts we quickly realized that ml is a very broad topic so we bought a few books machine learning with tensorflow and deep learning with python we are also currently doing some coursera free classes that revolve around ml one of my favorite books so far is machine learning with tensorflow it gives you the and ft field of view for the different approaches to using ml to solve a specific problem so to make a long story short my team and i feel that we have a very basic grasp of some of the concepts used to solve problems regression classification clustering and markov models we would like to deviate away from the sample datasets and begin working on small projects here and there that ml can be utilized one such project is the reason i am here i am kindly requesting the opinion and advice of potentially more experienced folks here on what subtopic of supervised or unsupervised would be more appropriate in a nut shell we have certain mechanical tools that require heat calibration files to operate properly so far they are done manually and it is a very tedious work to make matter simple we accomplish this by essentially setting our tools in a massive oven and start recording on certain sensors at different temperatures at the end we use an overall average at each recorded temperature to be put into our heat calibration file it looks something like this tmp value and we continue for discrete temp values our goal is trying to find the most optimal value at a certain temperature within a range of f to f our current method gives us a measly points of final data and a linear regression analysis is constructed to interpolate the missing values we feel this can be optimally redone using ml we were thinking if we should use regression to model this trend we have millions of datapoints or would this be more better solved of we push it through a clustering unsupervised model i would love thoughts and suggestions thank you']
