Representative_Docs: ['i need some advice on how to transition from public relations and communications into the world of machine learning or at the very least getting to a data analyst my background is mostly agency side i ve worked at places like gci health and golin handling communications campaigns for companies like bristol myers squibb and nintendo of america most of my work involved media relations stakeholder management executive visibility and a lot of writing and coordination across teams i ve always been good at understanding complex topics and turning them into clear engaging stories but outside of work i ve had a long standing interest in ai especially large language models and generative tools i was playing around with stable diffusion back when it was still fairly niche and i even experimented with training lora models to tweak and personalize image outputs i ve also been exploring prompt engineering and ai tools to speed up creative workflows especially in video and content production right now i know i need a serious career change pr agency life has been exhausting and i want to move into something more technical ideally where i can mix my communication skills with a deeper understanding of ai and machine learning here s where i need help i m a us citizen currently living in warsaw poland and i m open to going back to university joining a bootcamp or diving deep into something like coursera or edx i just don t know what the most efficient path is i want something that will actually prepare me for a new role without wasting time or money some specific things i m wondering is it realistic to pivot into ml or ai from a non technical background or should i focus on adjacent roles like technical writing developer relations or ai product communications would a formal degree in data science or cs be worth it or are bootcamps and self study more effective for career changers what kinds of roles would let me grow technical skills while still using my background in strategy writing and storytelling i m open to any honest advice suggested paths or even stories from people who have done something similar i know this won t be easy but i m ready to put in the work just trying to make smart decisions about where to start i appreciate any feedback', 'the field of mass communication is evolving rapidly driven by new media platforms changing audience expectations and technological advances in it demands fresh thinking creativity and adaptability beyond newsrooms and advertising agencies exciting opportunities now exist across multiple sectors offering rewarding careers to those who combine creativity with technical skills in demand roles content strategist designs and manages content plans across websites blogs social media video and podcasts requires strong writing trend awareness and multimedia skills social media manager crafts social calendars manages online communities handles crisis communication and tracks engagement needs expertise in platform specific strategies and content tools digital content creator produces engaging blogs podcasts and social posts with an understanding of audience psychology podcast producer plans edits and manages podcasts working with hosts and guests to create engaging episodes digital marketing specialist covers seo email campaigns and influencer marketing using analytics and algorithms to boost reach corporate communications expert builds internal and external communication strategies fostering trust and engagement multimedia analyst interprets communication data to measure campaign success and guide creative direction journalist researches writes and adapts stories for print broadcast or digital platforms with strong reporting skills video producer creates visual content for tv film and streaming platforms combining creativity with technical expertise public relations planner shapes public perception builds relationships and manages brand messaging traditional roles in advertising scripting photography anchoring and customer service remain relevant but today s professionals must adapt to emerging technologies like ai ar and vr which are transforming content creation and audience engagement essential skills for success technical skills proficiency in editing software social media tools and analytics platforms analytical skills ability to interpret data spot trends and plan effective campaigns writing communication strong storytelling and clear messaging for diverse audiences creativity innovation thinking beyond the conventional to design impactful content soft skills leadership teamwork time management and conflict resolution for high pressure environments preparing for a career in mass communication degree in mass communication builds a strong knowledge base and professional foundation practical experience internships volunteering and industry mentorship enhance skills portfolio showcases creative work and technical abilities to employers industry awareness keeping up with trends via workshops courses and professional networking networking building relationships across media sectors opens career opportunities the future outlook in mass communication is a vibrant field where creativity meets technology professionals have the power to shape perceptions influence change and connect audiences worldwide the key to thriving lies in versatility adaptability and a commitment to continuous learning for those ready to embrace the challenges and innovations of the digital era mass communication offers limitless possibilities whether you re crafting stories managing brands analysing data or producing immersive multimedia experiences this is not just a career path it s a platform to impact society through the power of communication', 'the media and communications landscape has changed more in the past five years than in the previous few decades combined the lines between advertising and public relations pr have blurred consumers expect authenticity at every touchpoint and artificial intelligence ai is reshaping how stories are created delivered and consumed in organizations can no longer rely on traditional press releases or expensive tv spots alone instead they must adopt an integrated approach where advertising strategies amplify pr efforts and pr strengthens the credibility of advertising campaigns those who master this synergy gain not just visibility but also long term trust and loyalty from their audiences this playbook explores the most relevant advertising and pr strategies for supported by current industry insights data and real world examples the blending of advertising pr traditionally advertising and pr were considered two distinct disciplines advertising was paid controlled messaging while pr focused on earned coverage and reputation management today however these boundaries are increasingly fluid pr and marketing convergence is one of the clearest signs of this shift over of pr professionals now consider marketing skills like seo content creation and analytics essential to their roles pr is no longer just about media relations it s about driving measurable business outcomes unified campaigns also show how the disciplines complement each other for instance nike integrates high quality video ads with socially conscious messaging that pr teams amplify in the media ads build awareness while pr coverage sparks conversations turning campaigns into cultural movements ai driven advertising pr ai is perhaps the most disruptive force shaping communication strategies today its impact is visible at multiple levels generative ai is now widely used for ad creation the interactive advertising bureau iab reports that of advertisers are using ai to design video ads in and nearly of all video ads will be ai generated by this levels the playing field allowing smaller businesses to compete with big brands ai also enables predictive outreach analyzing social media trends to forecast viral moments so pr teams can craft timely pitches it personalizes messages at scale creating culturally sensitive multilingual persona driven ads yet unmoderated use of ai has pitfalls automated press pitches if irrelevant can flood journalists inboxes and damage credibility the key is to combine ai efficiency with human oversight programmatic contextual advertising automation is transforming ad placement and targeting by of global digital display advertising spend was executed through programmatic platforms and this figure is projected to rise to by programmatic systems use algorithms to buy and place ads in real time ensuring relevance and efficiency meanwhile contextual advertising is becoming increasingly important as privacy laws tighten and third party cookies disappear instead of tracking individuals contextual systems evaluate the environment of a webpage and place ads that feel natural for example an eco friendly brand advertising alongside sustainability articles feels both authentic and relevant trust authenticity storytelling in the most powerful currency in communications is trust transparency has become non negotiable a edelman trust barometer survey revealed that of consumers buy or boycott brands based on perceived honesty authentic storytelling is equally vital people connect with stories not slogans brands like dove and patagonia have built strong equity by consistently telling narratives aligned with their values cultural relevance is also essential campaigns must resonate with current conversations or risk appearing tone deaf for example during the women s world cup brands promoting gender equality gained significant attention finally influencer dynamics have shifted nano and micro influencers with their smaller but loyal followings achieve higher engagement than celebrity influencers their voices are seen as genuine and relatable emerging trends shaping pr the pr industry is undergoing major changes in ai enabled workflows are streamlining tasks such as monitoring reporting and sentiment analysis social listening and collaboration with micro influencers are helping brands engage more directly with niche communities radical transparency has emerged as a necessity whether handling crises or disclosing partnerships audiences expect clear and honest communication localized storytelling is also on the rise with global brands tailoring their narratives to regional audiences without losing their core message metrics have evolved too instead of measuring pr success solely by media coverage brands now focus on reputation trust and audience behavior guided by frameworks like the barcelona principles advertising trends to watch advertising in continues to evolve alongside pr generative ai video ads dominate short form platforms like tiktok instagram reels and youtube shorts helping brands adapt content for diverse markets ai powered media platforms such as teads are democratizing access to sophisticated data rich ad solutions consumers now expect seamless omnichannel experiences whether interacting with a brand online in store or through social media the messaging must feel consistent this integrated approach creates stronger brand loyalty best practices for integrated strategies to succeed in today s landscape brands must focus on clarity authenticity and adaptability clear objectives help align pr and advertising efforts while authentic stories ensure credibility influencers should be chosen strategically with a balance between micro influencers for trust and larger creators for reach ai should be used wisely automating repetitive tasks without replacing human judgment programmatic and contextual ads must respect consumer privacy while maximizing impact measurement should go beyond clicks and impressions focusing on trust reputation and tangible outcomes finally agility is essential campaigns should adapt to cultural shifts and audience feedback in real time conclusion advertising and pr are no longer separate silos they are complementary forces driving brand storytelling in the integration of ai technology programmatic systems authentic storytelling and influencer collaboration has reshaped the industry yet the timeless principle remains people trust stories more than sales pitches the most successful brands will be those that balance data with empathy technology with creativity and efficiency with authenticity in this new era winning attention is important but winning trust is everything']

Name: 137_game_ai_hello world_games
Representative_Docs: ['i just published my first game coded entirely in vanilla javascript and i couldn t have done it without using ai as my coding mentor i wanted to build something real instead of just following tutorials i hit a wall at first googling led to fragmented answers and ai responses were often too complex or overwhelming then i stumbled on a trick that changed everything i d ask the ai for help e g how do i detect collisions in javascript instead of copy pasting the code i d rephrase the answer in my own words and ask did i understand this correctly the ai would correct my mistakes and explain gaps in a way that actually stuck this feedback loop helped me learn way faster in a few weeks i went from basics to a working game with collision detection score tracking and custom logic no frameworks just raw js why i m sharing this for beginners proof that you don t need to know everything to start building ai can bridge gaps if you engage with it actively not just copy code', 'i was pondering my next project in coding and one thing i ve always been interested in is ai and a personal favorite hobby of mine is lowering the god complex of complicated things so anyone can do it this opens the field of study to perspectives that might never otherwise have been considered for example within ai is a fear that you wouldn t intentionally do something malicious but still end up with a malicious outcome perspective is extremely important towards solving that issue what one person see s as a perfect solution another might realize there s a big problem ahead to me this is the beauty of open source it opens up the code to many different individuals with experiences that exceed one persons capacity in my professional experience this is a common problem in programming the more time you spend doing complicated things the lower your capacity is to explain it in simplistic terms and the more likely the codes creator is to make mistakes outside of their own perspective or introduce bias so here is my challenge lets say i m fairly new and i ve just completed hello world great i m feeling confident purposefully leaving the language as an irrelevant factor here feel free to use whatever language you would like what is the shortest simplest but most eye opening next step to learning ai or perhaps better phrased what s the hello world equivalent to ai i understand the normal response to this is you don t or x years experience or learn x algorithms i m looking for more out of the box thinking after all there is a whole world of things happening behind hello world that is not known by a first time programmer in order to create hello world its just scratching the surface to the giant world behind the scenes hence hello world not i now know everything about this world this is what i m looking for and that is my challenge i hope that others will find this question to be as fun to entertain as i have lately and i look forward to reading any responses i get edit in case anyone else was curious i found what i was looking for here it starts with an overview thats an extremely simplified explanation of the difficult stuff that then uses example code and the game snake to implement as a demo the code is well commented and allows the user to dig into details to figure out what s happening and how its making decisions thanks everyone for your suggestions as well i found them pretty helpful', 'the title is supposed to say first game with experience i can t change it game link hey everyone i recently got laid off from my job as a videographer and editor to keep myself busy and learn new skills i decided to try making a video game despite having zero experience i used the ai language model claude opus to write the game s code and it blew me away with how much it could do i created the backgrounds using ai tools like dalle and adobe generative fill but i m still working on making my own sprites using placeholders for now it s been a wild ride learning about game development and seeing how ai can help in the process i m considering monetizing the game in the future but it s still pretty rough in its current state i d appreciate any suggestions on what i could do to polish it up and make it more marketable also i d love to hear your thoughts and any experiences you ve had with ai assisted projects feel free to check out the game and let me know what you think please also feel free to post to the official forum on the games website p s this is still a work in progress and the game currently does not restart from the beginning on level so unfortunately the game ends on level this will be fixed soon there are many bugs at the moment but i don t know what i m doing and am completely relying on the help of ai this entire post was written by claude opus but reviewed by me please read the description on the games website before you begin also this has only been tested on a pixel a and should play in landscape mode please tell me if that doesn t work game link i m blown away that i never had to type a single line of code myself also if you are playing on desktop please make the browser window as small as possible i totally get any hate that i receive from this but i just wanted to show what is possible using just llm s i ve never been a game developer and i know nothing about it i found this experience to be truly eye opening and a lot of fun just imagine the possibilities game link']

Name: 138_physics_phd_astrophysics_theoretical
Representative_Docs: ['i m a physics grad ms physics st semester in csu fresno and have a bachelor s degree in physics as well initially as a kid i was really fascinated by science and wanted to jobs i won t say i m the cream of the crop in physics otherwise i would ve joined a premier institution for grad school even then i sought out to do a masters to see how things are in reality now i realize physics is not so fun to be honest not because it s complex but because it s not practical ofcourse that s what i should have expected before stepping into this field but i have become a bit considering data science as it pays well and i love math not an expert but i still love it regardless and it s a lot more practical since the amount of data is increasing each day so i ll probably finish my masters in physics and then maybe do a diploma another masters aimed towards data science analysis all i wanna see is whether i ve got the skills or at least the ability to learn it quickly to be able to dive deeper into data science or not what do i actually need to do in order to make this transition from physics to data science in terms of knowledge and application i have been doing a bit of coding in c in this semester and years in high school as well and i m gonna take undergrad python class next semester but other than that i would really love to get feedback from the experts on monte carlo simulation i really like working with the professor i m not yet sure whether the chair has the financial resources to finance a doctorate but that doesn t matter to me at the moment important i love theoretical physics but i m unsure what the chances are on the job market if you ve studied theoretical physics sure you ll find a job but from what i ve found out now it often doesn t have much to do with physics but i would probably also find data science interesting i could do a master s thesis in technical physics here i would be concerned with the development of a room temperature superconductor of course you always have to be careful when the keyword room temperature superconductor is mentioned but the professor s idea is promising but what is much more important is that the chair would finance the investigation development of a new type of spectrometer i came up with i haven t done any market analysis on this yet but it could be a very interesting component unfortunately i wouldn t have much to do with theoretical physics anymore but rather with the manufacturing of components which i also find exciting i find myself in a kind of dilemma i e if i do the work in theoretical physics then i would throw away the chance to develop my own component and possibly start a company from it if i do the work in technical physics then i probably won t have much to do with theoretical physics anymore or other people will be much more qualified than me and hence have better chances for phd positions i also thought about doing my master s thesis in technical physics and then possibly doing a doctorate in theoretical physics if among other things my idea wasn t good or there was simply no market it would be nice if someone has some advice on this perhaps from their own experience', 'tl dr finishing with a bs in engineering physics on track for ms in computer science considering going for a physics phd after working in software for years reasonable if so how to maximize chances of getting into a decent school because phd value is disproportionately determined by institutional prestige long story after bouncing around between bioengineering electrical engineering physics and computer science classes during my freshman and sophomore years i declared cs in my junior year i didn t feel great about the decision even then kind of felt like i was selling out and giving up on the search for fulfillment incidentally this happens so often that it s something of a meme at my school as luck would have it i took a small physics class group theory for physicists that year to fulfill a requirement and i loved it at the same time i was in an algorithms class that basically consisted of a professor who clearly hated having to talk to Representative_Docs: ['a little background i am an engineer that regularly uses python to help me do my work i am fairly proficient in python though definitely not an expert my python knowledge comes the linear regression notebook i felt like the topics were being thrown at me without prior explanation for example take a look at this import sklearn let s pull our handy linear fitter from our prediction toolbox sklearn from sklearn linear model import linearregression import numpy as np great for lists arrays of numbers initializing our x and y variables x car data age y car data selling price set up our model linear linearregression train the model linear fit x y title visualize the fit with this cell import matplotlib pyplot as plt y pred linear predict x plt plot x y pred color red plt scatter x y plt xlabel age set the labels of the x and y axes plt ylabel selling price lakhs plt show as you can imagine i was flabbergasted i know i can probably learn all of this if i was taken the concept is this how these python concepts are typically introduced', 'this is a popular topic but i don t often see a comprehensive answer i m by no means an expert and currently learning myself there s two key stepping stones before jumping into ai that being learning python and data science python has wide support and a host of libraries reflecting the latest research on ai development here the next step is to get a brief grasp of data science you can learn these from for python and r coursera course on data science udemy courses in python and r note these would most likely be paid consider ai specific courses found online theres two routes again here there s the heavily academic route that delves into the theory and mathematics then there s the practical route depends on the note the course uses octave which is similar to matlab style programming the courses when accessed individually are for free or you can pay for a certification various lectures on youtube for mit and stanford s artificial intelligence courses a really good text book to check out is artificial intelligence a modern approach ai was traditionally scripted in lisp or prolog this has been coverted into python over here practical krill ermenko ai https machine learning and deep learning from a z fast ai dives into keras a top level library']

Name: 144_biology_bioinformatics_computational_lab
Representative_Docs: ['i graduated with a bachelor s and master s degree in biology i took many classes and attended bootcamps to learn bioinformatics and now i ve started a phd where i will focus solely on computational work i won t be developing any tools or software my work will primarily involve bioinformatics data analysis that said i want to improve my machine learning skills so i plan to incorporate them into my project anyway i need to choose a department for my enrollment i can either enroll in a biology department since my research field is biology or in a bioinformatics department i m not sure if it matters and no one around me seems to know the requirements for both departments are the same i m in germany the institute i am working in is quite well known so i have its name already in my pocket i want to continue my career in bioinformatics and stay in research but i m unsure whether i ll stay in academia or move to industry after finishing my phd does it matter which department i enroll in would enrolling in bioinformatics be an advantage since my undergraduate degree is in biology', 'i am also nearing the end of my phd and am in a similar position i started off with wet lab and now almost all my work is computational i feel there are two types of labs when it comes to computational biology those that are working on developing new computational pipelines or statistical techniques and those that are generating and analyzing large amounts of data using existing protocols while the former need someone with a strong background in programming the latter are more interested in someone with a strong biology background and the ability to use existing tools for data analysis i have been working extensively with sequencing data like rna seq chip seq atac seq and hic and have never felt the need to learn a programming language like python i just know enough bash programming to run bioinformatics jobs and enough r to manipulate data and make good figures the fact that i have a strong base in biology helps me come up with better analysis ideas that the bioinformatician in my lab and i think even if i knew serious programming i probably would never have used it because for most genomics tasks there are extensive published resources and you just need to know enough programming to understand and use them i personally feel most genomics genetics labs need people who are not bioinformaticians but biologists with some computational skills when it comes to hardcore computational biology however i think the limiting factor is not the lack of programming skills because anyone can learn the basics of a language like python in a few months but inadequate background in maths and stats which are essential for jobs involving modelling and machine learning etc so in my view someone with strong biology background and basic computational skills would be in high demand in academic genomics genetics type labs but will probably have to learn a lot more to go into computational jobs in industry', 'a few days ago i reposted a list of questions that you can answer during a graduate school interview this is my response to the ones that were interview will be tomorrow so wish me luck amp x b tell us a little about yourself amp x b i graduated from my bachelors last year in molecular biology with a specific interest in genetics and biochemistry its aligned with the subjects i was most interested in as a kid the world of microscopic machines and dna research is something i definitely want in my future throughout my undergraduate years i took a special liking to the hours i spent at the lab i liked it so much that i decided to build my rudimentary lab at home i want to understand the fundamentals of biology and the chemistry within us and apply it to technology in a way that i believe is quite lacking in todays academic literature amp x b what motivated you to apply to grad school amp x b my fascination with synthetic biology and biomedical engineering lead me to get in contact with prominent researchers at the pasteur institute and max plank society to ask for guidance on how to go about realizing that dream they advised me that biomedical engineering degrees are a good way to learn interdisciplinary science and to be able to apply it is a huge plus amp x b why specifically x amp x b i find the way the program is structured is interesting with a first semester purely theoretical section and a second semester internship option in this structure it allows me to gain much more practical knowledge than if it was kept for the fourth semester like most other schools i find that the program is well suited and structured for individuals who are interested in pursuing a phd and this is something ive wanted to do for a long time and eventually have my own lab someday i have been in contact with both professors research institutions and alumni at the university to ask questions about the program and hear their opinion on it the responses were overwhelmingly positive which cant be a bad sign ive also been in touch with influential researchers like ceers bekker and kate adamala and i sent them a list of programs they could review they advised me to apply to x in particular as the opportunities at the collaborative universities are quite vast and the research going on in my field of interest is quite pronounced here amp x b why do you feel prepared to start graduate school amp x b i have finally been able to pinpoint exactly what i want to dedicate my life to during my undergraduate years i often speculated what kind of molecular biology research i wanted to conduct specifically it was during my senior year that i moved away from theoretical molecular biology to more applied molecular biology in the shape of nanotechnology and biomedical engineering i also showed myself that i am able to handle a large amount of stress during my final year of undergrad without giving up which i believe is a beneficial attribute to have going into graduate school and to be a successful student and researcher in the future amp x b biggest strengths and weaknesses amp x b im a detail oriented person which is a blessing in terms of getting things done on time and with the highest quality possible that said i find that ive needed to work on seeing the bigger picture of things this past year its still something im working on but i feel like ive made alot of progress in that regard academic achievement and learning has always been a priority for me and like most things in life the downside to that is i find myself to be too self critical this is also something i work on addressing how to balance becoming more flexible while still maintaining a commitment to achieve my goals right now im focusing more on my family this is why at least once every couple of days i call my parents and siblings i believe a cornerstone of the graduate experience is to be deeply involved and passionate in my research project a key factor to succeeding i would say would be to have a strong protective structure such as having a proper support system thats something ive been doing recently and has been incredibly fulfilling amp x b research interests amp x b this is a question with a two part answer every goal has a certain path but goals often have multiple paths in order to reach it amp x b first my ultimate goal has always been and probably will always be to increase the productive natural lifespan of a human being the fact that we only have years of life is pretty unfortunate in my point of view if i can learn enough about aging senescence and its molecular mechanisms then i hope to be able to advance the knowledge in this field in the future amp x b secondly the path towards my goal has been fluctuating somewhat throughout my academic life i base my changes on the utility it can have to the public currently the way i see is best to achieve my goal is through nanobiotechnology as i see it right now the first step towards that is to create nanoscale autonomous biosensors that imitate the quorum sensing capability in bacteria and report back to an artificial intelligence able to process the data in real time during my masters degree at x id like to develop this idea into a testable research project amp x b development of the field amp x b currently synthetic biology is used to create high value molecules in a joint team from mit and harvard actually produced several molecules like pyrrolnitrin within a remarkable short period of time research is ongoing to eventually be able to predictably engineer novel metabolic pathways genes or even entire organisms from scratch there is a lot of research into producing theranostic cell lines that can sense a diseases state and trigger an appropriate response but our metabolomic research isnt good enough to produce a disease fingerprint yet a lot of research is currently going into microfluidic systems and lab on chip development along with biosensors and biomaterial development and it seems most funding goes to these areas amp x b most significant accomplishment amp x b there are two but they are interlinked with each other amp x b the first one is actually my homemade lab ive managed to create machine that cost hundreds of thousands of dollars such as the nanodrop machine for a fraction of the cost the nanodrop machine cost me less than dollars in supplies it showed me that i dont need to rely solely on asking people for permission when it comes to doing things that i like i no longer needed to wait for my lab days to be in the lab i could just open a door and id be there it also gave me experience in asking questions to a more independent degree that i did before i started building it when designing your own experiments out of pocket you have to optimize every process in order to cut costs and work as effectively as possible in this way i learned a lot of methodology planning and general scientific procedures to reach a conclusion amp x b second is that i proved to myself that i could handle an immense amount of stress and mental discomfort by pulling myself out of my head after my kidnapping with the help of others since then ive learned that a large part of getting the places you want to get is largely dependent on yourself individually but the support network you have around you can help significantly in doing so those two points laid the groundwork for who i am today and will likely be responsible for my future accomplishments amp x b long term career goals amp x b if there is anywhere i want to be in the next years its in a lab of my interest theres a few in particular that i would like to explore if i am admitted to the program for instance amp x b pasteur institute david bikards synthethic biology team charles bariouds microfluidics and bioengineering team pierre lafayes antibody engineering team amp x b curie institute the multiscale physics biology chemistry and cancer department they are experts in the interdisciplinary field of synthetic biology and nanobiotechnology and the research they have produced is remarkable papers for example they detailed a method where they used click chemistry to label and visualize a large range of dynamic small molecules in vivo this is of great interest to me and my biosensor research interest amp x b university of paris microsystems and nanobiofluidics lab in the center for nanoscience and nanotechnology they have great team headed by pierre yves jourbert that deal with issues that are relevant to my research topic their biodevices and nanobiofluidics especially their research on microswimmers prompted me to actually send an email to joubert asking if he would be willing to allow me to shadow their team if i am admitted to the program functional and adaptive biology department they have a very relevant team for degenerative processes and aging that interests me their research involve the pathology of aging and it would be incredible to get to learn under a team with such a vision amp x b what else should we know about you what sets you apart from other candidates amp x b yes i dont believe we talked about it but i have quite a lot of experience in programming with different languages i currently know computer languages and plan to dive deeper into computer science by cs average salary is around k however i m going to bet that you re probably above th percentile at rutgers so i m honestly not sure how that translates accordingly to how well you ll do if you decide to take the transfer option it is not not not not not worth it to transfer to cornell if you re going to end up being average here and achieve the same outcome k base salary screams industry standard swe job in silicon valley seattle upper end of nyc as you would if you stayed at rutgers the supposed benefits of an ivy league education ex the supposed more intelligent friends better network better Name: 155_code_use ai_ai_use
Representative_Docs: ['avoid ai for most of your project i m in a similar boat to you going back to college and ya ai is a great tool to help get through it but your not actually learning that much by tweaking the code after it spits it out there s a lot more to learn in how to take an idea and how to start making it one of the biggest lessons i ve learned so far is in planning and design sitting down and actually breaking down each element what do i want what do i need who will be doing x and who does y what date should i be using and why learning how to define the success factors and set realistic goals is vital in coding as i m going through it i ll jot down possible approaches and methods i might need going forward i don t need to fully code them now but i need to make sure i have everything tight for when things get complicated later you won t be designing every system you work on from the ground up but you will need to be able to read and identify what the other coders were doing what were they thinking when they made this method or class and where does it go you won t be able to feed full applications into an ai to read them out to you when you re on a team i also put a lot of work in before i started college and got advanced entry into y at christmas i was miles ahead of most in the class and took the foot of the gas looking at other languages instead come april i realised i d slipped too much and had work my balls off to catch up yes i had a basic understanding of what we did but i couldn t use it effectively the best example was sql i could read sql i knew where the query was going how things joined but i struggled trying to fix it together myself i used ai while making my database connections and had to constantly look back to do them from scratch myself eventually i just stopped using the ai designed methods as this is something i need to be able to do we had interviews presenting our final projects at the end of the year and they asked you have well designed methods for database queries in the first frames but you don t use them later in the project why i explained that while i would be using those methods from the helper files in real world conditions i wasn t happy with my understanding of them i couldn t do them from memory and kept making errors so i decided to drill them in by typing them out each time when it came to exams i flew through the test because i a understood how i would do it b was able to identify what they were looking for and how to trace back to it and c i made the mistakes and worked my way through them so when they happened there i knew what i was looking at tldr don t use ai to design everything yes it ll spit out loads of lovely code that works for this window of the project but it won t improve your skills or work when you only have this small section on larger projects keep pushing in college you might be ahead right now but that ll slip real quick it you get lazy', 'hey there i m a rd year software engineering student and has now done significant coding and learning during these past years everything was going fine i got coding assignments and semester projects i struggled i coded stuff and i submitted most of the times things went as expected other times not as much in the end i got to learn new things well that was until recently we started getting to work with web technologies and considering the ease of access and other obvious reasons seeking help from ai tools was allowed for lab tasks i took the phrase seeking help differently from my fellows or rather they did while i tried learning and figuring out the generated snippets my fellows were already done with all the tasks basically copying and pasting the tasks done by the tool rather than coding it themselves this early completion set the bars higher for what can be called average this gradually influenced my thoughts about using ai and caused conflict internally within me yes ai boosts efficiency but we are not coding for a company making value for them we are here to learn and copying and pasting snippet ain t helping with that but since we are graded based on what we submit it was only natural to switch to what everyone was doing i got into this bad habit of just getting things done by ai tools the realisation hit me when during a lab examination no ai tools and not even a smart code editor was allowed just that good old ubuntu text editor for a programming course the recklessness of relying on ai made me pay the price i could not do great there it almost felt like i was left handicapped worst feeling i ve ever had i made a mistake or is using ai like this normal i m so confused when i talk about this to my fellows they laugh it off telling me everyone does it the skills i have are indeed fading due to the low amount of code written and thought about the amount of thinking writing and testing code what i used to write went down drastically over the last few months i could ve learnt a lot i have the concepts but the reliance on ai is setting me at a rather risky spot making me seriously concerned i tried re doing tasks at home but considering the university s tough schedule it got difficult to continue how do i genuinely balance this out can someone relate to this scenario i appreciate any help sorry to make it too long just wanted to address an issue that i thought many other people can probably relate to thanks', 'hello i m a rising senior in college studying computer science one thing that i still struggle with especially when learning a new language or framework or using a new library is that i don t know how much ai to use this may sound like a weird question but let me explain my thought process i feel like whenever i use google and forums to manually search for answers to my questions i just learn more sometimes i don t find the answer directly but i get pointed to somewhere else thanks to which i just learn more maybe indirectly related stuff also concepts just make more sense when i google stuff because usually i have to adapt the code from the forum to my own context which inevitably requires more thinking overall i just feel more satisfied and feel like i learn more but the downside is that sometimes i end up wasting time for configuring a project or finding the boilerplate code to get something just working i really don t know where to draw the line in contrast when i use any kind of generative ai i get the answers almost instantly sure sometimes the answer is not correct but i can rapidly fix the problems by going over the code this is a lot faster and more efficient than a google search yet i feel like i am not really learning it s so easy to just copy and paste the code and not really spend time understanding what s really happening even if i spend the time understanding what s going on it s really not exactly like learning the concept yourself i think a good analogy is looking at the answer of a practice exam question and being like yeah this one s easy i get it yet if you get the question later and have to solve it yourself you just get stuck i feel the same way with the solutions of generative ai when i integrate it to my own code i just wouldn t be able to code the same thing myself in the future if i get it from a generative ai the first time so my question for more experience programmers what is your take many programmers here who are in the industry right now didn t really have any of these ai tools in the past so they had to do the searching and learning manually through forums books etc this seems like a more sustainable way to learn yet i am curious if you experienced programmers are integrating ai into your workflow now if so how for example would you use ai if you re learning something entirely new or would you only use it when you re coding something that you already know but you would rather not write it all by hand and waste time i have been really contemplating canceling my chatgpt subscription so that i can get myself to google and learn more whenever i do that i just learn better and feel more satisfied yet ai is not going anywhere in the near future so it also feels like a loss to not really take advantage of it what are your thoughts']

Name: 156_email_emails_sent_colleges
Representative_Docs: ['i m actually shocked you are asking us and not ai lol but seriously i asked chatgpt your question and this was the response you re not as cooked as you might think these kinds of mistakes happen more often than you d imagine admissions officers understand that applicants are human and sometimes make errors here s what you can do to recover send a follow up email quickly acknowledge the mistake and clarify the intent keep it light but professional for example subject clarification on my previous email dear admissions officer s name i wanted to apologize for an oversight in my previous email it seems i accidentally included a note to myself at the bottom please disregard that portion and i appreciate your understanding thank you for your time and i look forward to hearing from you best regards your name keep perspective admissions officers are used to working with students who are learning to navigate professional communication a small mistake like this won t ruin your chances especially if the rest of your application is strong own it with grace and it ll likely be forgotten as a minor blip take this with a grain of salt but i thought it was interesting to see what ai had to say about it', 'fear not chatgpt can help with this one too the situation is definitely embarrassing but it s not the end of the world admissions officers are human too and they likely understand the increasing use of tools like ai in communication here s what you can do send a follow up email write a short sincere apology acknowledging the mistake for example i realized i accidentally included an unintended note in my previous email i apologize for the oversight and i hope it doesn t detract from the purpose of my message be honest not overly formal avoid making it a bigger deal than it is the follow up email is more about showing professionalism and awareness move on once you ve sent the apology don t dwell on it admissions officers are unlikely to hold this against you unless it s a repeated issue the mistake might even come across as humorous or relatable to some acknowledging it gracefully could work in your favor you re so cooked', 'i had Name: 157_phd_job_data science_ms
Representative_Docs: ['i ll give you the perspective of a th year neuroscience postdoc about to enter the tenure track job market your decision to pursue a ph d should be heavily determined by your end goal it sounds like you have a disdain for industry so i m guessing you really want to stay in academia if you want to be a tenure track professor you have a long and difficult climb between grad school and post docs you should expect at least years until you re competitive if you re field is similar to mine once you get to post doc you ll start around k and maybe get closer to k over the years depending on your funding mechanism you may have good benefits but you may not i had to resist being transferred to a funding mechanism that had no matching k and even worse stripped my ability to contribute my own money to a k plus health insurance was more expensive would have cost me an extra per year envision you re well into your s have little ability to save for retirement and not a lot of extra cash generally despite the sentiment in the other comments k is not much and will make starting a family saving for retirement and enjoying life a struggle look at daycare costs if your spouse has substantial income it s of course another story but who can assume that s how life will turn out maybe assume that won t happen another issue is the probability that you ll need to do multiple postdocs which often means uprooting your family and moving across the country i stayed at one place the entire time but i got penalized for that when i tried to get a k people start to assume you have nothing left to learn and you should move on if you are in one place for too long what s even worse about this track is not only do you have to sacrifice financially for many years but your success is far from guaranteed unless you re confident you can land in the top of all the people in your field going after the same jobs you may not get a good job however this is largely determined by what type of professor you want to be if you want to be a research intensive professor at a major research university it is very competitive if you are ok with mostly teaching and making a lot less money some of those positions start in the k area maybe it s easier to get a job a professor position at an r school will often start at k with potential for around k in summer salary if you get a grant that s a big if eventually you can get up into the high k or beyond if you are really successful that s also a big if say you try and fail to become a professor at that point you can probably pivot to industry and make good money but at what cost could you have avoided the many years of post doc if you really want to do data science is a ph d in computational neuroscience really the best route maybe you can get a masters in stats data science instead and in years be making k but then again maybe now you re in some poorly run corporation that only wants to hear what they want to hear and data science doesn t feel like science these are tough decisions with many unknowns and finance is only one of many considerations reach out now to data scientists and ask for an informational interview you need to really assess what a potential job will feel like to summarize identify one or maybe a few end career goals that really sound desirable and realistic then work backwards from there pursue the degrees and goals now that get you there and be realistic about the financial and general sacrifices it s not just about your passion especially if that means you jump headfirst into pursuing a degree without really assessing the probable outcome i appreciate your hesitation at this point and i think it is very healthy do your homework and make an informed decision', 'hey i m currently in the summer going into my senior year and i ve got a big question in front of me that i ve wanted some outside perspective on whether or not getting a phd is right for me without saying too much about my summer work i work at one of facebook google as a software engineering intern in the bay area after seeing the work the phds ai ml do at my company and on my team i think it would be really cool to do the type of work that they do model building and complex analytical problems as opposed to the standard software development tasks when it comes to my work i care mostly about getting to work on challenging and complex problems as well as making a living that will allow for me to one day provide for my family have a nice house with kids and yearly vacations etc furthermore i m actually a fan of school and i love learning technical things especially pertaining to data science and deep learning so i feel like i m not in a rush to leave school given how much there is there to learn and i enjoy it in terms of my intellectual fit for a phd i would love to do it and can definitely see myself doing it on an interest level the other thing is that i m not too big a fan of the bay area and would prefer to live in chicago nyc where i feel the career opportunities aren t the same as in tech and would probably require a move into finance trading to make comparable compensation i think i d be happier doing data science work and think that the phd is route to go there as it opens up doors to work in either a lab in industry or as a quant at a trading firm i feel like if i went the phd route i d want to be able to get into quant finance which would allow me to work in chicago nyc while making good money and while working on ml data science problems or at an ai lab in industry possibly in nyc where i could make good money at one of the big tech company labs there my concern with attempting to get a phd is not knowing whether i could get into a top school that would open the doors that i d want opened if i was doing a phd i ve read online that especially finance companies weigh the prestige of your school heavily i would want to be at one of stanford mit berkley cmu and maybe something in the next tier toronto uiuc cornell nyu to study deep learning which would be a very competitive thing and i m not sure about the feasibility of getting in from undergrad like i would want to but when it comes to the actual work i think the work that phds do from what i ve seen developing amazing technologies like duplex autonomous cars algorithmic trading algorithms etc would make me much happier than the kind of work i m doing now in my internship which is worth a lot to me if i knew i would get into a great school like those i would no doubt want to go that route because even if the pay was the same i d just be so much more interested in the work it s tough for me to pursue both because i d have to accept a full time offer at the end of this summer early fall which is well before any admissions decisions from graduate schools and i don t want to be on the outside of everything looking in i m a strong student at a top ish cs school but don t have much research experience only semesters worth of machine learning related research which means i might have a shot at one of those programs but it would be very tough considering how competitive they are so i m really conflicted and i don t know what to do tl dr i would love to get a phd but am not sure how to balance the need of chasing accepting a full time offer which i would need if i couldn t get into a phd program and would be my nd choice which i would need to decide on well before i know if i got into a phd program', 'i was in a similar situation to you here is my experience i did my phd also in the biological sciences with half experimental and half computational work i graduated after years and sold out for a data science job at a big tech company to be honest with you if i could go back in time i d have told my college self to go straight into tech instead the reasons are twofold i overestimated my chance to get a tt faculty job years spent in phd are diminishing returns if you are going into tech just like most grad students i entered my phd program because i m passionate about my field and would like to be a professor one day i knew that it s gonna be very competitive to land a faculty job i just didn t realize how brutal it actually is i was very fortunate to be in a great school and a great group and ended up with a nature paper from grad school but still i don t think i had a good shot at getting a tt job at a r university by the end of my phd i just wasn t ready to commit another years of my life in my s working hours a week as a postdoc to chase a pipedream while trying to settle down and have a family at the same time there is no doubt that a phd is going to be helpful for landing a data science job however if you are competitive enough to get into a top grad school you are competitive enough to get a good data science job new phd data scientists will usually have higher starting salaries than new bs grads but on average the peers of the phd data scientists who go straight into data science after their bs ms will have much higher salaries than the phd data scientists by the time they start their first job fwiw the y o kids in my team is making as much as i do coupled with the ridiculous tech employee compensations nowadays ask yourself the question are you ready to leave gt half a million dollar on the table to pursue a phd ultimately only you can determine if it s worth it to pursue a phd given your long term career goals do take the time to recognize the pros and cons of pursuing the two career paths and think hard about what you are willing to sacrifice for a academic career i had a great time in grad school but i do regret wasting my s on something that won t be useful to my future career hope my experience is helpful the situation would be very different if you are doing a phd in cs in some hot areas like deep learning however these are the exceptions not the rule total compensation for a new bs grad data scientist at a big bay area tech company is at least k k is not unheard of assuming a very modest pay raise per year in reality you can get a lot more by jumping job every years and a very generous phd stipend of k a year a conservative estimate of your net loss by the end of a year phd average of biological sciences is more like years is k']

Name: 166_research_papers_mentor_independent research
Representative_Docs: ['hi everyone i m a current college student and researcher with a background in aerospace machine learning and astrophysics i got started in research back in high school through a passion for astronomy and science olympiad eventually i founded a student research group where i collaborated with professors and peers on original projects that experience helped me grow tremendously and i was fortunate to become a regeneron finalist as a result notably when i was starting out i didn t have family connections to professors or research labs so whereas a lot of my peers found opportunities through their parents or their parents friends i had to figure out how to get started on my own hence if you re also someone who s motivated but not sure where to begin i can definitely offer perspective and advice from that angle furthermore i m hoping to mentor a few high school students who are seriously interested in contributing to some real technical research projects i ve already scoped out these are volunteer based collaborative opportunities for students looking to go beyond typical schoolwork and gain hands on experience in science and engineering in areas like remote sensing and machine learning on satellite imagery python fusion magnet fabrication for stellarators arduino rpi cad d printing python and astrophysics research using machine learning on x ray binary data python basic astronomy if this sounds exciting to you feel free to drop a comment i m also happy to answer general questions about how to get started with research in high school or share more about my own path if it s helpful', 'i m an undergraduate looking to do enter academics and work in research projects i study at a university where research is obligatory professors publish for the sake of maintaining university rankings ive been trying to enter professor projects talk to them about their work interact with some of the doctoral students but its all futile the only research my computer science department is doing is ai research this includes the usual nlp neural network ml deep learning pattern recognition disease detection and other stuff i m not interested in anything to do with ai i wish to study computational theory or signal processing while i don t understand all these publishings and i don t think they re all trash i m sure a good number of these are of questionable quality papers covering what could have been a single paper while applications review papers and other stuff a conference for undergraduate and graduate projects from within the university was recently held i saw projects presented every single one of them was ai undergraduates are only doing ai projects and so are the graduates i m tired and i m scared that when i pursue my own interests in grad school i might fail to get any funding or support i need someone to make me understand is everyone doing ai research because people are suddenly really interested in ai research or is it because institutes are only funding ai projects because everyone is in a stupid race to have the best model and publishers have a very low bar for rejecting dubious ai submissions because of the hype am i looking at things the wrong way and if this really is a terrible thing when is the hype gonna go away is this just my institute or is this commonplace now i d rather work in industry than study a subject i couldn t care less about', 'ms in geography i have used lots of statistical methods and also qualitative methods understanding and searching different quantitative and qualitative methods you might want to use is something students should get a big feel for before starting research you likely did this in undergrad whether it be explicitly stated or not for research projects think of how you want to conduct the research a model a review coding stats some advisors will have you do an extension of their research and may give you a research question for your thesis for example if your thesis advisor specializes in medical robotics you pick your advisor based on your expertise at least in my field then you probably will do something in medical robotics and they will work with you for that for my thesis i am mostly a historical geographer so i knew i wanted to work with a historical geographer one method i used was pearsons r in a correlation analysis involving r some of what i researched was climate variables and their correlation to streamflow for a major river thesis work is hard and you put in the work i came up with my thesis research question after a few months of knowing what i was generally interested in good at in geography from all of my coursework and experience in the field i am sure you have taken classes and have a topic or niche in your field you like more than others from there using google scholar is a great tool to searching key words terms etc to find relevant and recent try for studies within the last years for the most part this varies per field i guess studies also look into journals i e journal of artificial intelligence research reading through the abstract intro and discussion of said articles can give you a lot of the information without reading the entirety of the article you can check out their methods section too for methods used in robotics research etc also look at those articles citations in their bibliography from there you can find a bunch of literature and sift through titles you think will benefit you i am sure there s maybe also a core class in your program that helps with writing and research etc i would suggest downloading mendeley desktop not reference manager to your computer you can easily download the pdfs of the articles and save them so you can have them in your thesis in word download the mendeley plug in so you can insert your citations in your thesis with ease make sure to talk to friends in your classes other professors aside from your thesis advisor etc because they can help you articulate ideas and guide you on methods used in the field and send you articles to look at give you ideas a typical day for me was teaching ta going to whatever class i had that day and conducting my research on the side doing research and the writing chapter by chapter let me know if you want anything elaborated on it has been a ride but you learn so much become an expert in your thesis research and the thesis track really sets you up to be a great researcher']

Representative_Docs: ['so me and two other people have started a project that consists of analyzing buzzfeed shopping list articles and try to automate the process so if an article is top ten headphones to buy under we want to generate an article using machine learning and nlp that would put together ten headphones or something related to headphones under and also make a description for it so right now we have the amazon customer reviews dataset which has all the customer reviews of all the products till in another dataset we scraped data from buzzfeed shopping section of about articles meaning we have all the products with their corresponding amazon links what we re not exactly sure is how to proceed with what we want to do we re confused as to how to use nlp here for machine learning we have some idea as to what the features could be for each product like its ratings its category etc and so it trains on that and then gets the corresponding products from the customer reviews dataset but if you have some ideas as to how to proceed with the project do let me know tldr want to analyze a buzzfeed shopping list article and using nlp and machine learning want to create a similar article in a way trying to automate the process', 'hey everyone i m a postgraduate student who is looking for ideas to build an nlp project that is not only unique but also has the potential for publication not compulsory but recommended within a month i have a foundational understanding of nlp information retrieval and basic nlp techniques i know a bit about transformers but haven t trained any models yet given my tight timeframe and the high expectations from my professor i m seeking some guidance on potential project ideas here s what i m looking for nlp projects i need a project idea that goes beyond basic nlp tasks ideally it should involve a significant amount of task and novel applications of existing methods it can also include finetuning a model for specific task but there should be significant amount of work feasibility the project should be manageable within a month considering my current skill level and the time required for learning and development datasets it would be great if the project involves datasets that are easily accessible and well documented publication potential any suggestions that might lead to work of publishable quality would be especially valuable it is not compulsory but the prof asked me if i can do some work worthy of publication i ve tried getting suggestions from ai tools like chatgpt and claude but wasn t fully satisfied with the results i d really appreciate any recommendations resources or guidance you can provide thanks in advance', 'hello i am a second year cs student who just finished an introductory ds amp a course and our final project dealt with machine learning and nlp i found it very interesting and have a few questions here are the project specs you re free to read through all of it but the ml nlp stuff begins toward the bottom in the classifying piazza posts with nlp and ml section also if you re not familiar with euchre it s a card game i m really interested in taking the next step so to speak in terms of an ml nlp project so i could learn more because i find it interesting and as something to maybe put on my resume so here are my questions the project we did uses the bag of words model and the naive bayes classifier from what i understand this model isn t particularly effective because it doesn t consider word combinations the white house would be considered three separate words for example when it would be a lot easier to classify if we considered all three of the words together i believe i understand this concept very well but i m also seeing that another downside of this model is that it can overfit your model because every new word would increase your vector size i m not sure why this would be because it seems like you would have to consider every word regardless of the model you choose our project only considered a handful of labels topics this made me wonder what would be the next stage ideally you d want to be able to classify any string of text but that would mean an impossibly large number of labels wouldn t it if i wanted to create a moderately useful classifier i would want it to be able to classify more than just a few labels like in the project but i don t really know what s feasible as i thought more about this it occurred to me that a search engine is kind of a classifier itself isn t it if i search a phrase in google for example many links talking about the same thing are going to come up could you make use of that in creating a classifier something like inputting a word string of words searching it behind the scenes and then just find out which word words pop up most on a search engine and that would be the label tl dr in short i created a very simple classifier for a school project and i would like to create a new personal project both to learn about building my own project from scratch and to learn more about ml nlp and maybe have something to put on my resume where should i go from here what would be the next step up considering the project i already did any advice is greatly appreciated']

Representative_Docs: ['so a few observations things here the preponderance of evidence standard asks the following question more likely than not and a feather considering all the documentation and evidence presented at the hearing did a person violate the code in question check your academic integrity policy on the panel is it majority rules unanimous decision that makes a difference in how you approach things were you offered in your informal a chance to re do the work if you turned that offer down it will look worse for you did you get books from the library did you have an appointment with the reference librarian staff do you have sessions with the writing lab do you have the actual handwritten notes did you make an annotated bibliography in word or google doc as you were getting started on your paper did your professor request updates or have incremental assignments concerning this paper and did you turn them in and get graded on them do you have time stamped versions of your drafts can you request that it verify those versions in your school s cloud can you tell the panel how you came up with the idea and recap the paper and answer questions in an oral defense of it if you can produce all or even some of question s documentation for the love of all that s holy skip the video of you typing it because that will likely not be convincing at all to a panel unless you have a key logger all they ll see is your face or your screen which will show nothing persuasive a panel is human and the members will look at this as though you had arranged an alibi you don t need a lawyer you need an advisor who is familiar with the school s code and panel process if you have the right to have an advisor ask the student conduct folks or dean for one or find one yourself going forward prior to turning any paper in from the day you begin work on it i advise the following on your school s cloud based drive onedrive etc create a folder for the paper create word or googledoc notes in the form of an annotated bibliography saving as automatically prior versions will be saved do not use grammarly as it is ai request an appointment with your school s reference librarian with the general idea that you have and ask for assistance with sources and references keep that appointment ask if they will give your final draft a check for citation correctness create a first draft of your paper with first draft and the date in the file name well before the due date run it through turnitin for a plagiarism ai check and save the report in the folder change anything that raises a red flag if it s not a simple flag on using the same source request a review of the paper from either your professor or the writing lab attaching the draft document to the email attend and bring up any concerns you may have from the turnitin report and or the quality of your sources follow up the appointment with an email of thanks and a recap of the advice given implement advice work on revising your paper and save it as draft draft etc each time you make substantial changes move paragraphs add sections etc add your references and double check your citations and works cited references run it through turnitin and do a final check changing anything that looks suspicious if there s a weird flag that you can t address email your professor right away with that concern email a copy to your
