Name: 172_data science_science_data_masters
Representative_Docs: ['i m a part time online grad student for a course based master s in data science at boston university graduating in december i was also given an admit to kennesaw state s msit with an admit semester of next spring i rejected a similar offer from fsu due to cost and because they wanted me to start this semester without any way to defer i also work at a fortune that s going to experience layoffs in the coming months so i m trying to plan out my next steps and i feel like a part time program in it would help me with exploring my research interests while allowing me to work full time or do a full time job search given that i was given an admit to an r institution would i be placing myself at a disadvantage for the possibility of a phd program in it or a related field in the near future otherwise would an r master s hold some weight over let s say a foreign master s from the philippines i ask due to the drastic difference in cost why do i want to go the academic route i love looking at intersecting topics and my data science program has allowed for me to really dive deep into researching various fields via feature selection ml methods and the like i specifically want to explore emerging technologies especially pertaining to ethical ai concerns human computer interaction and proper data collection methods while having the opportunity to really absorb these topics and formulate a more conclusive thesis idea in the process i wanted to join a program that allows me to go from to research competency ksu s program allows students to get a beginner it certificate as a prerequisite to full admission i feel a different kind of imposter syndrome when i m doing coursework and the environment seems to be more supportive and open in industry they often want diverse cohorts without accommodating us years of being in industry and i still yearn to keep doing more academic coursework i started an msit at liberty university and i want a redo due to instant regret for doing the program out of desperation won t go too much into detail the total program cost including the prerequisites is also more affordable than any in state program i live in texas and it s available online my capstone project for the omds program will revolve around traffic accident modeling planned route after omds ksu s msit semesters as a part time student including the research seminar and thesis courses in lieu of a capstone as my current program already does a capstone again i plan to start in the spring semester alternative route i m eligible for birthright philippine citizenship i m a u s citizen by birthright as well and so i can apparently get a degree part time from the university of the philippines s open university at local cost however i would have to wait for the next admissions cycle and for my philippine citizenship paperwork to push through afterwards i m interested in phd programs that allow me to focus on research towards emerging technologies i was looking at suny binghamton s systems science program and while they somewhat stated that the omds would be sufficient background between the current political climate and not wanting to rush the process i feel like it would be best to gain more technical knowledge to help me with my research interests thank you to anyone who reads this and offers feedback', 'hi everyone i d love some advice from people who ve applied to competitive u s grad programs in ai data science or computational neuroscience especially if you came from nontraditional undergrad backgrounds i m choosing between two undergrad paths and want to understand how graduate admissions committees might view each one in terms of preparation credibility and long term outcomes my goal to apply for a strong u s master s in ai data science or computational neuroscience i want to build a solid foundation in math statistics and programming and eventually work globally in applied ai or research related fields option stay at tetr college degree data analytics and ai management it s a new project based global business school where students rotate countries every semester so far i ve studied in singapore nyc argentina and milan it emphasizes real world experience over traditional coursework pros rich international and project based learning affordable about k year freeing up time for self study and personal projects i ve worked on real world data projects singapore and nyc were especially valuable cons it s a new school no brand recognition or alumni network yet the degree is not fully stem it s more business focused with limited technical rigor weak formal background in math stats cs postgraduate work rights in the u s are unclear not guaranteed opt option transfer to kenyon college top u s liberal arts college major applied math and physics stem pros strong theoretical foundation in math stats and physics fully recognized u s stem degree eligible for years opt better academic credibility for u s grad schools cons around twice as expensive as tetr located in rural ohio with less access to global startup ecosystems not a major recruiting target for cs or data science internships networking would take extra effort what i m wondering would a business oriented degree in data analytics and ai management from a new school significantly hurt my chances at top u s master s programs in ai or data science if i stay at tetr can i compensate with a strong personal portfolio e g github gre quant kaggle publications how much weight do admissions committees give to formal coursework in linear algebra stats and computer science versus demonstrated ability and experience would the credibility and stem classification of kenyon significantly boost my chances for scholarships research access or admissions i d really appreciate your insights i want to make the best long term decision not just for getting into grad school but for building strong skills and a global career in data or ai', 'feel like i need to make some decisions about how i want to move forward in my career which has led me to consider data science or data science adjacent cs stats masters programs and i am looking for some advice on the matter i graduated from college with an economics degree and political science but that s kinda useless from a good but not absolute top tier university think nyu uva like with about a gpa and an interest in data science leaning roles after graduation i worked as a data analyst for a boston based e commerce company working mostly in sql and tableau got some experience with data manipulation and engineering and some business analysis but wanted to get more exposure to more sophisticated statistical analysis ml after about months i moved to a an analytics rotational development program at a major insurance firm in order to pursue more data science exposure i am about to finish that and have done three rotations the first was much like my old role so not super notable my second was in machine learning dev ops and i created a system for automating the retraining of a suite of models my third rotation has been using advanced modeling and analysis to improve our pricing in an upcoming industry i have also worked on a range of personal projects since graduating college after this rotation concludes i will place into a permanent role that will probably be in line with one of my final two rotations but could be less in the data science space if i get unlucky my current role has been useful for getting practical data science and machine learning experience but regardless of what my next position is i believe i may encounter some issues advancing into a fully certified data scientist level position in my company without an advanced degree i also do not want to be perpetually tied to this company anyway and when scanning for data scientist positions asking for advanced degrees seems to be the norm i have started to consider masters programs but have hesitation about how successful i can be in the admissions process considering my undergrad degree is not technical only went to calc on the math side and my gpa is not super impressive i think i have developed solid experience through my work and personal projects which i could hopefully convey with letters of recommendation or application attachments and i am fairly confident in my ability to do well on the gre did very well on the sat with minimal prep so hopefully a well prepped gre should be doable but i am skeptical that that will be sufficient furthermore i am still deliberating on what variety of program i should be looking at seems like this sub and others hold computer science and statistics masters in higher esteem than the newer collection of data science masters but i wonder if my background would be even less applicable to admissions for those programs so ultimately looking for input on if a masters is worth pursuing maybe some program recommendations and what my chances might be i have a bit of a preference for a full time program since i think i d benefit more from that even if the cost is higher but willing to consider otherwise and i d love to stay in boston but the program selection isn t the best i imagine i have no shot at mit or harvard and am in the midst of assessing the reputation of bu tufts north eastern so would consider to expanding to other schools in the north eastern us big post so appreciate any help or feedback zq']

receives was measured to be insufficient to draw many of the conclusions they make when learning language there is also evidence that certain social norms like the tendency toward certain facial expressions maybe genetically encoded as well at the same time while the brain is elastic there are genetic predispositions around brain structure and subconscious activities of the brain like breathing which are connected to conscious activities of the brain and likely impact our learning brains tend to have a common layout for which part handles touch or hearing etc and that topology is going to have an impact on which parts can learn most easily since they start out connected another aspect of the brain that seems inherited from out dna rather than painted onto a blank slate is emotion and primal drives like for food sex pleasure avoiding pain etc basically while an infant is blank slate relative to an adult there is a lot of evidence that the brain s inherited structure encodes a lot of key pieces of information or mechanism that are tailored to drawing certain conclusions which is basically the same thing by being born with a brain that rewards us for eating and punishes us for sitting still and alone a huge driving component of intelligence is pre written for us one of the hardest things about ai is that without some underlying drive there is no reason to do anything and therefore no measure by which the intelligence can distinguish good correct ideas from bad incorrect ideas similarly a very different set of drive from humans might result in apparent unintelligent behavior it s incredibly hard to clearly articulate a drive that makes ai turn out like a human in one example ai trying to learn how to play tetris simply learned to pause the game in order to not lose because every other strategy it tried resulted in it losing as i said above it would be valid for ai to simply not choose to play a game in order to be smart at the game the ai has to choose to play and choose to follow the rules that the human does this is a very challenging behavior to create which is necessary for artificial general intelligence and these narrow problem solutions e g go playing ai chess playing ai get around solving that by creating an ai that doesn t have such a choice and programming in the desire to win the game and follow the game s rules basically autonomy purpose etc are essential to general intelligence and are really hard to create in ways that will mimic what we consider intelligence gt most of the problems you outline here just sound like compartmentalization computers are super good at this it s the opposite of compartmentalization in general intelligence you cannot completely compartmentalize because you have no understanding of what the compartments should be to know how to compartmentalize you d have to already be super intelligent when i use a smartphone it s good that my brain is able to ease its way through this fuzzy connection that lets my expectations be intertwined with how computers act while also somehow not unlearning all of the computer things my knowledge isn t in compartments it s all intertwined gt so when i say please bring me an apple the logic might go like this please means the speaker wants me to do something bring is the action to perform apple and me are objects to perform the action on then it does an analysis of previously heard sentences to get context so it knows last time when i said please bring me an orange i showed it what that meant and it can just replace orange with apple computers that do this have existed for a while the problem is that you seem to treating does an analysis and showed it what that meant like magic and didn t explain how either of those things actually happen on a scale that leads to what we see as general intelligence it turns out that it s really hard to do an analysis of sentences because most sentences cannot be understood simply by looking at the words in them we need to be able to evaluate the world to establish which context is relevant we need to be able to judge the speaker to think what they would want mean judging people s thoughts is easier when you are also a person when there is ambiguity we have to be able to simulate the various possibilities in our head to eliminate ones that would obviously not make sense we also often have to compensate for mistakes awkward phrasing incomplete information etc a lot of ai systems exist that can respond decently well to language that follows a very specific structure we don t really consider these that intelligent because they easily get tripped up in real world situations that are much uglier it s also really hard to show what it means for one thing if you show it bring me an orange once it might not be able to distinguish between the the particulars of that situation e g the shape of orange the amount of time you took the way you hand it and the part it s actually supposed to associate with the phrase i e the general orange and putting it somewhere near you or in your hand heck it doesn t even know what an orange is so you ll have to teach it that so realistically you d have to give it an orange many times while varying many things about the situation so that it sees that the consistent thing across all is what those words actually mean this is extremely time consuming when you consider how many things you d have to show the system i mean i have no doubt that you can make ai that would respond to speech by giving you the fruit you ask for but general intelligence requires training it on an extremely large number of cases and the amount of time you have to invest is enormous and after you invest that time you might start to see it has difficulty learning certain distinctions because of the way you programmed it and might need to start the process again after you tweak the ai long story short systems like what you re describing here exist and they aren t that smart because what you re describing doesn t solve any of the hard problems it doesn t tell them how to analyze language it doesn t teach them the psychology and philosophy we are informed by when deciding the meaning of language and the speaker s intent it can t show the ai everything and some things are abstract and not showable where it does the amount of things it has to learn are enormous who is me and why does that keep changing what is an orange is that because it s orange colored apple isn t a color bringing mean to put it in that hand shaped thing they were sticking out but the way there fingers are bent this time it doesn t look like what i thought a hand was etc all for one instance of showing gt i know i m very wrong in my thinking on this i m still just not fully grasping where the training differs from childrearing i guess basically we ve never made ai that s as smart as a newborn newborns aren t blank slates they have structured developed brains that are tweaked to solve a lot of the problems we ll throw at them before we could train ai like we train a baby we d need to program in enormous amounts of intelligence to get it to the level of a baby while babies might not have many facts built into them they start with a lot of complex structures that encode some assumptions about intelligence that help them learn']

Name: 183_job_developer_team_junior
Representative_Docs: ['hi everyone tldr is which skills do i need to work on to get another job like the one that i m currently doing and kind of rolled into by happy accident i am working in a weird hybrid role now doing copywriting flow building technical analysis and some not really code configuration for a chatbot lots of talking to developers from the different platforms we implement with trying to find solutions for needs that come from the business then actually executing as well config and copywriting troubleshooting no actual designing and no actual coding though which is the source of my question i genuinely like my job and i think i m pretty good at it my job title isn t really set in stone bc i m a consultant but i m now considered the technical analyst of our small bot building team so the main question what are the skills that i would need to work on to find another job that is like this so with a focus on analysis problem solving implementation writing requirements my boyfriend is british i m from belgium which is where we are now living and we will probably want to move to the uk in the next years if you ve followed the new post brexit immigration requirements i m going to need all the qualifications i can get a role that is categorised as a business technical analyst ux designer or developer counts as a shortage occupation i am currently following a certification course on ai from ibm which will include a basic module on python if that doesn t make me cry i m considering learning more about that i m also considering a basic course in ux wireframing but i don t know if i m thinking in the right direction here any input from people working in this field super appreciated', 'i m unsure if this is cs enough i m in a bit of a weird role sorry if not appropriate i did read the faq amp rules tldr is which skills do i need to work on to get another job like the one that i m currently doing and kind of rolled into by happy accident i am working in a weird hybrid role now doing copywriting flow building technical analysis and some not really code configuration for a chatbot lots of talking to developers from the different platforms we implement with trying to find solutions for needs that come from the business then actually executing as well config and copywriting troubleshooting no actual designing and no actual coding though which is the source of my question i genuinely like my job and i think i m pretty good at it my job title isn t really set in stone bc i m a consultant but i m now considered the technical analyst of our small bot building team so the main question what are the skills that i would need to work on to find another job that is like this so with a focus on analysis problem solving implementation writing requirements my boyfriend is british i m from belgium which is where we are now living and we will probably want to move to the uk in the next years if you ve followed the new post brexit immigration requirements i m going to need all the qualifications i can get a role that is categorised as a business technical analyst ux designer or developer counts as a shortage occupation i am currently following a certification course on ai from ibm which will include a basic module on python if that doesn t make me cry i m considering learning more about that i m also considering a basic course in ux wireframing but i don t know if i m thinking in the right direction here any input from people working in this field super appreciated', 'hi all i m currently at a point in my career where i m about to quit my first job and start looking into the next one at this point i feel like i ve spent a good amount of time at the current job but the skills that are brought out of it aren t enough i d like to gain some advice from the community about my feelings what i should do next let me give you some background info amp x b i graduated college spring of with a comp sci degree and needless to say the program was sub par when i graduated i pretty much had a base foundation in c java and would quickly realize that i wasn t totally prepared for the job i was about to land right before i graduated i landed a job at a small benefits administration company as an application developer the tech team consisted of a project manager programmer a db developer and myself over the years that i ve been here i went from floundering on my first project to gaining a solid understanding of what a software development team was about to give a basic rundown we use sql server for our db c net for our main application and python for smaller applications automated tasks i had to learn python from scratch but on the way learned about pep good coding practices and the like i will say that i have come a pretty long way since my first day amp x b today i m appreciative of what i learned but i still feel under qualified for the amount of time i spent here why is that our team was all of people and we had no definitive development methodologies agile scrum ect we rarely if not never added tests to our code because the boss wanted tasks completed immediately this was a major problem as most of the stuff we were patching in python were due to bugs lack of tests in the main application thankfully the project manager and myself dove a bit into testing since we noticed that lack of tests would just open up even more bugs along the way but this wasn t a main priority due to the boss s need for immediate completion there was no definitive deployment pipeline making sure code passes whatever tests there were ect we use redmine to track tasks bugs and other projects and it has become hard to maintain i should also mention there is a developer in india we are based in new jersey usa that is the most senior he would constantly shoot down ideas for change improvements and new features that the project manager and i started to discover because he wasn t comfortable outside of his net development world amp x b about months ago the project manager which i should add has been working for the company for years at this point decided it was time to move on and landed himself a remote job i couldn t be more proud amp thankful of him because he s been a mentor to me and really put me on the right path to realizing the correct steps to become a software developer i am now the nd most senior behind the developer from india so i took over all the tasks that the project manager use to work on plus what i was doing currently i have no time to work on anything innovating or programming related i ve been regulated to providing reports to our clients through the database and again working on bug fixes that are caused by the main application most recently the boss decided he would hire a consulting company to revamp the main applications look that was finished but now the developer from india and another employee are being pulled to implement this into the main application and i m pretty much left alone let us not forget that the developer from india likes punting other problems my way as well it is all very overwhelming amp x b it is seriously putting my career growth to a screeching halt since i m not doing anything to improve or learn new skills i ve burned out hard and as i mentioned previously will be quitting this job has really stripped away any drive i have towards software development and i feel like for someone who has been in the field for years i should know a lot more than i do currently not only has it stripped away my drive it has stripped away my confidence as i look at job postings on the internet i feel as if the skills i learned so far are far behind what some of these postings are asking for for a reference i said we used python but we don t really do anything outside of web development data applications no machine learning no deep data analysis data visualization no devops ect i ve tried to work on personal projects outside of work and have completed a few but nothing in the previously mentioned realms in my down time i also learned javascript es and react through a udemy course amp x b even if i do apply to some jobs i feel like i will easily be overlooked as my coding skills aren t up to par with the amount of time i ve put in at the job i think i m a decent programmer love learning new technologies and am very good at learning on the fly honestly that s my best aspect in life when i m shown something about times i will generally have a pretty good grasp on it i do have other marketable skills such as team work worked in a restaurant for years communication and don t mind the occasional presentation from time to time amp x b so i m looking for what the community thinks of my experience so far and if i m actually falling behind from where i need to be any advice as to what i can do to get out of what i believe is a rut would be greatly appreciated i live minutes from nyc so my thought process is trying to land a job out there that has an actual software development team']

Name: 184_amazon_offer_sde_aws
Representative_Docs: ['i gave my final interview for sde intern last week and i m waiting on the results amazon says within days but from what i ve seen on this sub it can be pretty inconsistent also last week i got an offer for gen ai software engineering intern at a mortgage insurance company that pays hr they said they want to hear back from me asap should i tell them that i m waiting on amazon and to push back their offer deadline as long as possible i also am thinking of using my possible amazon offer as leverage to get a higher salary because amazon pays hr additionally should i email amazon recruiting and tell them that i now have a pending offer i didn t have an offer prior to taking the final interview at amazon so maybe if they take that into account they will have a faster response', 'not really a casual question but since my thread got deleted by the automod i accepted an offer at a smallish local aerospace and defense company working on a great project i d be doing machine learning graph theory work basically as an applied mathematician data scientist today i received a call from amazon telling me i passed all their interviews and that they have a position open in seattle for me this summer after they had rejected me approximately one month ago due to their capacity being full i asked them to send me a formal email since i had to go to class and couldn t really process all this information in the minute and a half before i had to return to my class i ll get the full details of their offer tomorrow but basically i would get the same deal as any of their other interns i m not too worried about the money in the short term yes the pay at amazon is better hr free housing signing bonus vs amazon s standard rate for seattle however i have no idea what kind of career opportunities this could create for me i come from a relatively poor family and i almost feel like giving this up would be throwing away something i ve been working for my entire life it would be easy if i didn t just spend the last week in my family s poorly furnished one bedroom apartment sleeping on an air mattress but we don t get to decide the circumstances of our life so i guess i ll just have to accept that it s a part of my life one of the pros of working at the aerospace and defense company is that i would be on a small highly educated team where i would work closely with a mentor on a really interesting project i also am personally more interested in math than coding but i don t know a ton about graph theory machine learning outside of some of my coursework and research at my university i think i would also probably enjoy this project more than my work at amazon but i won t know what team at amazon i ll be a part of until i accept the offer it also seems likely that since this company is in the boston area i would be able to potentially pursue graduate school while working there however i am unsure if this is guaranteed and it s not something that i ve discussed given that most of my team would be people with graduate degrees mostly phds it seems likely but i don t know for sure i think the pros of amazon pretty much speak for themselves i don t know if i m buying into the hype too much or if i m just letting my personal situation get the best of me but all i ve wanted is to ensure that i could have a stable life and enjoy my life as much as possible i ve heard the horror stories of amazon and i ve reached out to faculty members i respect at my school but even with their advice it s hard to be strong enough to turn them down even if it s the right thing to do as a last ditch effort is there any hope of me delaying my internship at amazon until the fall i m kind of desperate here and i have no idea what my best course of action is i ve never been in this position and i wish that i had the moral convictions to say no to amazon outright', 'this is a huge first world problem and i hope no one gets too upset at me for this just got an offer at both amazon and salesforce for a summer internship and though i m beyond excited that i was fortunate enough to have this opportunity i m torn trying to choose one over the other and wanted to hear what others had to say from their own past experiences or what they ve heard of from companies from colleagues the pros of amazon from what i can tell is that i find amazon s work really interesting i have a huge interest in cybersecurity and somewhat of an interest in data science which draws me to amazon with their work in aws and alexa i d love to be in seattle and find the thought of living in a new city for the summer to be really interesting and think that amazon s name would be impressive and would help me in the future the issues i have with amazon is the apocalyptic stories of their company culture work life balance and expectations from their employees though i m sure these stories are somewhat exaggerated for media attention i have lingering fears that amazon could be working their employees to the bone with little concern for their work life balance especially when i contrasted it with salesforce s ohana culture i have heard that any of these issues are few and far between when i asked my friends who worked at amazon last summer and are largely team dependent but i heard many complaints about the aws team and that was something that i was interested in working on i don t have an immediate interest in salesforce s work but was really impressed with how well it seems like they treat their employees they offered me a very large housing stipends which i could just keep since i live in the area and tons of cool perks that go beyond amazon s free bananas and dog parks my concern with salesforce is that the location i was extended an offer from is not their headquarters in the bay area and am somewhat concerned that the office might not be what i expect salesforce pays less hourly but with the stipends would give me a few thousand more at the end of the summer especially since i live in near the area so i could eat at commute from home if it means anything i thought the salesforce interview was really easy the technical question i was given would have taken anyone less than minutes to solve amazon had a few rounds of many design oriented questions where my interviewer would have cool conversations with me and created hypothetical situations to make me rethink what i was doing even when it was right i m not sure if that would reflect in the interns if one company was more selective than the other but it s just something to note tldr salesforce seems nicer and overall would pay more amazon has some scary stories but i m more interested in their work any personal stories opinions about these two companies internship programs any thoughts would be greatly appreciated']

Name: 185_prompts_gmail com_gmail_tools
Representative_Docs: ['most professional development tools are boring so i tried to flip it what if learning felt like playing a board game now let me show you how it works i built thinkfast arena a monopoly style property building game where you take on a professional role software developer marketer etc and face real world career challenges the game generates personalized scenarios that test your actual workplace skills you can create custom learning boards on any topic leadership technical skills interview prep compete against ai opponents acquire career properties and level up as challenges get more complex runs as a progressive web app so it works on any device whether you re a student exploring career paths or a professional sharpening interview skills and industry knowledge thinkfast arena makes career growth as engaging as your favorite game would this be fun or useful for you what do you think would make it more engaging or actually something people would want to use demo', 'hello everyone i m looking to collaborate with committed individuals who are interested in setting up managing and optimizing accounts for ai model training workflows rather than traditional gig style work this is about forming a partnership where we share tasks tools best practices and split value what i bring willingness to commit to weekly collaboration calls and tracking what i m looking for from you trustworthy professional attitude remote collaboration works best when we treat it like a real team how we ll work together we ll define a workflow account creation data ingestion labeling qa iteration we ll choose tools and platforms together voice channel shared document spreadsheet task tracking we ll split responsibilities and agree on transparent tracking profit sharing revenue split or hourly rate bonus depending on what we land weekly check in to review progress tasks completed blockers and next steps', 'hey everyone i m completely new to reddit and honestly new to the business world so i know i m bound to make lots of mistakes thanks for reading this i created a curated list of different ai personas basically prompts that can modify an ai chatbot into behaving like a tutor rather than just giving you an answer i ve spent years using ai tools even before they became mainstream over the years i created specific prompts so the ai could actually teach me how to learn new things today i m here because to be frank i m desperate for feedback from people feel free to comment anything under this post i really mean anything or email me at vertechacademy gmail com mailto vertechacademy gmail com and i ll send you my catalog of ai persona prompts for free i m giving it away to the first people that email me if you want let me know what parts of the product are good what you like or what you don t like you can even tell me it sucks and i won t take it personally i m just a regular guy trying to make a living while also trying to build something that people can use and enjoy i want to help others stay away from all the brainrot that comes with doomscrolling for hours on social media i also want to give students a leg up because when i was in school none of the new tech we have today was available and i had a really hard time thanks for your time and for any feedback you re willing to give']

Name: 186_csc_development_software_web

online it may also make the process of building a startup easier with the skills gained the hex course would look good on resume and the skills can transfer into my future job i may have been wrong programming for awhile it s just now feeling intensive and practical i am hoping to be able to apply for a jr dev role or freelance small stuff by then or at least an internship i know i need to have some form of portfolio but i m struggling with focusing on what to actually create work on for it my program is mostly java focused with some mysql python and c sprinkled in very brief stuff with webgl and js i ve played with mobile dev platforms my degree will use android for one unit though i m more interested in ios i ve been doing some web design stuff but it feels like a waste of time i Name: 189_professor_use ai_assignment_ask
Representative_Docs: ['talk to your advisor gpd chair look at school s policy my school embraces ai generally and each professor has the liberty of setting up their own ai expectation in their syllabus you also have to respect plagiarism policy as per your school my pi has no problem with me using ai to offload certain aspects of my research like writing python scripts or finding papers provided i am transparent about my use of ai but vehemently does not want me to use it to write entirely understandable down the line all that you ll get from this post here is the general feeling of how people believe whether ai is acceptable or not and it s a very divisive and emotionally loaded topic your best bet is to refer to your school departmental and advisor s policy on the matter', 'here s the most practical advice go to talk to the professor ask him what is the best evidence to show that ai wasn t used explain how you have a firm grasp on the class material and if you used ai you likely wouldn t have that anyways don t be mean don t try to pull a gotcha that will likely cause him to just hunker down in his preconceived notions if that fails bring this up with your chair and discuss how you re disappointed in the lack of policies disputing ai use work towards a solution that benefits everyone in grad school you have the privilege of not immediately seeming like you re trying to skirt around requirements use that to your advantage it s a weird time for us all and the decisions we make now will have everlasting impacts on academia', 'i will try it is a broad question the chair outside of research group or profs don t really know much about your area will ask high level questions since they didn t even read the thesis that thoroughly those questions are very difficult to answer they will not take your methodology for granted even though it would seem natural to your advisor or other experts in the area because they don t know the methods any way so you need to tell a cogent story for them since you have been preparing for this i will just leave it there i think you should be ok even if they asked you something too difficult to answer i ve seen some pretty philosophical ones in our engineering department don t panic they probably don t expect you to give a perfect answer once some prof asked the student if ai is strong enough why do we still need your method of classification i was like ouch if you don t understand the question what they want to hear ask them again to make sure while you answer the experts are a different story they will go for everything they found questionable from typos to week conclusions you need to reread your thesis well keep one handy with you during the defense and bring a pen to write down their advices if there are a few weak spots known in the thesis that s what you need to prepare for you probably would have to defend them your advisor would jump in if things go too far and it no longer looks good on them advisor will ask you a question that s their way of helping so think fast and extend their answers at the end of day if your advisor agreed you are up for defence you will get it don t worry tell a convincing story show your work you will pass all the best']

Name: 190_model_models_results_machine
Representative_Docs: ['the machine learning thing isn t as hard as it may appear from the outside for many algorithms it is just standard regression techniques in a trench coat pretending to be something it s not it s one of the reasons i am so disenfranchised by academia s new obsession with it i made sure to write my first paper of my phd using double machine learning with lasso and random forest just to get it out of the way and so i could slap machine learning experience on my cv if you have decent data you re using for computation now and as you say you can easily get into lt journals i suggest a post lasso or a lasso you could even do it as an extension to a current paper from the computer science perspective the poor saps designing the algorithms it s hard from a computational perspective the empirical numpties using the algorithms it s just a case of running a different estimation technique and reporting the results no biggie', 'obtaining a more rigorous derivation with clear assumptions and limitations of a heuristically derived model is still an important advancement and there s bound to be someone else in the field that can appreciate that even with machine learning the physical interpretation of the results can be limited unless the training sets and model are informed by some knowledge of the system being investigated maybe it s not immediately apparent but your derivation could provide some useful context for the implementation and interpretation of these ml models that said it s hard not to be critical of an incremental improvement on prior work as you ve described i struggled with that a lot during my own thesis and as another commenter mentioned it s important to acknowledge that you ve picked up useful skills along the way while accepting disappointment in this stage of your work it s been way easier to deal with the disappointment since i ve graduated as i m not constantly critiquing my research anymore', 'one ml application that i think is mostly valid is when they fit an ml regression to a series of numerical model input output combinations e g input rock strength parameters and output displacement strain that way you can estimate parameter combinations and outputs in the parameter space in between actual model results it s not claiming to replace or somehow circumvent the actual physical modeling it s just a convenient way to interpret results in other words they re not claiming that you no longer have to run the initial models the problems with this are it isn t really physically justified and can t be used for any sort of extrapolation plus in reality it s not something you couldn t do with classical regression techniques with similar results it just has the ai brand recognition amp x b edit found the paper surrogate models']

Name: 197_internship_ml_abroad_company
Representative_Docs: ['hello i m wondering what you all think about doing an internship abroad for a us student in terms of help for future prospects career development etc i got offered a month internship at a large international bank s headquarters in europe paris london zurich specifically their ai ml research lab and i m wondering if i should take it for more information i m currently an undergrad at a recognized us engineering school mit stanford except my degree is in the humanities but i ve taken a few cs classes and one intro ml class out of interest i m definitely not as experienced as my eecs engineering friends and have never even been abroad which is why the offer blindsided me it would be cool to go abroad but i ve got a few concerns as doing a longer internship abroad isn t typical so it s been hard to find proper advice mainly i am worried about the opportunity cost tradeoff of this internship months vs staying in school and finding a us summer internship the pay for this internship is low compared to the sv intern salaries but col is also much lower i would also probably delay graduation by a year or so i m not sure how beneficial having a european network is especially because i currently don t intend to stay there i m not sure about the job content especially what an undergrad can do at an ai research lab i raised this concern with them in the interviews and they gave vague assurances about the quality of work that interns get and brief details about what the lab does red flag or just privacy concerns the lab doesn t publish in any top tier conferences and only a few people on the team hold a phd come from academia so it seems they are more focused on implementing ai for internal use unlike the more research y places like gb msr fair wondering what a value of this internship would be compared to being a research assistant in an ai lab on my university campus any advice would be greatly appreciated probably there are considerations i haven t even thought of', 'im a student at a well reputed univeristy in the us and im doing my ms in data science i am in my first year of the program and have started hunting for a summer internship for the market is hard also i come from a non tech background my hope is to start working in the machine learning space after i graduate i am an international student so the context is complex in regards to visa stuff i know that everyone wants ml right now and with my lack of background this can get harder my hope was to get some experience in it thru a summer internship so itll improve my prospects when i graduate to get a full time job in ml somewhere here s the deal i interned in a non tech role at a place where there was a lot of ml going on they are looking for ml research interns at the moment in the space that i am interested in however its a very very small company and wont pay much for sure thankfully not a problem for me the company is hqed in the silicon valley but the ceo doesnt even live in the us my question is given the market and given my background would it make more sense to apply for this internship and do you think it will increase my job prospects after i graduate i worked there as an intern in my undergrad for over a year and have a nice relationship with them i surely have an increased chance of being accepted the position is remote and im torn about how itll look on my resume if i intern at the same place twice once in undergrad and then in postgrad i do think i have a sweet deal here where i stand a good chance of interning in ml in this market even without a background the catch is if i apply now and get another offer elsewhere im likely to take that offer and risk souring my relationship at this company justw anted youre Name: 198_turnitin_plagiarism_flagged_detection
Representative_Docs: ['i m a teacher too and complete scam is hyperbolic turnitin catches unmitigated ai generated content even a reasonably read person can often detect ai content by sight alone turnitin has a disclaimer on every paper it analyzes that its ai detection alone is not enough for disciplinary action on that i agree teachers have to have more than one measure for me i require body paragraphs to be drafted in class only i teach high school however turnitin catches any foul play when my students try to use grammarly to revise or chatgpt to write their intro conclusions in some ways the age of ai writing will improve academic integrity it forces us teachers to pay more attention to our student writing at each stage of the writing process', 'honestly it s weird how turnitin flags things you ve written yourself i ve had papers where even citations and common phrases were marked i started using winston ai to avoid that it gives more reliable results and handles both plagiarism and ai detection way better than turnitin in my experience', 'you re welcome is turnitin flagging the citations for originality issues not ai there are two separate report tabs if it is flagging the citations unless they are fictional it probably just means that someone else has cited the same source as you and turnitin has found that same cite online and thinks it is a sentence i explain this to faculty at the beginning of semesters false hits in originality reports and no one listens haha you may be able to see the turnitin report as a student as we integrate it on canvas']

professors research assistants undergrad students institutions universities corporate research centers paperguide helps discover relevant papers read research papers faster using chat and summaries conduct comprehensive literature reviews manage and organize references annotate and take research notes enhance academic writing at paperguide our mission is to accelerate research so that solutions to the world s intricate problems including climate change and diseases can be found faster paperguide originally known as chatwithpdf was launched as a simple tool to comprehend large pdfs since its launch last year more than users have used our tool through user feedback we discovered that students and researchers form the majority of our user base rather than remaining a generic tool we recognized an opportunity to make a more significant impact by tailoring our focus to the needs of researchers and students if you are a researcher or involved in research related work please give it a try how can paperguide assist with your research process we d love to hear your thoughts and feedback thank you in advance', 'hey everyone i ve been testing a few tools for serious pdf work apps that actually help you read explore and understand your documents instead of just summarizing them these are the four that stood out most to me notebooklm askyourpdf pdf ai and pdfury i tried all of them personally and want to share just my honest impressions so you can pick what fits your workflow best notebooklm pricing free notebooklm by google focuses on summarization and concept generation rather than deep document reading pros completely free automatically creates summaries flashcards and concept maps can generate video or audio summaries good for idea visualization and overview cons doesn t display the actual pdf only extracted text difficult to verify which source each answer uses not suitable for research or detailed text analysis best for creating summaries quizzes and quick conceptual overviews askyourpdf pricing limited free tier paid plans month askyourpdf is one of the more established and feature rich options pros side by side layout pdf on the right chat on the left works with scanned pdfs ocr easily chat with some or all of your documents at a time screenshot q a ask about images or figures directly share documents via link clean functional interface chat export for saving conversations offline cons occasional lag or layout shifting shared chats aren t real time each link creates a copy to another account source highlights are sometimes inaccurate best for users who want a stable rich featured document platform pdf ai pricing limited free tier paid plans month pdf ai is simple and well designed though somewhat heavier in performance pros side by side layout pdf on the left chat on the right supports scanned pdfs ocr includes screenshot capture and a chrome extension exports chats as pdf files cons slower interface upload limit mb on paid plans source references appear only in the end and can be off topic best for small scale academic or business document work pdfury pricing limited free tier paid from week or month pdfury combines a simple intuitive design with great speed and collaboration tools pros side by side layout pdf on the left chat on the right inline source highlighting shows exactly which text was used screenshot q a ask about images or figures directly collaboration mode real time shared chat anonymous users can view in read only mode chat export save the entire conversation as a pdf for offline use works with scanned pdfs ocr fast and minimal interface cons doesn t support chatting across multiple pdfs smaller ecosystem than the older platforms best for deep reading research and collaborative studying summary feature notebooklm askyourpdf pdf ai visual pdf view scanned pdfs ocr collaboration partial real time source highlights text only end of message sometimes off end of message sometimes off inline precise chat with multiple pdfs limited chat export video audio summary flashcards quiz generation pricing free mo mo wk or mo if you just need summaries or flashcards notebooklm is excellent but if you re working with complex or academic pdfs and need to verify sources and actually see your documents then askyourpdf pdf ai or pdfury are far better options personally i found pdfury the most efficient for research and collaboration it has some key strengths like pdf viewer and accurate source highlight that powerful notebooklm doesn t have yet remain the cheapest among the paid three i still use notebooklm for video summaries though since it s free but all four tools have their strengths depending on how you study or work hope this helps someone who s been looking for a solid pdf workflow tool happy to answer questions if anyone s curious about details also would like to know about new tools that you ve found']
